a+y=0

y = -a

hence it is prove that (a,-a) is a solution of  x+y =0

questions.question: The linear equation 2x + 3y = 6 has
questions.explanation:
2x + 3y = 6
2x = 6 - 3y
x = {tex}\frac{6-3 y}{2}{/tex} 

x 0 {tex}3\over 2{/tex} 3
y 2 1 0

This table continues for infinte terms for different values of x &  y. So for infinite value of y we have infinite value of x. 
Therefore, this equation has Infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: The graph of the linear equation 2x - 3y = 6, cuts the y-axis at the point
questions.explanation:
The linear equation 2x - 3y = 6, cuts the y-axis when x co-ordinate is 0.
So we put x = 0 in given equation 2x - 3y = 6
2 {tex}\times{/tex}0 - 3y = 6
0 - 3y = 6
-y = {tex}\frac{6}{3}{/tex} 
-y = 2
y = -2
So the co-ordinates are (0, -2).

questions.question: The graph of the linear equation 3x – 2y = 6, cuts the x-axis at the point

the linear equation 3x – 2y = 6,   cuts the x-axis

when y co-ordinate is 0

so we put y = 0 in given equation 3x – 2y = 6

3x - 2.0 = 6

3x = 6
{tex}x=\frac{6}{3}{/tex}
x=2

so the co-ordinates are (2,0)

questions.explanation:

the given co-ordinate is solution of a eqution if on puting the co-ordiates L.H.S = R.H.S

3.2 + 4.3

questions.question: How many linear equations can be satisfied by x = 2 and y = 3?
questions.explanation:
There are infinite many eqution which satisfy the given value x = 2, y = 3
for example
x + y = 5
x - y = -1
3x - 2y = 0
etc.......

questions.question: The graph of the linear equation x – y = 0 passes through the point
questions.explanation:
The graph of the linear equation x – y = 0 passes through the point {tex}\left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex}

because the co-ordinate of x and y axis satisfy the  given equation

x - y =0

{tex}\eqalign{ & {1 \over 2} - {1 \over 2} = 0 \cr & 0 = 0 \cr}{/tex}

so we can say  {tex}\left( {\frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{2}} \right){/tex} is a solution of above equation

so we can say the value of x co-ordinate must be equal to y co -ordinate

questions.question: The graph of the linear equation x + y = 0 passes through the point
questions.explanation:
The graph of the linear equation x + y = 0 passes through the point (1, -1) because the co-ordinate of x and y axis satisfy the given equation.
x + y = 0
1 - 1 = 0
So we can say  (1, -1) is a solution of above equation.

questions.question: If (k, -3) lies on the line 3x – y = 6, then the value of ‘k’ is
questions.explanation:
(k, -3) lies on the line 3x – y = 6,

it means that (k, -3) is a solutio of a line 3x – y = 6,

so

{tex}\eqalign{ & 3k - \left( { - 3} \right) = 6 \cr & 3k + 3 = 6 \cr & 3k = 6 - 3 \cr & 3k = 3 \cr & k = {3 \over 3} = 1 \cr & k = 1 \cr} {/tex}

questions.question: If x = 3 and y = -2 satisfies 2x - 3y = k, then the value of k is
questions.explanation:
If x = 3 and y = -2 satisfies 2x - 3y = k
It means x = 3 and y = -2 is a solution of equation 2x - 3y = k
2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 3(-2) = k
6 + 6 = k
k = 12

questions.question: For the equation 5x + 8y = 50, if y = 10, then the value of x is
questions.explanation:
For the equation 5x + 8y = 50, if y = 10
put y = 10 in given equation
5x + 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 50
5x + 80 = 50 
5x = 50 - 80
5x = -30
x = -{tex}\frac{30}{5}{/tex} 
x = -6

questions.question: The area of the triangle formed by the line 3x + 4y = 12 and the co-ordinate axis is
questions.explanation:
To find the area of the triangle AOB  formed by the line 3x + 4y = 12 and co-ordinate axis
we put x = 0 in given equation to find the point on  y axies
 so at x = 0
 3(0) + 4y = 12
 4y = 12
we get y = 3
at y = 0
3x + 4(0) = 12
3x  = 12
we get x =4
so the line cut y axis at 3 and x axis at 4
so the hight of triangle AOB is OB = 3 unit and base OA= 4 unit
area ot triangle AOB = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(base {tex}\times{/tex} height)
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
= 6 unit square

questions.question: The graph of every linear equation in two variables is a
questions.explanation: Because for one value of one variable their is only one and unique value of other variables.

questions.question: The equation 3x + 4y = 7 has a unique solution, if x and y are
questions.explanation:
3x + 4y = 7
3x = 7 - 4y
x = {tex}\frac{7-4 y}{3}{/tex} 
The equation will have a unique solution only if x and y are natural numbers with only one value which is for y = 1.
x = {tex}\frac{7-4.1}{3}{/tex} 
x = {tex}\frac{7-4}{3}{/tex} 
x = {tex}\frac{3}{3}{/tex} = 1
x = 1
i. e x = 1, y = 1 will be unique value for this equation.

questions.explanation:

questions.question: If we multiply both sides of a linear equation with a non-zero number, then the solution of the linear equation:

options.html: Changes in case of multiplication only
Changes in case of division only

questions.explanation:
If for any c. where c is any natural number
Like addition and subtraction we can multiply and divide both sides of an equation by a number, c, without changing the equation, where c is any natural number.

questions.question: If we divide both sides of a linear equation with a non-zero number, then the solution of the linear equation
questions.explanation: If then for any non-zero c.  We can divide both sides of an equation by a non-zero number c, without changing the equation.

questions.question: The point on the graph of the linear equation 2x + 5y = 19, whose ordinate is 1½ times its abscissa is
questions.explanation:
Ordinate means y-coordinate. It means we need to find a point on the given line where y-coordinte = 3/2 (x-coordinate) . Just put y = [ (3/2) .x ] in the given eqn.

{tex}2 x+5 \cdot \frac{3}{2} x=19{/tex}
{tex}2 x+\frac{15}{2} x=19{/tex}
{tex}\frac{4 x+15 x}{2}=19{/tex}
{tex}\frac{19 x}{2}=19{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{19 \times 2}{19}{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{3}{2} x{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{3}{2} \times 2{/tex}
y=3

so the co-ordinate are (2,3)

questions.question: If the point (3, 4) lies on the graph of 3y = ax + 6, then the value of ‘a’ is
questions.explanation:
the point (3, 4) lies on the graph of 3y = ax + 6

so it will satisfy the equation
3 y=a x+6
3 (y)=a x+6
12=3 a+6
12-6=3 a
3 a=6
{tex}a=\frac{6}{3}{/tex}
a=2

questions.question: Express y in terms of x in the equation 5y - 3x - 10 = 0.
questions.explanation:
5y - 3x - 10 = 0
5y - 3x = 10
5y = 10 + 3x 
y = {tex}\frac{10+3 x}{5}{/tex}

questions.explanation:
A linear equation in two variables is of the form ax + by + c = 0 as a and b are cofficient of x and y

therefore when a {tex}\ne {/tex} 0 and b {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 then only the equation will be in two variable

y = 3

because when a line parallel to x axis in that case equation of line is y = a

where a is the co-ordinate of y-axis and 3 units above the origin value x -cordinate is 3

x = 4

because when a line parallel to y axis in that case equation of line is x = a

where a is the co-ordinate of x-axis and 7 units to the left of the origin value x -co-ordinate is -7

so required equation is x = -7

questions.question: If the line represented by the equation 3x + ky = 9 passes through the points (2, 3), then the value of k is
questions.explanation:
If the line represented by the equation 3x + ky = 9 passes through the points (2, 3) then (2, 3) will satisy the equation 3x + ky = 9
3 (2) + 3k = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 6 + 3k = 9
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3k = 9 - 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3k = 3
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k = 1

questions.question: The equation y = 2x - 7 has
questions.explanation:
y = 2x - 7
Has many solution because for different value of x we have different value of y for example.
At x = 1
y = 2 (1) - 7
y = 2 - 7
y = -5
at x = 2
y = 2(2) - 7
y = 4 - 7
y = -3
So we can say for many value of x there is many value of y.

questions.question: If a linear equation has solutions (-2, 2), (0, 0) and (2, -2), then it is of the form:

Linear equation has solutions (-2, 2), (0, 0) and (2, -2), then the equation will be 
x + y = 0
As all the given three points satisfy the given equation

questions.question: The positive solutions of the equation ax + by + c = 0 always lie in the

questions.question: The graph of the linear equation 3x – 5y = 15, cuts the y-axis at the point
questions.explanation:
The graph of the linear equation 3x - 5y = 15, cuts the y-axis at the point when line cut y-axis the co-ordinate of x becomes zero.
So we put x = 0 in given equation to find the co-ordinate.
3x - 5y = 15
3(0) - 5y = 15
-5y = 15
y = {tex}\frac{-15}{5}{/tex} 
y = -3
So the required cordinate is (0, -3).

questions.question: The graph of the linear equation 4x + 2y = 12, cuts the x-axis at the point
questions.explanation:
The graph of the linear equation 4x + 2y = 12, cuts the x-axis at the point

when line cut x axis the co-ordinate of y becomes zero.

so we put y = 0 in given equation to find the co-ordinate

{tex}\eqalign{ & 4x + 2y = 12 \cr & 4x + 2(0) = 12 \cr & 4x = 12 \cr & x = {{12} \over 4} \cr & x = 3 \cr} {/tex}

so the required cordinate is (3,0)

questions.question: How many linear equations in ‘x’ and ‘y’ can be satisfied by x = 1, y = 2?
questions.explanation:
There are many linear equations in ‘x’ and ‘y’ can be satisfied by x = 1, y = 2
for example

2x + y =4
and so on there are infinte number of examples

questions.question: The value of k if x = 3 and y = -2 is a solution of the equation 2x - 13y = k is
questions.explanation:
We have to find the value of ‘k’ if x = 3 and y = -2 is a solution of the equation 2x – 13y = k
2x - 13y = k
2(3) - 13(-2) = k
6 + 26 = k
k = 32

questions.question: Which of the following pair is a solution of the equation 3x – 2y = 7?

3x – 2y = 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3(1) - 2(-2) = 7
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 + 4 = 7
LHS = RHS

questions.question: Which of the following is a linear equation in two variables?
questions.explanation: In linear equation power of variable x and y should be 1 and here,  the given linear equation has two variable x and y.

questions.question: x = 5 and y = -2 is the solution of the linear equation.

2.5 - (-2)
10 + 2
12

let force applied  be y and accleration produced be x

{tex}\eqalign{ & y\alpha x \cr & y = kx \cr} {/tex}

where k is proportionality constant

questions.question: The point which lies on y-axis at a distance of 3 units in the negative direction of y-axis is
questions.explanation:
At the y-axis, the value of x co-ordinate is 0, y-axis at a distance of 3 units in the negative direction, so the co-ordinate of the y-axis is -3.

questions.question: The point which lies on y-axis at a distance of 6 units in the positive direction of y-axis is

At y-axis the value of x co-ordinate is 0 and y-axis at a distance of 6 units in the positive direction so the co-ordinate of the y-axis is 6.

questions.question: The point which lies on x-axis at a distance of 4 units in the negative direction of x-axis is

questions.question: For the equation 5x – 7y = 35, if y = 5, then the value of ‘x’ is
questions.explanation:
For the equation 5x – 7y = 35, if y = 5,

{tex}\eqalign{ & 5x - 7y = 35 \cr & y = 5 \cr & 5x - 7.5 = 35 \cr & 5x - 35 = 35 \cr & 5x = 35 + 35 \cr & 5x = 70 \cr & x = {{70} \over 5} = 14 \cr & x = 14 \cr} {/tex}

questions.question: The area of the triangle formed by the line 2x + 5y = 10 and the co-ordinate axis is
questions.explanation:
To find the area of the triangle formed by the line 2x + 5y = 10 and co-ordinate axis
We put x = 0 in given equation at x = 0, we get y = 2
at y = 0 we get x = 5
So the line cut y-axis at 2 and x-axis at 5
So the height of the triangle is 2 unit and the base is 5 unit
Area of triangle = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} base {tex}\times{/tex} height
= {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
= 5 sq. units

questions.question: Express y in terms of x in the equation 5x - 2y = 7.
questions.explanation:
5x - 2y = 7
- 2y = 7 - 5x
2y = 5x - 7
y = {tex}\frac{5 x-7}{2}{/tex}

questions.question: Express ‘x’ in terms of ‘y’ in the equation 2x – 3y – 5 = 0.
questions.explanation: {tex}\eqalign{ & 2x - 3y - 5 = 0 \cr & 2x = 3y + 5 \cr & x = {{3y + 5} \over 2} \cr}{/tex}

questions.question: The cost of 2 kg of apples and 1 kg of grapes on a day was found to be ₹160. A linear equation in two variables to represent the above data is
questions.explanation:
Let the cost of apples be ₹x per Kg and cost of grapes be ₹y per Kg. The cost of 2 kg of apples and 1 kg of grapes on a day was found to be ₹160.
So the equation will be
2x + y = 160

questions.question: If (-2, 5) is a solution of 2x + my = 11, then the value of ‘m’ is
questions.explanation:
If (-2, 5) is a solution of 2x + my = 11

then it will satisfy the given equation
2 .(-2)+5 m=11
-4+5 m=11
5 m=11+4
5 m=15
{tex}m=\frac{15}{5}=3{/tex}
m=3

questions.question: The graph of the equation x + y = 4
questions.explanation:
The graph of the equation x + y = 4

Put x=0 cut y axis at y = 4

Put y=0 cut x axis at x = 4

questions.question: The line represented by the equation x + y = 16 passes through (2, 14). How many more lines pass through the point (2, 14)
questions.explanation:
There are many lines pass through the point (2, 14).
For example
x - y = -12
2x + y = 18
and many more.

questions.question: The taxi fare in a city is as follows: For the first kilometer, the fare is ₹8 and for the subsequent distance it is ₹5 per kilometer. Taking the distance covered as x km and total fare as ₹y, write a linear equation for this information.
questions.explanation:
Taxi fare for first kilometer = ₹8
Taxi fare for subsequent distance = ₹5
Total distance covered = x
Total fare = y
Since the fare for first kilometer = ₹8
According to problem, Fare for (x - 1) kilometer = 5(x - 1)
So, the total fare y = 5(x - 1) + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 5(x - 1) + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 5x – 5 + 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 5x + 3 
Hence, y = 5x + 3 is the required linear equation.

questions.question: The graph of a linear equation {tex}y = \frac{9}{5}x + 32{/tex} cuts the y-axis at the point
questions.explanation:
when the graph cut at y axis in that case the value of x- coordinate is 0
{tex}y=\frac{9}{5} x+32{/tex}
{tex}y=\frac{9}{5} \cdot 0+32{/tex}
y=32

so the co-ordinates are (32,0)

questions.question: The graph of a linear equation x - 5y + 3 = 0 cuts the x-axis at the point
questions.explanation:
When a line cuts x -axis in that case y co-ordinate is 0, so to find the co-ordinate of x we put y = 0 in given equation.
x – 5y + 3 = 0
At y = 0
x - 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 0 + 3 = 0
x + 3 = 0
x = -3
So the co-ordinate are (-3, 0)

questions.question: The graph of the linear equation y = 3x passes through the point
questions.explanation:
y = 3x
{tex}\frac {y}{3}{/tex} = x
For x = {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}, the value of y = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex} = 2
So ({tex}\frac{2}{3}{/tex}, 2)

questions.question: Write the linear equation such that each point on its graph has an ordinate 5 times its abscissa.
If a linear equation has solutions (1, 2), (-1, -16) and (0, -7), then it is of the form
questions.explanation:
Since all the given co- ordinate (1, 2), (-1, -16) and (0, -7) satisfy the given line y = 9x - 7
For point (1, 2)
y = 9x - 7
2 = 9(1) - 7
2 = 9 - 7
2 = 2
Hence (2, 1) is a solution.
For point (-1, -16)
y = 9x - 7
-16 = 9(-1) - 7
-16 = -9 - 7
-16 = -16
Hence (-1, -16) is a solution.
For point (0,-7)
y = 9x - 7
-7 = 9(0) -7
-7 = -7
Hence (0, -7) is a solution.

questions.question: The graph of x = -4 is a straight line
questions.explanation:
We know that the general equation of a line parallel to y-axis is
x = a
So x = -4 is a line parallel to y-axis.

questions.question: The three steps from solids to points are
questions.explanation: In each step we lose one dimension. solid- 3 dimension. surface- 2 dimension. lines- 1 dimension. point- has no dimension

questions.question: The number of dimension, a point has
questions.explanation: because point has no part.

questions.question: The number of dimensions, a line has
questions.explanation: Because a line is a breathless length.

questions.question: The number of dimensions, a surface has
questions.explanation: the surface is that which has length and breadth.  (1 dimension + 1 dimension = 2 dimension)

questions.question: The number of dimensions, a solid has
questions.explanation: a solid shape also has a depth.solids by their nature have an inside and an outside separated by a surface. that's why solid is a three dimensional struture. it has length, breadth and depth.

questions.question: Euclid divided his famous treatise “The Elements” into
questions.explanation:
Book 1 contains Euclid's 10 axioms
Book 2 is commonly called the "book of geometric algebra" 
Book 3 deals with circles and their properties
Book 4 constructs the incircle and circumcircle of a triangle
Book 5 is a treatise on proportions of magnitudes.
Book 6 applies proportions to geometry: similar figures.
Book 7 deals with elementary number theory.
Book 8 deals with proportions in number theory and geometric sequences.
Book 9 applies the results of the preceding two books and gives the infinitude of prime numbers.
Book 10 attempts to classify incommensurable (in modern language, irrational)
Book 11 generalizes the results of books 1–6
Book 12 studies volumes of cones, pyramids, and cylinders 
Book 13 constructs the five regular Platonic solids.

questions.question: The total number of propositions in the Euclid’s Elements is
questions.explanation: Euclid's Elements is a mathematical and geometric treatise consisting of 13 books attributed to the ancient Greek mathematician Euclid in Alexandria, Ptolemaic Egypt circa 300 BC. It is a collection of definitions, postulates (axioms), propositions (theorems and constructions), and mathematical proofs of the propositions. There are 131 definitions, 465 propositions ,5 Postulates and 5 common notions in Euclid's Elements.

questions.question: The boundaries of surfaces are
questions.explanation: lines and curves.

questions.question: The boundaries of solids are
questions.explanation: Because solid has both inner and outer part separated by surface.

questions.question: A pyramid is a solid figure, the base of which is
questions.explanation: A pyramid is a polyhedron formed by connecting of polygonal base and an apex. Each base edge and apex forms a triangle, called the lateral face.It is a conic solid with polygonal base.

questions.question: The side faces of a pyramid are
questions.explanation: because when we connect base edge and apex it forms a triangle.

questions.question: The side faces of a prism are
questions.explanation: When the side faces of a polyhedron are rectangle then it is known as prism.

questions.question: It is known that if a + b = 4 then a + b + c = 4 + c. The Euclid’s axiom that illustrates this statement is
questions.explanation: if equals are added to equals, the whole are equals.

questions.question: It is known that if a + b = 4 then a + b - c = 4 - c. The Euclid’s axiom that illustrates this statement is
questions.explanation: If equals be subtracted from equals, the remainder are equal.

questions.question: It is known that if a + b = 4 then 2(a + b) = 8. The Euclid’s axiom that illustrates this statement is
questions.explanation: Things which are double of the same things are equal to each other.

questions.question: It is known that if a + b = 4 then {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}(a + b) = 2. The Euclid’s axiom that illustrates this statement is
questions.explanation: things which are halves of the same things are equal to each other.

questions.question: A surface is that which has
questions.explanation: because surface is a 2 dimensional structure.

questions.question: The number of lines passing through one point
questions.explanation: Infinite number of lines can pass through a single point.

questions.question: The number of lines passing through two distinct points
questions.explanation: Only one line can pass through two given distinct points.

questions.question: The whole is _________ the part.
questions.explanation: According to Euclid's common notions, the whole is greater than the part.

questions.question: Every line has:
questions.explanation: The midpoint of a line segment divides it into two equal parts, therefore, a line can have only one mid-point.

questions.question: Two lines are said to be _________, if they intersect at a right angle.
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines are two or more lines that intersect at a 90-degree angle or right angle.

questions.question: If point C lies between two points A and B such that AC = BC, then
questions.explanation:
Point C is the mid point of AB. Hence, AB = AC + BC
or, AB = AC + AC = 2AC (since, AC = BC)
Hence, AC = {tex}\frac{1}{2}AB{/tex}

questions.question: Euclid belongs to
questions.explanation: Euclid, sometimes called Euclid of Alexandria to distinguish him from Euclides of Megara, was a Greek mathematician, often referred to as the "father of geometry".

questions.question: Thales belongs to the country:
questions.explanation: Thales, a Greek mathematician cum philosopher lived in Pre-Socratic times around 620-625 BC. He is commonly known as Thales of Miletus as he belonged to Miletus in Asian region.

questions.question: Pythagoras was influenced by
questions.explanation: Pythagoras of Samos is one of the most famous names in the history of mathematics and is recognized as the first true mathematician. When Pythagoras was 18, he visited Miletus- an ancient Greek city on the western coast of Anatolia; where he met Thales– the first known Greek philosopher and scientist. By that time Thales was very old and is not believed to have taught Pythagoras a great deal. However, it was this meeting which triggered his interest in the science of mathematics and astronomy.

questions.question: Which of the following needs a proof?
questions.explanation:
Theorem — a mathematical statement that is proved using rigorous mathematical reasoning.  In a mathematical paper, the term theorem is often reserved for the most important results.

Axiom/Postulate — a statement that is assumed to be true without proof. These are the basic building blocks from which all theorems are proved (Euclid’s five postulates, Zermelo-Fraenkel axioms, Peano axioms).

Definition — a precise and unambiguous description of the meaning of a mathematical term.  It characterizes the meaning of a word by giving all the properties and only those properties that must be true.

questions.question: Euclid stated that all right angles are equal to each other in the form of
questions.explanation: Eucid's fourth postulate states that all right angles are equal to one another.

questions.question: In Indus Valley Civilisation,the bricks used for construction work were having dimensions in the ratio
questions.explanation: In Indus Valley Civilization, the bricks used for construction work were having dimensions in the ratio length : breadth : thickness = 4 : 2 : 1.

questions.question: In ancient India, the shapes of altars used for household rituals were
questions.explanation: In ancient India, squares and circular altars were used for household rituals. The geometry of the Vedic period originated with the construction of altars (or vedis) and fireplaces for performing Vedic rites. Square and circular altars were used for household rituals, while altars, whose shapes were combinations of rectangles, triangles and trapeziums, were required for public worship.

questions.question: “Lines are parallel if they do not intersect” is stated in the form of
questions.explanation: Euclid gave the definition of parallel lines in Book I, Definition 23 just before the five postulates.

questions.question: The edges of a surface are
questions.explanation: Edge is a line or border at which a surface terminates.

questions.question: The boundaries of the solids are
questions.explanation: A solid has shape, size, position and can be moved from one place to another. Its boundaries are called surfaces. They separate one part of the space from the other.

questions.question: If the point P lies in between M and N, C is the mid-point of MP then
questions.explanation:
Since, P lies between M and N, MN = MP + PN .....(i)

Now, C is the mid-point of MP, so, MP = MC + CP

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} MN = MC+CP+PN

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} MN = MC +CN     (CP+PN =CN)

questions.question: Axioms are assumed

options.html: universal truths in all branches of mathematics

questions.explanation: From ancient times, axioms have been acquired by man through the day to day experiences. No mathematical deduction is needed to prove them. So axioms are assumed universal truths in all branches of mathematics.

questions.question: Two intersecting lines cannot be parallel to the same line is stated in the form of
questions.explanation: Euclid's fifth postulate can also be stated in the following form: Two distinct intersecting lines cannot be parallel to the same line.

questions.question: The things which coincide with one another are
questions.explanation: "The things which coincide with one another are equal" is one of the axioms given by Euclid. Example:Segment AB = Segment BA ; ∠A = ∠A .

questions.question: Euclid stated that if equals are added to equals, the wholes are equal in the form of
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's second axiom stating addition of equals. An algebraic version of Euclid’s second axiom would read “if x = y, and if a = b, then x + a = y + b.”

questions.question: Euclid stated that if equals are subtracted from equals, the remainders are equal in the form of
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's third axiom stating subtraction of equals. An algebraic version of Euclid’s second axiom would read “if x = y, and if a = b, then x – a = y – b.”

questions.question: The number of line segments determined by three non-collinear points is
questions.explanation: You need two points to draw a line segment. If the points A,B and  are non-collinear,we can draw the lines :AB , AC, BA , BC, CA , CB. Now, line AB is the same as line BA, same for lines AC and CA and BC and CB. So,the lines are: AB, BC and AC ; 3 lines only.

questions.question: The number of  line segments determined by three collinear points is
questions.explanation: If the points are collinear then only one line can pass through them as collinear means "on the same line".

questions.question: If C lies between A and B and AB = 10cm, AC = 3cm, then BC2 =
questions.explanation: Since, AB = 10cm, C = 3 cm, therefore BC = AB - AC = 10 - 3 = 7cm. Hence, BC2 = 49 cm2

questions.question: Euclid’s fifth postulate implies the existence of
questions.explanation: According to Euclid's fifth postulate, if a straight line falling on two straight lines makes the interior angles on the same side of it taken together less than two right angles, then the two straight lines, if produced indefinitely, meet on that side on which the sum of angles is less than two right angles.

questions.question: The Sriyantra consists of ________ interwoven isosceles triangles.
questions.explanation: The Sri Yantra ('great object') belongs to a class of devices used in meditation, mainly by those belonging to the Hindu tantric tradition. The diagram consists of nine interwoven isosceles triangles four points upwards, representing Sakti, the primordial female essence of dynamic energy, and five-point downwards, representing Siva, the primordial male essence of static wisdom The triangles are arranged in such a way that they produce 43 subsidiary triangles, at the centre of the smallest of which there is a big dot known as the Bindu.

questions.question: A ________ is an exact location in space.
questions.explanation: Point is the basic building block of Geometry. Every shape is made through combining the points. A small dot marked by a pencil is a point. A point has no length or width. It has no thickness. Point is a mark of position. A point specifies the exact location. Point is denoted by a dot (.) and is named by an alphabet.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The basic facts which are taken for granted, without proof, are called
questions.explanation: An axiom is a proposition regarded as self-evidently true without proof

questions.question: A line segment has definite
questions.explanation: A line segment is a piece, or part, of a line in geometry. A line segment is represented by end points on each end of the line segment. A line in geometry is represented by a line with arrows at each end. A line segment and a line are different because a line goes on forever while a line segment has a distinct beginning and end.

questions.question: A line segment, when extended indefinitely in one direction is called a
questions.explanation: A ray is part of a line, has one fixed endpoint, and extends infinitely along the line from the endpoint.

questions.question: Three or more lines intersecting at the same point are said to be
questions.explanation: When three or more lines intersect in one point, they are concurrent. The point at which they intersect is the point of concurrency.

questions.question: The two lines which are parallel to the same line are _______ to each other.
questions.explanation:
There are 3 lines AB, CD, EF, where AB and EF are parallel, and CD and EF are parallel.
Let line PQ cross AB at G, EF at H, CD at K. On parallel lines AB and EF, angle AGK = GHF (Alternate interior angles).
On parallel lines AB and EF, angle AGK = GHF. (Alternate interior angles)
So, angle AGK = GKD. (Alternate interior angles)
So, AB is parallel to CD.

questions.question: The two lines which are perpendicular to the same line are _______ to each other.
questions.explanation: If a transversal cuts two different lines and if the sum of the corresponding angles is equal to 180 degrees. Then, the two given lines are parallel to each other and here, both the lines are perpendicular to the transversal. So, the sum of angles made by them on the transversal is 180 degrees. Hence, both the given lines are parallel to each other.

questions.question: A, B and C are three collinear points. How many lines can be determined by them?
questions.explanation: Since the three points are collinear, they lie on the same line,so only one line can be determined by them.

questions.question: Which one of the following statements is true?

options.html: A point determines always a unique line.
Three lines are concurrent when they have only one point in common.
A ray has two end points

questions.explanation: Lines which are concurrent have only one point in common and is called the point of concurrency.

questions.question:
Which one of the following statements is false?

  1. Only one line can pass through a single point
  2. Two circles are equal when their radii are equal
  3. A figure formed by line segments is called a rectilinear figure.
  4. A terminated line can be produced indefinitely on both the sides.
questions.explanation: Infinite number of lines can pass through a single point.

questions.question: Given four distinct points in a plane. How many line segments can be drawn using them when no three of them are collinear?
questions.explanation: If the four points are A,B,C and D, we can drawthe lines: A–B, A–C, A–D, B–C, B–D, C–D

questions.question: The number of end points a line has:
questions.explanation: A line has no end point. It extends in both the directions endlessly.

questions.question: The number of end points a line segment has:
questions.explanation: A line segment has two end points.

questions.question: The number of end points a ray has
questions.explanation: A ray starts at a given point and goes off in a certain direction to infinity. The point where the ray starts is called  the endpoint.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a rectilinear figure?
questions.explanation: A rectilinear figure is a figure all of whose edges meet at right angles. A circle has no edge.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a solid?
questions.explanation: Any 3D figure is considered to be a solid. A circle is a 2D figure, so, it cannot be considered as a solid.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following is a solid?
questions.explanation: All the other figures are 2D except cylinder which is 3D. Any 3D figure is considered to be a solid.

questions.question: If p, q and t are three straight lines such that p || q and p || t, then
questions.explanation: When two lines are parallel to the same line, they are parallel to each other.

questions.question: If AB, AC, AD and AE are parallel to a line q, then the points A, B, C, D and E are
questions.explanation: Since, AB, AC, AD and AE are parallel to the line ‘q’, therefore, point A is outside 'q' and lines AB, AC, AD and AE are drawn through A and each line is parallel to 'q'. But by parallel line axiom one and only line can be drawn through A parallel to the line 'q'. This is possible only when the points A, B, C, D and E all lie on the same line.

questions.question: Two lines are said to be _________, if they intersect at right angles.
questions.explanation: A line is perpendicular to another if it meets or crosses it at right angles (90°).

questions.question: The line segment with one end point at the centre and the other at any point on the circle is called ________.
questions.explanation: The radius of a circle is the distance from the center of the circle to any point on its circumference.

questions.question: A and B have the same weight. If they gain weight by 3 kg, then

options.html: Weight of A < Weight of B
Weight of A > Weight of B
Weight of A = Weight of B

questions.explanation:
Let the weights of A and B be x kgs. If both of them gain weight by 3 kgs, their new weight would be 'x + 3' kgs.
According to Euclid's axiom if equals are added in equals, then whole are equal.
Hence, Weight of A = Weight of B.

questions.question: If C is the mid-point of the line segment AB and L is the mid-point of AC, then
questions.explanation:
If C is the mid-point of the line segment AB ,then, AB = 2AC.

Now, L is the mid-point of AC, then, AC = 2AL.This follows that AB = 4AL.Hence,{tex}AL = \frac{1}{4}AB{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\overline {AB} = \overline {PQ} {/tex} and {tex}\overline {PQ} = \overline {XY} {/tex}, then
questions.explanation:
According to Euclid's first axiom that thing which is equal to the same thing, are equal to one another.
Hence, If two lines are equal to a third line, they will be equal in length.

questions.question: In this figure, if AC = BD, then
questions.explanation:
We have,AC = BD
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB + BC = BC + CD
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB = CD

questions.question: In the figure, if AB = BC and BX = BY, then
questions.explanation:
Since,AB = BC, we have AX + BX = CY + BY
and we have, BX = BY, hence, we can say that AX = CY

questions.question: In the figure, if AX = CY and BX = BY, then
questions.explanation: We have AX = CY and BX = BY, then we can say that AX + BX = CY + BY which means AB = BC

questions.question: A polygon is a closed figure made up of
questions.explanation: Polygons are 2-dimensional shapes. They are made of straight lines, and the shape is "closed" (all the lines connect up). Polygon comes from the Greek words, Poly- means "many" and -gon means "angle".

questions.question: The whole is _____________ the part.
questions.explanation: The whole is made up of its parts, hence, it will always be greater than the part.

questions.question: A statement whose truth can easily be deduced from a theorem is called
questions.explanation: A corollary is a proposition that follows from (and is often appended to) one already proved.

questions.question: If a straight line falling on two straight lines makes the interior angles on the same side of it taken together less than 180{tex}^\circ {/tex}, then the two straight lines, if produced indefinitely, meet on that side on which the angles taken together are
questions.explanation: According to Euclid's fifth postulate, if a straight line crossing two straight lines makes the interior angles on the same side less than two right angles, the two straight lines, if extended indefinitely, meet on that side on which are the angles less than the two right angles.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is 80{tex}^0{/tex} and two interior opposite angles are equal. Measure of each of these angles is :
questions.explanation:
We know that the exterior angle so formed is equal to the sum of the two interior opposite angles.
Let the two interior opposite angles be x.
So
x + x = 80o
2x = 80o
x = 40o

questions.question: The angle which is equal to 8 times its complement is:
questions.explanation:
We know that two angles, whose sum is 90o, are called the complementary angle.
Let one angle be x then its complementary angle be 8x
x + 8x = 90o
9x = 90o
x = 10o
its complementary angle is 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 = 80o

questions.question: If two supplementary angles are in the  ratio 2 : 7, then the angles are :
questions.explanation:
We know that supplementary angles are those angles whose sum is 180º
The two given supplementary angles are in the ratio 2 : 7
Let the common ratio be x
So angles are 2x and 7x respectively
{tex}2 x+7 x=180^{0}{/tex}
{tex}9 x=180^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{180^{\circ}}{9}=20^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}2 x=2 \times 20^{\circ}=40^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}7 x=7 \times 20^{0}=140^{0}{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, the value of x which makes POQ a straight line is:
questions.explanation:
We know that  he measure of a straight angle is 180°
(2x + 30°) + 4x = 180°
2x + 30° + 4x = 180°
6x = 180° - 30°
6x = 150°
x = {tex}\frac{150^{0}}{6}{/tex} = 25°

questions.question: If two interior angles on the same side of a transversal intersecting two parallel lines are in the ratio 2 : 3, then the greatest of two angles is
questions.explanation:
let a and b are two interior angles on the same side of a transversal intersecting two parallel lines are in the ratio 2 : 3  and we know that If a transversal intersects two parallel lines, then each pair of interior angles on the same side of the transversal is supplementary
let common ratio is x
a = 2x and b = 3x
a + b = 180o 
2x + 3x = 180o
5x = 180o
x = {tex}\frac{180^{0}}{5}{/tex} = 36o
x = 36o
3x = 3 {tex}\times {/tex} 36o = 108o

questions.question: In figure, PQ {tex}\parallel {/tex} RS, {tex}\angle{/tex} QPR = 70°, {tex}\angle {/tex} ROT = 20° find the value of x.
questions.explanation:
PQ {tex}\parallel{/tex} RS
{tex}\angle{/tex}QPR = {tex}\angle{/tex}SRO = 70° (Corresponding, Angle)
NOW IN {tex}\triangle {/tex}RTO
x + 20° = 70° (exterior angle)
x = 70° - 20°
x = 50°

questions.question: The exterior angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of two
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 = 180{tex}^0{/tex}  (Angle sum property)....(a)
{tex}\angle{/tex}3 + {tex}\angle{/tex}4  = 180{tex}^0{/tex} (Linear Pair)....(b)
On equating equations a and b, we get
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2  = {tex}\angle{/tex}4

questions.question: The angles of a triangle are in the ratio 5 : 3 : 7, the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let the angles of the triange be 5x, 3x and 7x
We know that the sum of the angles of a triangle is 180º
5x + 3x + 7x = 180º
15x = 180º
x = 12o 
Therefore the angles are
{tex}5 x=5 \times 12^{0}=60^{\circ}{/tex} 
{tex}3x = 3 \times {12^0} = {36^0}{/tex}
{tex} 7x = 7 \times {12^0} = {84^0} {/tex} 
Since all the angles are less than 90o there fore it is a acute angled triangle.

questions.question: If one angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the other two angles, then the triangle is
questions.explanation:
The sum of the angles of triangle is 180o.
let the angles of triangle be a, b, c
We have given that one angle of a triangle is equal to the sum of the other two angles
so we have
c = a + b
a + b + c = 180o
Substitute c for a + b
c + c = 180o
2c = 180o
c = 90o
Therefore the triangle is a right triangle.

questions.question: The complement of (90o - a) is :
questions.explanation:
Two angles, whose sum is 90o, are called the complementary angle.
Let x is a complimentary angle of (90o – a)
x + (90o - a) = 90o
x = 90o - (90o - a)
x = 90o - 90o + a
x = ao

questions.question: Given that lines l1, l2 and l3 in figure are parallel. The value of x is:
questions.explanation:
In the given figure 
40° + {tex}\angle{/tex}a = 180° (linear - pair)
Therefore {tex}\angle{/tex}a = 180° - 40° = 140°
Now {tex}\angle{/tex}a = {tex}\angle{/tex}b (corresponding - angles)
Similarly {tex}\angle{/tex}b = {tex}\angle{/tex}x = 140°
Therefore {tex}\angle x{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}x = 140°

questions.question: The number of lines that can pass through a given point is:
questions.explanation:
As seen from the above image, any number of lines can be drawn through a given point. 
Hence the answer may be given as "Infinity".

questions.question: In the given figure, the measure of {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC is :
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 20° (VERTICAL OPP ANGLE)
IN {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC
the exterior angle so formed is equal to the sum of the two interior opposite angles
{tex}\angle{/tex}A +{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 100°
20° +{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 100
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 100° - 20°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 80°

questions.question: In figure, lines l1 {tex}\parallel {/tex} l2. The value of x is :
questions.explanation:
40° + x = 70°(exterior angle)
{tex}\angle{/tex}x = 70° - 40°
 {tex}\angle{/tex}x = 30°

questions.question: The number of line segments determined by three given non-collinear points is:
questions.explanation: Three because non-collinear points means the point does not lies in a same line.

questions.question: An exterior angle of a triangle is {tex}{\rm{8}}{0^0}{/tex} and the interior opposite angles are in the ratio 1 : 3. Measure of each inte4rior opposite angle is :
questions.explanation:
let the common ratio is x
the ratio of interior angles are 1 : 3
so angles are x and 3x
x+3 x=80
4 x=80
{tex}x=\frac{80}{4}{/tex}
x=20
so angles are 200 and 600

questions.question: Two planes intersect each other to form a :
questions.explanation: As can be seen from the above diagram, the two planes "P" and "Q" are intersecting in a line, which is AB.

questions.question: In figure, AB and CD are parallel to each other. The value of x is:
questions.explanation:
let us draw a line from point E parallel to line AB , CD
X = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2
AB {tex}\parallel{/tex} EF
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + 120° = 180° (co - interior angle)
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 = 180° - 120°
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 = 60°
CD {tex}\parallel{/tex} EF
{tex}\angle{/tex}2 + 140° = 180° (co - interior angle)
{tex}\angle{/tex}2 = 180° - 140°
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 = 40°
X = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2
X = 60° + 40°

questions.question: If two interior angles on the same side of a transversal intersecting two parallel lines are in the ratio 5 : 4, then the smaller of the two angles is :
questions.explanation:
We know that sum of two interior angles on the same side of a transversal intersecting two parallel lines is 180o.
Let the common ratio is x.
So the angles are 5x, 4x.
So 5x + 4x = 180o
9x = 180o
x = {tex}\frac{180^o}{9}{/tex}
x = 20o
So the angles are 5x = 100o.
4x = 800
So smallest angle is 80o.

questions.question: The number of triangles that can be drawn having angles as 50o, 60o and 70o are :
questions.explanation:
As we know similar triangles can be drawn for any given triangle.
These similar triangles will have the same angles as the original triangle (ie {tex}\angle 50^0, \angle 60^0{/tex} and {tex}\angle 70^0 {/tex}) and will be infinite in number.

questions.question: How many triangles can be drawn having angles as 45o, 60o and 85o?
questions.explanation:
If we add up the three given angles we get 
45o + 60o + 85o = 190o
But as we know that the angles of any triangle only add-up to 180o. 
Hence the above-given triangle is not possible.

questions.question: The sides of a quadrilateral are extended in order to form 4 exterior angles. The sum of these exterior angles is :
questions.explanation:
As seen from the above figure: 
{tex}\angle 1+\angle2= 180^0{/tex} (Linear Pair)
Similarly {tex}\angle3+\angle4 = 180{/tex} = {tex}\angle 5+\angle 6= \angle7+\angle 8 = 180^0{/tex} 
Adding up all of the above
{tex}\angle 1+\angle 2+\angle 3+\angle 4+\angle5+\angle 6+\angle7+\angle8{/tex}= 180 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 7200  
But we know that the angles of a quadrilateral add up to 3600 
therefore {tex}\angle 2+\angle 3+\angle 5+\angle 7= 360^0{/tex} 
Substituting in the above equation we get: 
{tex}\angle 1+ \angle4+\angle6+\angle8 + 360^0 = 720^0{/tex} 
Therefore {tex}\angle 1+ \angle4+\angle6+\angle8 = 360^0{/tex} 
Hence the sum of all exterior angles = 3600

questions.question: In figure, if AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CF, CD {tex}\parallel {/tex} EF, then the value of x is :
questions.explanation:
In the above figure, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCF (Alternate Interior angles)
Now {tex}\angle{/tex}FCA = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}FCB 
= 60° + 50° = 110°
Now {tex}\angle{/tex}FCA = {tex}\angle{/tex}x (Alternate interior angles)
Therefore {tex}\angle{/tex}x = 110°

questions.question: The sum of all the angles of a quadrilateral is
questions.explanation:
Sum of the angles of a polygon = (n - 2) {tex}\times{/tex}180o
Quadrilateral has 4 sides,
So sum of interior angles = (4 - 2) {tex}\times{/tex} 180{tex}^0{/tex} = 360o

questions.question: Each angle of an equilateral triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let the angle of an equilateral triangle be xo
x + x + x = 180o (Angle sum property)
3x = 180o 
x = 60o

questions.question:
The incorrect statement is:

  1. Two lines drawn in a plane always intersect at a point.
  2. A line segment has definite length.
  3. Three lines are concurrent if and only if they have a common point.
  4. One and only one line can be drawn passing through a given point and parallel to a given line.
questions.explanation: If two lines intersect then they must lie in one plane but its converse is not necessarily true.

questions.question: If a transversal intersects two parallel lines, then which of the following alternatives is not true?

options.html: Each pair of co-interior angles is supplementary.
Each pair of co-interior angles is complementary.
Each pair of alternate interior angles is equal.
Each pair of corresponding angles is equal.

questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary not complementary.

questions.question: Two angles measure (70 + 2x)o and (3x – 15)o. If each angle is the supplement of the other, then the value of x is :
questions.explanation:
70 + 2x + 3x - 15 = 180 (Supplimentary angles)
5x = 180 - 55
x = 25

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, the value of x is:
In the adjoining figure, {tex}\angle {/tex}a and {tex}\angle {/tex}g are called:-
questions.explanation: {tex}\angle{/tex}a and {tex}\angle{/tex}g are on alternate side and are exterior.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, if m {tex}\parallel {/tex} n, then {tex}\angle {/tex}4 + {tex}\angle {/tex}7 is equal to -
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 = {tex}\angle{/tex}5 (Corresponding angle)

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, m {tex}\parallel {/tex} n, if {tex}\angle {/tex}1 = 50°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}2 is equal to -
questions.explanation:

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, m {tex}\parallel {/tex} n. If {tex}\angle {/tex}a : {tex}\angle {/tex}b = 2 : 3, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}h is -
questions.explanation:

2x + 3x = 180°
x = 36°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD and AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} EF. The value of x is :-
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}FEC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ECD = 180°    (sum of 2 supplimentary angles is 180°)

{tex}\angle{/tex}X=  {tex}\angle{/tex}BCE  = {tex}\angle{/tex}ECD 
{tex}\angle{/tex}X= 30° + 30° = 60°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, if AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} DE, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD is :-
questions.explanation:
x + 110° = 180° (Supplimentary angles)
x = 70°
y + 150° = 180° (Supplimentary angles)
y = 30°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 70° + 30° = 100°

questions.question: In the below figure, the value of x is :-
questions.explanation:
2x - 20° + x + 100° = 110° (Exterior angle is equal to sum of its interior opposite angles)
3x  = 110° - 100° + 20°
x = 10°

questions.question: In the given below figure, the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}AED is :-
questions.explanation:
In  {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 180°  (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180° - 25° - 45° 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 110°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 180°  (Linear pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 180° - 110° = 70°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}CED 
{tex}\angle{/tex}AED =  {tex}\angle{/tex}ECD + {tex}\angle{/tex}EDC  (Exterior angle is equal to sum of its two interior opposite angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}AED = 60° + 70° = 130°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, if QP {tex}\parallel {/tex} RT, then x is equal to –
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}QPR = {tex}\angle{/tex}PRT = 40° (Alternate interior angles)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}QPR
{tex}\angle{/tex}PQR + {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR + {tex}\angle{/tex}PRQ = 180° (Angle sum property)
65° + 40° + x° = 180°  
x° = 180° - 40° - 65°
x° = 75

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, l {tex} \parallel {/tex} m then {tex} \angle {/tex}x is equal to :-
questions.explanation:
x + 51° = 107° (Alternate interior angles)
x = 107° - 51° = 56°

questions.question: A, B, C are the three angles of a triangle. If A - B = 15o and B - C = 30o, then angles A, B, C are respectively:
questions.explanation:
Since ABC is a triangle 
A + B + C = 180o  (Angle sum property)....(i)
A - B = 15o 
A = 15o + B ....(ii)
B - C = 30o   
C = B - 30o ....(iii)
From (i) equation
15o + B + B + B - 30o = 180o 
B = 65o 
From equation (ii) and (iii)
A = 15o + B = 15o + 65o = 80o
C = B - 30o = 65o - 30o = 35o

questions.question: The angles of a triangle in ascending order are x, y, z and y - x = z - y = 10o. The smallest angles is
questions.explanation:
x + y + z = 180o ....(i) (Angle sum property)
y - x = 10o
y - 10o = x ....(ii)
z - y = 10o 
z = 10o + y ....(iii)
On putting the value of x and z in equation (i)
y - 10o + y + 10o + y = 180o 
y = 60o 
x = 50o (From equation ii)
z = 70o (From equation iii)
Smallest angle is x = 50o

questions.question: In the adjoining figure PQ || XR. If QP {tex} \bot {/tex} SP, then the values of x and y are:-
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}QXR
28° + {tex}\angle{/tex}QXR = {tex}\angle{/tex}QRT (Exterior angle property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}QXR = 65° - 28° = 37°
y = {tex}\angle{/tex}QSR = 37° (Alternate interior angles)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQX
{tex}\angle{/tex}PQX + {tex}\angle{/tex}QXP + {tex}\angle{/tex}QPX = 180° (Angle sum property)
37° + x + 90° = 180°
x = 53°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, the three lines AB, CD and EF all pass through the point O. If {tex}\angle {/tex}EOB = 90° and x:y = 2:1 then {tex} \angle {/tex}BOD and {tex}\angle {/tex}COE:-
questions.explanation:
x + y + 90° = 180° (Linear Pair)
2a + a + 90° = 180° (Since, x:y = 2:1)
a =  30°
x = 2a = {tex} \angle{/tex}COE = 60° (Vertically opposite angles)
y = {tex} \angle{/tex}BOD = 30° (Vertically opposite angles)

questions.question: In the adjoining figure {tex}\angle {/tex}QPR = 62° and {tex}\angle {/tex}PRQ = 64°. If OQ and OR and bisectors of {tex}\angle {/tex}PQR and {tex}\angle {/tex} PRQ respectively, then {tex}\angle {/tex}OQR and {tex}\angle {/tex}QOR :-
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR 
{tex}\angle{/tex}QPR + {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR + {tex}\angle{/tex}PRQ = 180° (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = 180° - 62° - 64°
{tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = 54°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ORQ = 32°  (OR is a bisector )
{tex}\angle{/tex}OQR = 27°  (OQ is a bisector)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OQR 
{tex}\angle{/tex}OQR  + {tex}\angle{/tex}ORQ + {tex}\angle{/tex}QOR = 180°  (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}QOR = 180° - 32° - 27° = 121°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD and AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} EF. If EA {tex} \bot {/tex} BA and {tex}\angle {/tex}BEF = 55°, then the values of x, y and z :-
questions.explanation:
x + 55 = 180° (Sum of supplementary angles or co-interior angles)
x = 125°
x = y = 125° (Corresponding angles)
z + {tex}\angle{/tex}EAB = y (Exterior angle property)
z = 125° - 90° = 35°

tests.name: Lines and Angles Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Lines and Angles CBSE Online Test 04. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: If two angles are supplementary and the larger is 20o less then three times the smaller, then the angles are
questions.explanation:
Let the two supplementary angles be xo and 180o - xo 
Let 180o - x be the larger angle
180{tex}^0{/tex} - x = 3x - 20o 
4x = 200o 
x = 50o 
So the angles are 50o and 130o.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, if DE {tex}\parallel {/tex} BC, then the values of x and y are:-
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}EAB  +  {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180°  (Sum of supplemetary angle)
2x - 5° + 3x + 60° = 180° 
5x = 180° - 60° + 5 °
x = 25°
{tex}\angle{/tex}EAC + y = 180° (Sum of supplemetary angle)
2x - 5° + y = 180° 
y = 180° + 5° - 50° = 135°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, if A = 40°, B = 95° and D = 60°, then E is equal to:-
questions.explanation:
In  {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}CAB = 180° (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = 180° - 95° - 40° = 45°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = {tex}\angle{/tex}ECD = 45° (Vertically opposite angle)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECD 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ECD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DEC + {tex}\angle{/tex}CDE = 180° (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ECD = 180° - 45° - 60° = 75°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, the bisectors of {tex}\angle {/tex}CBD and {tex}\angle {/tex}BCE meet at the point O. If {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC = 70°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}BOC is equal to :-
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 90° - {tex}\frac12{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 90° - 35° = 55°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, BE and CE are bisectors of {tex} \angle {/tex}ABC and {tex} \angle {/tex}ACD respectively. If {tex} \angle {/tex}BEC = 25°, then {tex} \angle {/tex}BAC is equal to :-
questions.explanation:
In {tex} \triangle{/tex}BEC 
{tex} \angle{/tex}BEC + {tex} \angle{/tex}EBC = {tex} \angle{/tex}ECD (Exterior angle property)
{tex} \angle{/tex}BEC = {tex} \angle{/tex}ECD - {tex} \angle{/tex}EBC 
In {tex} \triangle{/tex}ABC 
{tex} \angle{/tex}ABC + {tex} \angle{/tex}BAC = {tex} \angle{/tex}ACD 
{tex} \angle{/tex}ABC + 2{tex} \angle{/tex}EBC = 2{tex} \angle{/tex}ECD
{tex} \angle{/tex}ABC = 2({tex} \angle{/tex}ECD - {tex} \angle{/tex}EBC)
{tex} \angle{/tex}ABC = 2({tex} \angle{/tex}BEC)
{tex} \angle{/tex}ABC = 50°

questions.question: In the given figure AB is a mirror, PQ is the incident ray and QR is the reflected ray. If {tex}\angle {/tex}PQR = 108°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}AQP = ?
questions.explanation:
According to question,
{tex}\angle{/tex}AQP = {tex}\angle{/tex}BQR = x
{tex}\angle{/tex}AQP + {tex}\angle{/tex}BQR + {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR  = 180° (Linear Pair)
2x + 108° = 180°
x = 36°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD and O is a point joined with B and D, as shown in the figure such that {tex}\angle {/tex}ABO = 35° and {tex}\angle {/tex}CDO = 40°. Reflex {tex}\angle {/tex}BOD = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABO + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOE = 180° (Sum of supplementary angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOE = 180° - 35° = 145°
{tex}\angle{/tex}CDO + {tex}\angle{/tex}DOE = 180° (Sum of supplementary angles)
Reflex of {tex}\angle{/tex}BOD = {tex}\angle{/tex}BOE + {tex}\angle{/tex}DOE = 145° + 140°
Reflex of {tex}\angle{/tex}BOD = 285°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD. If {tex}\angle {/tex}BAO = 60° and {tex}\angle {/tex}OCD = 110°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}AOC = ?
questions.explanation:
Construction: Extend the line CD such that it intersect AO and is parallel to AB
x = 60° (Corresponding angles)
x + y + 180° - 110° = 180° (Angle sum property)
y = 110° - 60° = 50°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD. If {tex}\angle {/tex}AOC = 30° and {tex}\angle {/tex}OAB = 100°. then {tex}\angle {/tex}OCD =?
questions.explanation:
Draw {tex}O E\|A B\| C D{/tex}
Now, {tex}O E \| A B{/tex} and OA is the transversal.
{tex}\therefore \angle O A B+\angle A O E=180^{\circ}{/tex} [Angles on the same side of a transversal line are supplementary]
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle O A B+\angle A O C+\angle C O E=180^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 100^{\circ}+30^{\circ}+\angle C O E=180^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle C O E=50^{\circ}{/tex}
Also,
{tex}O E \| C D{/tex} and OC is the transversal.
{tex}\therefore \angle O C D+\angle C O E=180^{\circ}{/tex} [Angles on the same side of a transversal line are supplementary]
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle O C D+50^{\circ}=180^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle O C D=130^{\circ}{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD, If {tex}\angle {/tex}CAB = 80° and {tex}\angle {/tex}EFC = 25°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}CEF = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAF  =  {tex}\angle{/tex}DCF = 80° (Corresponding angle)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}CEF 
{tex}\angle{/tex}CFE + {tex}\angle{/tex}CEF = {tex}\angle{/tex}DCF (exterior angle is equal to the sum of its two opposite interior angles )
25° + {tex}\angle{/tex}CEF = 80°
{tex}\angle{/tex}CEF  =  55°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD, If {tex}\angle {/tex}APQ = 70° and {tex}\angle {/tex}PRD = 120°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}QPR = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}APQ  =  {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR  = 70°  (Alternate interior angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}PRQ  +  {tex}\angle{/tex}PRD  = 180°  (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}PRQ  = 180° - 120° = 60°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR 
{tex}\angle{/tex}PQR + {tex}\angle{/tex}PRQ + {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR = 180°  (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}QPR = 180° - 70° - 60° = 50°

questions.question: In the given figure the value of x =?
questions.explanation:
OBCA is a quadrilateral
{tex} \angle{/tex}OAC + {tex} \angle{/tex}BOA + {tex} \angle{/tex}ACB + {tex} \angle{/tex}CBO = 360°
{tex}\gamma{/tex} + {tex}\beta{/tex} + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB + {tex}\alpha{/tex} = 360°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 360{tex}^0{/tex} - {tex}\gamma{/tex} - {tex}\beta{/tex} - {tex}\alpha{/tex}
x = 360° - {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB
x =  {tex}\gamma{/tex} + {tex}\beta{/tex}  + {tex}\alpha{/tex}

questions.question: If {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 4{tex}\angle {/tex}B = 6{tex}\angle {/tex}C, then A : B : C ?
questions.explanation:
Let A be x
B = {tex}\frac14{/tex}x 
C ={tex}\frac16{/tex} x 
A : B : C
x : {tex}\frac14{/tex}x :{tex}\frac16{/tex} x
LCM of 4 and 6 is 12
12 : 3 : 2

questions.question: Side BC of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC has been produced to D on left-hand side and to E on right-hand side such that {tex}\angle {/tex}ABD = 125° and {tex}\angle {/tex}ACE = 130°. Then {tex}\angle {/tex}A = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC  = 180° (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180{tex}^0{/tex} - 125° = 55°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACE + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180° (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180° - 130° = 50° 
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 180° (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 180° - 50° -55° 
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC  = 75°

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC = 30°, {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = 50° and {tex}\angle {/tex}CDE = 40°. Then {tex}\angle {/tex}AED = ?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA  = 180° (Angle sum property)
50°  + 30° + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA  = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = 100°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECD 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ECD +  {tex}\angle{/tex}CDE  +  {tex}\angle{/tex}CED  = 180° (Angle sum property)
180° - {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA  + 40° + {tex}\angle{/tex}CED = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}CED = 100° - 40° = 60°
{tex}\angle{/tex}CED + {tex}\angle{/tex}AED = 180° (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}AED = 180° - 60° = 120°

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
