If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC = 40°, {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB = 90° and {tex}\angle {/tex}BED = 100°, Then {tex}\angle {/tex}BDE =?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180°  (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180° - 90° - 40°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 50°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BED 
{tex}\angle{/tex}BED + {tex}\angle{/tex}EBD + {tex}\angle{/tex}BDE = 180°  (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BDE = 180° - 50° - 100°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BDE= 30°

questions.question: In the given figure, BO and CO are the bisectors of {tex}\angle {/tex}B and {tex}\angle {/tex}C respectively. If {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 50°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}BOC = ?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}​​​​\triangle{/tex}ABC
2x + 2y + {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 180° (Angle sum property)
x + y + ({tex}\angle{/tex}A/2) = 90°
x + y = 90° – (A/2) ...1
In {tex}​​​​\triangle{/tex}BOC, we have
x + y + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 180°
90° - ({tex}\angle{/tex}A/2) + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 180° [From (1)]
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 180° - 90° + (A/2)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 90° + (A/2)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 90° + 25° = 115°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD. If {tex}\angle {/tex}EAB = 50° and {tex}\angle {/tex}ECD = 60°, then {tex}\angle {/tex}AEB =?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD ={tex}\angle{/tex} ABE = 60° (Vertically opposite angle)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}EAB
{tex}\angle{/tex}EAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}EBA + {tex}\angle{/tex}AEB + 180{tex}^0{/tex}   (Angle sum property)
50° + 60° + {tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 70°

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\angle {/tex}OEB = 75°, {tex}\angle {/tex}OBE = 55° and {tex}\angle {/tex}OCD = 100°. Then {tex}\angle {/tex}ODC = ?
questions.explanation:
In  {tex}\triangle{/tex}OEB
{tex}\angle{/tex}OEB + {tex}\angle{/tex}EBO + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOE = 180° (Angle sum property)
75° + 55° + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOE = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOE = 50°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOE = {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 50° (Vertically opposite angle)
In  {tex}\triangle{/tex}ODC
{tex}\angle{/tex}ODC + {tex}\angle{/tex}DOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}DCO = 180° 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ODC = 180° - 100° - 50°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ODC = 30°

questions.question: An angle is one-fifth of its supplement. The measure of the angle is :-
questions.explanation:
Let one angle be xo
Its supplementary angle will be 180o - xo
According to question
x = {tex}\frac15{/tex}(180o - x) 
5x + x = 180o
6x = 180o
x = {tex}\frac {180}6{/tex}
x = 30{tex}^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex} \parallel {/tex} CD, If {tex}\angle {/tex}ABO = 45° and {tex}\angle {/tex}COD = 100° then {tex}\angle {/tex}CDO = ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC =  {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 45° (Alternate interior angles)
In COD
{tex}\angle{/tex}COD + {tex}\angle{/tex}CDO + {tex}\angle{/tex}DCO = 180° (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}CDO = 180° - 100° - 45°
{tex}\angle{/tex}CDO = 35°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex} \parallel {/tex} DC, {tex}\angle {/tex}BAD = 90°, {tex}\angle {/tex}CBD = 28° and {tex}\angle {/tex}BCE = 65°. Then {tex}\angle {/tex}ABD = ?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}DBC
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCE = {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC    (Exterior angle property)
65°  = 28° + {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = 37
As, AB is parallel to CD
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = 37° (Alternate interior angle)

questions.question: For what value of x shall we have l{tex} \parallel {/tex}m?
questions.explanation:
(2x-30)° = (x+20)° (corresponding angle)
2x -30° = x + 20°
2x - x = 30° + 20°
x = 50°

questions.question: In the given figure, sides CB and BA of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC have been produced to D and E respectively such that {tex}\angle {/tex}ABD = 110° and {tex}\angle {/tex}CAE = 135°. Then {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB =?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}EAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 180° (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}EAC = 135°
135° + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 180° - 135°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC  = 45°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180° (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = 110°
110° + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180° - 110°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 70°
In ABC
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180°
45° + 70° + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180°
115° + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180° - 115°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 65°

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, BD {tex} \bot {/tex} AC, {tex}\angle {/tex}CAE = 30° and {tex}\angle {/tex}CBD = 40°. Then {tex}\angle {/tex}AEB =?
questions.explanation:
In BDC
{tex}\angle{/tex}BDC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 180°
BD {tex}\perp{/tex} AC
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 90°, {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 40°
90° + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD + 40° = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD + 130° = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 180° - 130°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 50°
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAE + {tex}\angle{/tex}C ...(exterior angle)
{tex}\angle{/tex}CAE = 30°
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 50°
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 30° + 50°
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 80°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex} \parallel {/tex} CD, CD {tex} \parallel {/tex} EF and y : z = 3 : 7, then x =?
questions.explanation:
y : z = 3 : 7
Let common ratio be a
y = 3a
z = 7a
x = z (corresponding angle)
x = 7a
x + y =180° (interior angle)
7a + 3a =180°
10 a = 180°
a = 180/10
a = 18
x = 7a
x = 7x 18
x =126°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex} \parallel {/tex} CD {tex} \parallel {/tex} EF, EA {tex} \bot {/tex} AB and BDE is the transversal such that {tex}\angle {/tex}DEF = 55°, Then {tex}\angle {/tex}AEB =?
questions.explanation:
EA {tex}\perp{/tex} AB
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEF = 90°
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEF = {tex}\angle{/tex}BEF + {tex}\angle{/tex}AEB
{tex}\angle{/tex}BEF + {tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 90°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BEF = 55°
55° + {tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 90°
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 90° - 55°
{tex}\angle{/tex}AEB = 35°

questions.question: In the given figure, AM {tex} \bot {/tex}BC and AN is the bisector of {tex}\angle {/tex}A. If {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = 70° and {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB = 20°, then MAN = ?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC+ {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = 180° (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 180° - 70° - 20°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 90°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ANC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}ANC + {tex}\angle{/tex}NAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACN = 180° (Angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ANC + 45° + 20° = 180° (AN is angle bisector of {tex}\angle{/tex}A)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ANC = 115°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AMN
{tex}\angle{/tex}AMN + {tex}\angle{/tex}MAN = {tex}\angle{/tex}ANC  (Measure of exterior angle is equla to the sum of two opposite interior angles)
90° + {tex}\angle{/tex}MAN = 115°
{tex}\angle{/tex}MAN = 25°

questions.question: The sides BC, CA and AB of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC have been produced to D, E and F respectively as shown in the figure, forming exterior angles {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD, {tex}\angle {/tex}BAE and {tex}\angle {/tex}CBF. Then, {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD +{tex}\angle {/tex}BAE + {tex}\angle {/tex}CBF =?
questions.explanation:
We know that the exteior angle is sum of opposite interior angle
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 ...(i)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAE = {tex}\angle{/tex}3 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 ...(ii)
{tex}\angle{/tex}CBF = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 ...(iii)
ADD Equation (1i) , (ii)  and (iii)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3)
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 = 180° (ASP)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + We know that the exteior angle is sum of opposite interior angle
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 ...(i)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAE = {tex}\angle{/tex}3 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 ...(ii)
{tex}\angle{/tex}CBF = {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 ...(iii)
ADD Equation (1i) , (ii)  and (iii)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} ({tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3)
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 = 180° (ASP)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF = 360°

questions.question: The sides BC, BA and CA of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC have been produced to D, E and F respectively, as shown in the give figure, Then, {tex}\angle {/tex}B ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}FAE = {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC(VOA)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 35°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 180° (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB + 110° = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180° - 110°
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 70°
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180°
35° + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + 70° = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}B + 105° = 180°
{tex}\angle{/tex}B 180° - 105°
{tex}\angle{/tex}B = 75°

tests.name: Triangles Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Triangles CBSE Online Test 01. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: An exterior angle of the triangle is 110o, One of the opposite interior angle is 50o. What are the other two angles?
questions.explanation:
Let the other opposite interior angle be x and the remaining angle of the triangle be z.
Sum of the opposite two interior angle = Exterior angle
50o + x = 110o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 60o
Now , 60o + 50o + z =180o    (Angle sum property)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = 70o
Therefore, the other two angles of the triangle = 60o and  70o

questions.question:
Which of the following statements is true?

  1. A triangle can have two acute angles.
  2. A triangle can have two right angles.
  3. A triangle can have two obtuse angles.
  4. All are true.
questions.explanation: A triangle can have two or even all three acute angles (in case of an equilateral triangle) but it cannot have two right angles or two obtuse angles as the sum of the interior angles of a triangle is 180o and two right angles or two obtuse angles would sum up to 180o or more leaving the no space for the third angle.

questions.question: If all the altitudes from the vertices to the opposite sides of a triangle are equal, then the triangle is
questions.explanation: In an equilateral triangle all the altitudes, sides, angles, perpendicular bisectors, medians and angular bisectors are equal.

questions.question: If O is any point in the interior of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC, then, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation:
Sum of any two sides of a triangle is greater than the third side.
Join O with all sides of the triangle, we have   
OA + OB > AB .....(i) 
OA + OC > AC. ....(ii)
and, OB + OC > BC .....(iii)
Adding the three inequalities i.e. (i) + (ii) + (iii), we get 
2(OA + OB + OC) > AB + BC + AC
i.e.(OA + OB + OC) > {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} (AB + BC + CA).

questions.question: Which of the following is not the criterion for similarity of triangles?
questions.explanation: LCM

questions.question: The perimeter of a triangle is 36 cm and its sides are in the ratio a : b : c = 3 : 4 : 5  then a, b, c are respectively:
questions.explanation:
Let the three sides a, b, c be 3x, 4x and 5x respectively.
Then according to the conditions given in the question, we have
3x +  4x + 5x = 36
12x = 36
x = 3 cm
Thus, the three sides are :
a = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 9 cm, b = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 12 cm and c = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 15 cm

questions.question: It is given that {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC{tex} \cong {/tex}{tex}\triangle {/tex}FDE in which AB = 5 cm, {tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}{40^ \circ }{/tex},{tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}{80^ \circ }{/tex}and FD = 5 cm. Then,which of the following is not true?
questions.explanation:
Since, {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC{tex} \cong {/tex}{tex}\triangle {/tex}FDE,

Therefore, {tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}\angle {/tex}D = 40o, {tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}\angle {/tex}F = 80o and  {tex}\angle {/tex}C = {tex}\angle {/tex}E = 60o

questions.question: Two sides of a triangle are of lengths 5 cm and 1.5 cm. The length of the third side of the triangle cannot be
questions.explanation: Given that: Two sides of triangle are 5 cm and 1.5 cm. We know that the sum of two sides of the triangle is always greater than the third side. Hence, 3.4 cm cannot be the third side. If it is the third side the sum of 3.4 cm and 1.5 cm will be smaller than 5 cm, so, the triangle will not be possible.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle {/tex} ABC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF, AB = DE and {tex}\angle A{/tex} = {tex}\angle D{/tex}. Then, the two triangles will be congruent by SAS axiom if:
questions.explanation:
The SAS rule states that :
If two sides and the included angle of one triangle are equal to two sides and included angle of another triangle, then the triangles are congruent.
Here, in {tex}\Delta{/tex}ABC, the two sides are AB and AC and the included angle is {tex}\angle A{/tex}. For {tex}\Delta {/tex} DEF, the two corresponding sides are DE  and DF and the included angle is {tex}\angle D{/tex} 
Hence, the two triangles will be congruent by SAS axiom if AC = DF.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 35o and {tex}\angle B{/tex} = 65o, then the longest side of the triangle is:
questions.explanation:
As per angle sum property, {tex} \angle A\ + \angle B + \angle C{/tex} = 180o   
Hence, 35o + 65o + {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 80o which is the greatest angle.
We know that the side opposite to the greatest angle i.e AB would be the greatest.
Hence, AB is the longest side.

questions.question: P is a point on side BC of a {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC such that AP bisects {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC. Then
questions.explanation:
Since, AP bisects {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC. Hence the point P on BC also bisects it as it the opposite side of {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC.
Hence, we can conclude that, BA > BP.

questions.question: In the given figure, AD is the median, then {tex}\angle {/tex}BAD is:
questions.explanation:
In the given {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, AB = AC
Hence, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 40o
Now , by angle sum property,
{tex}\angle{/tex}A +  {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}A + 40o + 40o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow {/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 100o
Now, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAD and {tex}\triangle{/tex}DAC
AB = AC (given)
BD =DC ( D is the mid-point of BC)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 40o
Hence by SAS ,  {tex}\triangle{/tex}BAD and {tex}\triangle{/tex}DAC are congruent.
This means , {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 100o = 50o
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD =  50o

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR, AB = PR and {tex}\angle A{/tex} = {tex}\angle P{/tex}. Then, the two triangles will be congruent by SAS axiom if:
questions.explanation: {tex}\angle A{/tex} is included between AB and AC and {tex}\angle P{/tex} is included between PQ and PR and corresponding sides must be equal. Since AB = PR, hence AC = PQ for the given triangles to be congruent by SAS axiom.

questions.question: If {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex}{tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR by SSS congruence rule, then:
questions.explanation: If {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC {tex} \cong {/tex}{tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR by SSS congruence rule, then the corresponding sides must be equal i.e AB = PQ, BC = QR and AC = PR.

questions.question: D, E and F are the mid points of sides AB, BC and CA of {tex}\Delta ABC{/tex}. If perimetre of {tex}\Delta ABC{/tex} is 16 cm, then perimetre of {tex}\Delta DEF{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Using relation
{tex}perimeter.\Delta DEF = \frac{1}{2}perimeter.\Delta ABC{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{1}{2} \times 16 = 8cm{/tex}

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR, {tex}\angle P{/tex} = 60o, {tex}\angle Q{/tex} = 50o. Which side of the triangle is the longest?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle PQR{/tex}, {tex}\angle P{/tex} = 60o, {tex}\angle Q {/tex} = 50o 
Now, by angle sum property, {tex}\angle \ P \ + \angle \ Q \ +\angle \ R{/tex} = 180o 
60o + 50o + {tex}\angle\ R{/tex} = 180o
or, {tex}\angle\ R{/tex} = 180o - 110o = 70o
So, {tex}\angle\ R{/tex} is the largest angle and the side opposite to it ,i.e, PQ will be the longest side.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR, {tex}\angle P{/tex} = 60o, {tex}\angle Q{/tex} = 50o. Which side of the triangle is the longest?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle PQR{/tex}, {tex}\angle P{/tex} = 60o, {tex}\angle Q {/tex} = 50o 
Now, by angle sum property, {tex}\angle \ P \ + \angle \ Q \ +\angle \ R{/tex} = 180o 
60o + 50o + {tex}\angle\ R{/tex} = 180o
or, {tex}\angle\ R{/tex} = 180o - 110o = 70o
So, {tex}\angle\ R{/tex} is the largest angle and the side opposite to it ,i.e, PQ will be the longest side.

questions.question: AD is the median of the triangle. Which of the following is true?
questions.explanation:
In triangle ADB 
AB + BD > AD
In triangle ADC
AC+DC > AD
Adding both
AB + AC + BD + DC > 2AD
Now BD + DC = BC
So, AB + AC + BC > 2AD

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 50o, {tex}\angle B {/tex} = 60o, Find the longest side of the triangle
questions.explanation:
By angle sum property, we have,
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 50o + 60o + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o - (50o+ 60o) = 70o
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}C is the largest angle in the triangle and the side opposite to it i.e. AB is the longest side.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle {/tex}AOC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}XYZ, {tex}\angle A = \angle X{/tex}, AO = XY, AC = XZ, then by which congruence rule {tex}\triangle AOC \cong \triangle XYZ{/tex}?
questions.explanation: According to SAS criterion, if the corresponding sides and their included angles are equal, then the triangles are congruent. Here, in  {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}XYZ, AO = XY, and AC = XZ are the corresponding sides and {tex}\angle A = \angle X{/tex} are included angles, Hence, {tex}\triangle AOC \cong \triangle XYZ{/tex}, by SAS.

questions.question: It is not possible to construct a triangle when its sides are:
questions.explanation: In a triangle, the sum of any two sides must be greater than the third side and here 2.3 + 3.1 = 5.4 and hence, the triangle is not possible with the given measurements.

questions.question: {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} {tex}\cong {/tex} {tex}\triangle PQR{/tex}, then which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Corresponding sides are equal for two congruent triangles.

questions.question: In figure, ABCD is a quadrilateral in which AB = BC and AD = DC. The measure of {tex}\angle BCD{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Join AC. We get two isosceles triangles,{tex}\Delta{/tex} ABC and {tex}\Delta{/tex} ACD
In {tex}\Delta{/tex} ABC,{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC= 108o
{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = (180o - 108o) / 2 = {tex}\frac{72^\circ}{2}{/tex} = 36o
In {tex}\Delta{/tex} ACD,{tex}\angle{/tex}ADC= 42o
{tex}\therefore{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}DAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}DCA = (180o - 42o) / 2 = 138o/2 = 69o
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex} BCD = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}DCA = 36o + 69o = 105o

questions.question: D is a point on the side BC of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} such that AD bisects {tex}\angle BAC{/tex} then:
questions.explanation: Since,{tex}\angle BAC{/tex}  is bisected by AD, then {tex}\angle BAD{/tex} is  less than {tex}\angle ABC{/tex} ,hence the side opposite {tex}\angle ABC{/tex}, i.e. BA  is greater than the side opposite to {tex}\angle BAD{/tex} i.e.BD

questions.question: In right-angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF if {tex}\angle E {/tex} = 90o, then:
questions.explanation: In a triangle, only one right angle is possible, hence it is the greatest angle and the side opposite to it is the longest side. Here, the side opposite to {tex}\angle\ E{/tex} is DF, hence, it is the longest side.

questions.question: In the above figure AB {tex}\parallel{/tex} CD ,O is the mid point BC. Which of the following is true?

options.html: O is the mid point of AD

questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB and {tex}\triangle{/tex}DOC
{tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = {tex}\angle{/tex}ODC (alternate interior angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}OBA={tex}\angle{/tex}OCD
OB = OC (given)
So, from ASA congruence ,we have 
{tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB ≅ {tex}\triangle{/tex}DOC 
Now, from CPCT ,we have
AB = CD
OA = OD which means O is the mid-point of AD.
Hence ,all the given statements are true.

questions.question: PQR is a right-angled triangle in which {tex}\angle{/tex}P = 90o and PQ = PR. What is the value of {tex}\angle{/tex}Q and {tex}\angle{/tex}R
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}P = 90o
Since, PQ = PR
∠Q =∠R 
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = {tex}\angle{/tex}R = {tex}\frac{180^o - \angle P}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{180^o - 90^o}{2}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{90^o}{2}{/tex} = 45o

questions.question: In the above quadrilateral ACBD, we have AC = AD and AB bisect the ∠A .Which of the following is true?
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABC and ABD ,we have
AC = AD
{tex}\angle{/tex}AB = {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD 
AB = AB
By SAS ,we have
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD 
Hence, we have BC = BD and {tex}\angle{/tex}C = {tex}\angle{/tex}D.
So,all the given options are true.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR, {tex}\angle{/tex}R = {tex}\angle{/tex}P and QR = 4 cm and PR = 5 cm. Then the length of PQ is
questions.explanation:
In a triangle, if two of its angles are equal then the sides opposite to equal angles are also equal.
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQR, {tex}\angle{/tex}R = {tex}\angle{/tex}P
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} QR (side opposite to {tex}\angle{/tex}P) = PQ (side opposite to {tex}\angle{/tex}R)
Given that, QR = 4 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} PQ = 4 cm

questions.question: In an isosceles, {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} AB = AC and side BA is produced to D such that AB=AD. Then the measure of {tex}\angle BCD{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Given in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, AB = AC
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB (Since angles opposite to equal sides are equal)
Also given that AD = AB
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD (Since angles opposite to equal sides are equal)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = x(AB = AC = AD)
Also,{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = A{tex}\angle{/tex}CB + ∠C{tex}\angle{/tex}D = x + x = 2x
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BCD, {tex}\angle{/tex}CBD + BC{tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = 180o
x + 2x + x = 180o
4x = 180o
x = 45o
{tex}\angle{/tex}BCD =  2x = 90o

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, the rule by which {tex}\triangle ABC \cong \triangle ADC{/tex}
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC,we have,
AB = AD (4cm)
BC = DC (2.7 cm)
AC = AC (commom in both)
Hence ,{tex}\triangle ABC \cong \triangle ADC{/tex}, by SSS criterion.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, BC = AD, CA{tex} \bot {/tex}AB and BD{tex} \bot {/tex}AB. The rule by which {tex}\triangle ABC \cong \triangle BAD{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex} ABC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}{tex}\angle \ BAC \ = \angle\ ABD{/tex}
BAD, we have   (Right angles)
BC = AD (Hypotentuses and Given)
AB = AB (common in both)
Hence, {tex}\triangle ABC \cong \triangle BAD{/tex} by RHS criterion.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB = AD and AC{tex} \bot {/tex}BD. The rule by which {tex}\triangle ABC \cong \triangle ADC{/tex}is
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC, we have,
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD (Right angles)
AB = AD ( Given and hyptenuses)
AC = AC (common in both)
Therefore, {tex}\triangle ABC \cong \triangle ADC{/tex} by RHS.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB = AC and AD is bisector of {tex}\angle {/tex}A. The rule by which {tex}\triangle ABD \cong \triangle ACD{/tex}
questions.explanation:
In  {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD and {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC, we have
AB = AC (Given)
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC   ( Since AD, bisects {tex}\angle{/tex}A)
AD = AD ( common in both)
Hence, {tex}\triangle ABD \cong \triangle ACD{/tex} by SAS

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, {tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}\angle {/tex}C and AD{tex} \bot {/tex}BC. The rule by which {tex}\triangle ABD \cong \triangle ADC{/tex}
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD and {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADC, we have
{tex}\angle {/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD   (given)
{tex}\angle {/tex}BDA = {tex}\angle {/tex}CDA    ( 90o)
AD = AD (common in both)
Hence, {tex}\triangle ABD \cong \triangle ADC{/tex}, by AAS criterion

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB = FC, EF = BD and {tex}\angle {/tex}AFE = {tex}\angle {/tex}CBD. Then the rule by which {tex}\triangle AFE \cong \triangle CBD{/tex}
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}DBC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}AEF, we have
AB = FC (given)by adding BF on both sides
AF= CB
{tex}\angle {/tex}AFE = {tex}\angle {/tex}CBD  (given)
EF = BD        (given)
Hence, {tex}\triangle AFE \cong \triangle CBD{/tex} by SAS as the corresponding sides and their included angles are equal.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB = AC and AD is median of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, then {tex}\angle {/tex}ADC is equal to
questions.explanation: As AD is the perpendicular bisector of BC, so {tex}\angle {/tex}ADC = {tex}\angle {/tex}ADB = 90o

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, {tex}\triangle ABC \cong \triangle ADC{/tex}. If {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC = {tex}30^\circ {/tex}and {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = {tex}100^\circ {/tex} then {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD is equal to
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABC, {tex}\angle {/tex}BAC = 30o and {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = 100o (Given)
{tex}\angle {/tex}BAC + {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle {/tex}BCA = 180o
{tex}\angle {/tex}BCA = 50o
Also ∠ACD = 50o (Since, △ABC≅△ADC)

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, O is Mid – point of AB. If {tex}\angle {/tex}ACO = {tex}\angle {/tex}BDO, then {tex}\angle {/tex}OAC is equal to
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle {/tex}OAC and {tex}\triangle {/tex}OBD,
AO = OB   As O is the mid point of AB
{tex}\angle {/tex}AOC = {tex}\angle {/tex}BOD (Vertically Opposite Angles)
{tex}\angle {/tex}ACO = {tex}\angle {/tex}BDO (Given)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle {/tex}OAc ≅ {tex}\triangle {/tex}OBd (AAS Axiom)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} OAC = OBD (C.P.C.T.)

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB = BC and {tex}\angle {/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle {/tex}CBD, then another angle which measures {tex}30^\circ {/tex} is
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABD and CBD
AB = BC and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}CBD (Given)
BD (Common)
Therefore In triangle ABD and CBD are congruent by SAS criteria.
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}BDA = 30o (by CPCT)

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AC = BD. If {tex}\angle {/tex}CAB = {tex}\angle {/tex}DBA, then {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB is equal to
questions.explanation:
In Triangle CAB and traingle DBA,
AC = BD and {tex}\angle{/tex}CAB = {tex}\angle{/tex}DBA (Given)
AB (Common)
Therefore, Triangle CAB and traingle DBA are congruent by SAS criteria
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = {tex}\angle{/tex}BDA (by CPCT)

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, ABCD is a quadrilateral in which AD = CB and AB = CD, then {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB is equal to
questions.explanation: As AB = CD, so ,{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD (alternate angles)

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB = AC and {tex}\angle {/tex}A = {tex}70^\circ {/tex}, then {tex}\angle {/tex}C is
questions.explanation:
Since, It is given that AB = AC, then {tex}\angle {/tex}B = {tex}\angle {/tex}C (Isosceles triangle property)
Given {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 70o, Let angle B and C be xo
Sum of all the three angles of triangle = 180o, therefore {tex}\angle {/tex}A + {tex}\angle {/tex}B + {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 180o
70 + x + x = 180
x = 55o
{tex}\angle {/tex}C = 55o

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, PQ = PR. If {tex}\angle {/tex}Q = {tex}70^\circ {/tex}, then measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}P is
questions.explanation:
Since, It is given that PQ=QR, then {tex}\angle {/tex}Q = {tex}\angle {/tex}R (Isosceles trangle property)
As {tex}\angle {/tex}Q = 70o, therefore {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 70o
Sum of all the three angles of triangle = 180o, therefore {tex}\angle {/tex}P + {tex}\angle {/tex}Q + {tex}\angle {/tex}R = 180o
{tex}\angle {/tex}P = 180 - 70 - 70 = 40o

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB = AC. If {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD = {tex}115^\circ {/tex}, then the measure of {tex}\angle {/tex}A is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle {/tex}ACD = 115o, {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB = 180 - 115 = 65o (Linear Pair)
Since, It is given that AB = AC, then {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB (Isosceles trangle property)
As {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB = 65o, therefore {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC = 65o
Sum of all the three angles of triangle = 180°, therefore {tex}\angle {/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB + {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 180o
{tex}\angle {/tex}A = 180 - 65 - 65 = 50o

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, BC = AC. If {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD = {tex}115^\circ {/tex}, the {tex}\angle {/tex}A is
questions.explanation:
As BC = AC, therefore triangle ABC is an isoscelestriangle.
Given {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 115o, {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180 - 115 = 65o (Linear Pair)
As AC = BC, therefore {tex}\angle{/tex}A ={tex}\angle{/tex}B
As sum of all the three angles of atriangle is 180°
Therefore,{tex}\angle{/tex}A +{tex}\angle{/tex}B +{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}A ={tex}\angle{/tex}B = 57.5

questions.question: In the given figure, ABC is an equilateral triangle. The value of x + y is
questions.explanation:
As triangle ABC is an equilateral traingle, therefore all the three angles are equal, that is , 60o each.
x = 180 - 60 = 120o
y = 180 - 60 = 120o
x + y = 120 + 120 = 240

questions.question: In the adjoining Figure, AB = AC and BD = CD. The ratio {tex}\angle {/tex}ABD : {tex}\angle {/tex}ACD is
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
AB = AC
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB(angles opposite to equal sides of a triangle are equal) ......1
in {tex}\triangle{/tex}DBC,
DB = DC,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = {tex}\angle{/tex}DCB(angles opposite to equal sides of a triangle are equal) ......2
subtract 2 from 1
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC - {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB - {tex}\angle{/tex}DCB(equals subtracted from equals gives equal)
= {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD
divide both the sides by {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{\angle ABD}{\angle ACD}{/tex} = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD : {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD = 1 : 1

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, AB{tex} \bot {/tex}BE and FE{tex} \bot {/tex}BE. If AB = FE and BC = DE ,then
questions.explanation:
Given:
AB = FE, BC = ED,
AB {tex}\perp{/tex} BE and FE {tex}\perp{/tex} BE
To Prove: AD = FC
Proof: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD and {tex}\triangle{/tex}FEC,
AB = FE    ...(1) (Given)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}FEC ...(2)
Each = 90o
BC = ED (Given)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC + CD = ED + DC
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BD = EC ...(3)
In view of (1), (2) and (3),
{tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}FEC using SAS congruence rule

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, ABCD is a quadrilateral in which BN and DM are drawn perpendiculars to AC such that BN = DM. If OB = 4 cm. then BD is
questions.explanation:
In Triangle DMO and traingle BNO,
BN = DM and {tex}\angle{/tex}DMO = {tex}\angle{/tex}BNO (90o)
{tex}\angle{/tex}DOM = {tex}\angle{/tex}BON
Therefore, Triangle DMO and traingle BNO are congruent by AAS criteria
Therefore,OB = OD (by CPCT)
So OD = 4 cm, BD = OD+OB = 4+4 = 8cm

questions.question: The length of two sides of a triangle are 7 units and 10 units. Which of the following length can be the length of the third side?
questions.explanation:
As per the rule in a triangle, the sum of any 2 sides should be greater than the third side. So, the length of the third side should be 13, Since with 7, 10 and 13 we have
7 + 10 > 13, 7 + 13 > 10 and 13 + 10 > 7

questions.question: If A, B and C are the lengths of the sides of a triangle, then _________.
questions.explanation:
Put the sides of triangle A, B, C.
For a possible triangle, the following are possible.
a + b > = c
b + c > = a
a + c > = b

questions.question: It is not possible to construct a triangle when the lengths of its sides are
questions.explanation:
Put the sides of triangle a, b, c.
For a possible triangle, the following are should possible.
a + b > = c
b + c > = a
a + c > = b
Here 2.2 + 3.1 = 5.3
So a + b = c
So the triangle becomes a straight line.
So we cannot draw a triangle with sides 5.3 cm, 2.2 cm, 3.1 cm.

questions.question: If triangle PQR is right angled at Q, then
questions.explanation: Then the hypotenuse should be always greater than the remaining two sides.

questions.question: If triangle ABC is obtuse angled and {tex}\angle {/tex}C is obtuse, then
questions.explanation: Side opposite to the largest angle is always greatest and a triangle can have only 1 angle as obtuse.

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, if {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 30o and {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 70o, then which of the following is the longest side?
questions.explanation:
Since the sum of all sides of a triangle is 180°.
So, angle C = 70°, angle B = 30o, angle A = 80°.
We have a theorem which states that the side opposite to the greatest angle is the longest.
So, the side opposite to angle A is the longest.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, PQ > PR. If OQ and OR are bisectors of {tex}\angle {/tex}Q and {tex}\angle {/tex}R respectively, then
questions.explanation: Since PQ > PR then {tex}\angle {/tex}R > {tex}\angle {/tex}Q and hence their bisectors follow the same I.e {tex}\frac{R}{2}{/tex}> {tex}\frac{Q}{2}{/tex} and hence OQ>OR

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, if {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 45o and {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 70o, then the shortest and the longest sides of the triangle are ________.
questions.explanation: Smallest angle is A and greatest angle is B and hence sides opposite to these angles are BC and AC and they are shortest and longest respectively.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, if AC = AD, then
questions.explanation:
Angle D = angle C (As AC = AD)
and angle C > angle B  and   angle D > angle B
hence AB > AD

questions.question: {tex}\angle x{/tex} and {tex}\angle y{/tex} are exterior angles of a triangle ABC at the points B and C respectively, Also, {tex}\angle B{/tex} >{tex}\angle C{/tex}, then the relation between {tex}\angle x{/tex} and {tex}\angle y{/tex} is:
questions.explanation:
Since interior angle B > interior angle C
Hence x < y.

questions.question: If the bisector of the angle A of a {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is perpendicular to the base BC of the triangle then the triangle ABC is:
questions.explanation: Angle bisector is perpendicular to the opposite side only in equilateral triangle.

questions.question: In quadrilateral ABCD, BM and DN are drawn perpendiculars to AC such that BM = DN. If BR = 8 cm. then BD is
questions.explanation:
In triangles {tex}\triangle DNR{/tex} and {tex}\triangle BMR{/tex},
{tex}\angle {/tex}N = {tex}\angle {/tex}M = 90o
{tex}\angle {/tex}NRD = {tex}\angle {/tex}MRB (vertically opposite angles)
BM = DN(Given)
Therefore,  {tex}\triangle DNR{/tex} and {tex}\triangle MRB{/tex} are congruent
Therefore, BR = DR = 8 cm
BD = 16 cm

questions.question: In triangles ABC and PQR three equality relations between some parts are as follows: AB = QP, {tex}\angle B{/tex} = {tex}\angle P{/tex}, BC = PR. State which of the congruence conditions applies:
questions.explanation: Two sides and included angle are equal and is SAS axiom

questions.question: D, E, F are the mid-point of the sides BC, CA and AB respectively of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC. Then {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF is congruent to triangle
questions.explanation: DEF is the median triangle and it divides the given triangle into 4 identical triangles including median triangle

questions.question: For any {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, AB + BC is always:
questions.explanation: Sum of any two sides is greater than third side.

questions.question: Which of the following is not possible in case of triangle ABC?

options.html: AB = 2 cm, BC = 4 cm, CA = 7 cm.
{tex}\angle {/tex}A = 50{tex}^\circ {/tex}, {tex}\angle {/tex}B = 60{tex}^\circ {/tex}, {tex}\angle {/tex}C = 70{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.explanation: Sum of any two sides is greater than third side, but here 2 + 4 < 7.

questions.question: In figure, AB = AC and {tex}\angle ACD{/tex} = 115o. Find {tex}\angle A{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
C = 180o - 115o =65o  AB = AC And hence {tex}\angle{/tex}B ={tex}\angle{/tex}C = 65o  
Then A = 180o -  2 {tex}\times{/tex} 65o = 50o

questions.question: In fig, AC = BC and {tex}\angle ACY{/tex} = {tex}140^\circ {/tex}. Find X and Y:
questions.explanation: The two equal angles are 70 since angle C =180 - 140 = 40  X = Y = 180 - 70 = 110

questions.question: In fig. which of the following statement is true?

options.html: {tex}\angle B{/tex} is the smallest angle in the triangle
{tex}\angle B{/tex} is the greatest angle in the triangle
{tex}\angle A{/tex} is the smallest angle in the triangle.

questions.explanation: In a triangle angle opposite to smallest side is least AC is least side and hence B is smaller

questions.question: In fig., {tex}\triangle ABD \cong \triangle ACD{/tex}, AB = AC, BD = DC name the criteria by which the triangles are congruent:
questions.explanation: Given that two sides are equal and third side is common  I.e AD hence all three corresponding sides are equal

questions.question: In fig, in {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, AB = AC, then the value of x is:
questions.explanation:
Triangle ABC is an iscosceles triangle and hence in the triangle other two angles are 50 and 50
Therefore,
X = 180 - 50 = 130

questions.question: In a triangle ABC, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 35o and {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 60o, then the shortest side is
questions.explanation: Side opposite to smallest angle is shortest side angles of triangle are 35, 60 and 85.

questions.question: In figure, PS = QR and {tex}\angle{/tex}SPQ = {tex}\angle{/tex}RQP.Then
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}QPR and {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQS,
QR = PS  (Given)
{tex}\angle{/tex}RQP = {tex}\angle{/tex}SPQ (Given)
PQ = PQ (Common)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}QPR ≅ {tex}\triangle{/tex}PQS (SAS Axio)
{tex}\therefore{/tex} PR = QS (C.P.C.T.)

questions.question: In the adjoining fig, PQ = PR. If {tex}\angle QPR{/tex} = 48{tex}^\circ {/tex}, then value of x is:
questions.explanation:
It is an iscosceles triangle and hence angles opposite to equal sides are equal
Angle PQR and PRQ will be equal. Let suppose Angle PQR be Y
I.e Y+Y+48=180
= Y = 66
X = 180 - 66 = 114

questions.question: In the adjoining fig. AB = AC. If {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 50{tex}^\circ {/tex}, then the value of x and y are:
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABC,  AB = AC ,hence their opposite angles will be equal.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle B{/tex} = {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 50o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 50o
Now, by angle sum property,
{tex}\angle A{/tex} + {tex}\angle B{/tex} + {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 180o
or, x +  50o +  50o = 180o
or,  x + 100o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 80o

questions.question: The length of each side of a rhombus is 10cm and one of its diagonal is of length 16cm. The Length of the other Diagonal is:
questions.explanation:
Use pythagoras theorem in right triangle,

102 -[16/2]2 = 100 -64 = 36 = [6]2 ; hence the other diagonal = 6x2 = 12cm

questions.question: If ABCD is a Parallelogram with 2 Adjacent angles {tex}\angle A = \angle B{/tex}, then the parallelogram is a
questions.explanation: The sum of the adjacent angles of a parallelogram is 1800.  Opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal. Hence it is a rectangle.

questions.question: If an angle of a parallelogram is two-third of its adjacent angle, the smallest angle of the parallelogram is:
questions.explanation:
Let x be one angle. Then {tex}x+\frac {2}{3}x{/tex} =180 {sum of the adjacent angles of a parallelogram is 180o}
x = 108, adjacent angle= 180 - 108 = 72o

questions.question: If area of a Parallelogram with sides ‘a’ and ‘b’ is A and that of a rectangle with sides ‘a’ and ‘b’ is B, then
questions.explanation:
Area of Parallelogram = Base * Height

if 'a' is the side and 'b' is the base the height will be less than 'a' using Pythagorus theorem, a as Hypotenuse, h as height , A< B

questions.question: The Parallel sides of a trapezium are ‘a’ and ‘b’ resp. The line joining the mid-points of its non-parallel sides will be
questions.explanation: Join one of the diagonals which intersects the line joining mid points of the non parallel sides. This point of intersection become midpoint of the diagonal by using converse of midpoint theorem and we know that by mid point theorem that the line joining midpoints of any two sides of triangle measures half of the third side.so, by combining the results of two triangles we get above result.

questions.question: Two parallelograms stand on same base and between the same parallels. The ratio of their areas is
questions.explanation: Parallelograms on the same base and between the same parallels are equal in area. Hence the ration of two parallelograms will be 1:1.

questions.question:
Three Statements are given below:
(I) In a, Parallelogram the angle bisectors of 2 adjacent angles enclose a right angle.
(II) The angle bisector of a Parallelogram form a Rectangle.
(III) The Triangle formed by joining the mid-points of the sides of an isosceles triangle is not necessarily an isosceles triangle.
Which of the statement/statements is/areTrue?
questions.explanation:
I. The adjacent angles of a parallelogram are supplementary. Their halves add up to 90o . so the angle bisectors enclose a right angle.

II. All the adjacent angle bisectors enclose right angles.Sowehave a rectangle being enclosed by the angle bisectors of a parallelogram.

III. The triangle formed by joining the mid-points of the sides of an isosceles triangle is always an isosceles triangle, because halves of equal sides are also equal

questions.question:
Three statements are given below:

  1. In a Rectangle ABCD, the diagonals AC bisects {tex}\angle A{/tex} as well as {tex}\angle C{/tex}.
  2. In a Square ABCD, the diagonals AC bisects {tex}\angle A{/tex} as well as {tex}\angle C{/tex}.
  3. In rhombus ABCD, the diagonals AC bisects {tex}\angle A{/tex} as well as {tex}\angle C{/tex}.

Which is True?
questions.explanation: In square and rhombus, all sides are equal. By joining  the  points A and C diagonal AC formed. we get two traingles ABC and ADC which are congruent ( SAS conguence). Also, opposite sides are parallel. So, by using altenate angle property we can prove that angle BAC= angleDCA and angle DAC= angle ACB. But by CPCT angleDAC= angleBAC. So, all four angles made by diagonal AC with end points A and C  are equal which proves that diagonal AC bisects angle A and C both.

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, P, Q and R are the mid-points of the sides BC, CA and AB respectively. If AC = 21 cm, BC = 29 cm and AB = 30 cm, find the perimeter of the quadrilateral ARPQ?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} ln {tex}\triangle A B C{/tex}, R and P are the mid-points of AB and BC
{tex}\therefore R P \| A C,{/tex} {tex}R P=\frac{1}{2} A C{/tex} [By mid-point theorem]
In a quadrilateral RPQA,
{tex}\Rightarrow R P \| A Q{/tex}, RP = AQ
{tex}\therefore{/tex} RPQA is a parallelogram
{tex}\Rightarrow A R=\frac{1}{2} A B{/tex}
{tex}\therefore A R=\frac{1}{2} \times 30=15 \mathrm{cm}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AR = PQ = 15cm [Since, opposite sides are equal]
{tex}\Rightarrow R P=\frac{1}{2} A C=\frac{1}{2} \times 21=10.5 \mathrm{cm}{/tex} [Since, opposite sides are equal]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Perimeter of ARPQ = AR + QP + RP + AQ
= 15 + 15 + 10.5 + 10.5
= 51cm
{tex}\therefore{/tex} Perimeter of ARPQ is 51 cm

questions.question: In a Quadrilateral ABCD, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 90o and AB = BC = CD = DA, Then ABCD is a
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral with pair of opposite sides equal and having one right angle is called Rectangle and a rectangle with all sides equal is called Square. So, ABCD is a Square.

questions.question: In a Quadrilateral ABCD, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD, then ABCD is a
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral with only one pair of opposite sides parallel is called Trapezium.

questions.question: ABCD is a Rhombus such that {tex}\angle ACB{/tex}= 40{tex}^\circ {/tex}, then {tex}\angle ADB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
In Rhombus, digonals bisect each other right angle. By using angle sum property in any of the four triangles formed by intersection of diagonals, we get {tex}\angle{/tex}CBD = 50 and {tex}\angle{/tex}CBD = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC ( alternate angles).
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC = 50

questions.question: The Quadrilateral formed by joining the mid-points of the sides of a Quadrilateral PQRS, taken in order, is a rectangle if

options.html: Diagonals of PQRS are at right angles.

questions.explanation: Diagonals of PQRS are at right angles form all the internal angles as right angles. [according to angle property of rectangle, i.e, all the angles of a rectangle are right angle(90{tex}^0{/tex})]

questions.question: The Quadrilateral forms by joining the mid-points of the sides of a Quadrilateral PQRS, taken in order, is a Rhombus if
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral formed by joining the mid points of the sides of the Rectangle is a rhombus. In rectangle , diagonals are equal.

questions.question: Given 4 points A, B, C, D such that 3 Points A, B, C are collinear. By joining these points in order we get
questions.explanation: If three or more points lie on same line then they are called collinear points. A, B and C are collinear means they form a line. Point D is outside the line. Point D can be join with the end points of the line formed by points A, B, and C. For this we need total three lines to form a closed figure. A closed figure with three sides is called triangle.

tests.name: Quadrilaterals Test 02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Quadrilaterals CBSE Online Test 02. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In a Quadrilateral ABCD, AB = BC and CD = DA, then the quadrilateral is a
questions.explanation: A quadrilateral having equal adjancent sides is called Kite. AB and BC are adjancent sides and AD and DC are also adjancent sides which are equal.

questions.question: Given a triangular prism, then what can we conclude about the lateral faces?
questions.explanation: There are five faces in triangular prism. two triangles and three quadrilaterals in which both pair of opposite sides equal. A quadrilateral with both pair of opposite sides equal is called parallelogram. So, lateral faces are parallelogram.

questions.question: Consecutive angles of a Parallelogram are
questions.explanation: In parallelogram, both pair of opposite sides are parallel. So, consecutive angles form a pair of co-interior angles. Cointerior angles add upto 180. It means they are supplementary.

questions.question: A diagonal of a Rectangle is inclined to one side of the rectangle at an angle of {tex}25^\circ {/tex}. The Acute Angle between the diagonals is :
questions.explanation: Two diagonals of a rectangle divides it into four triangles. Out of these four triangles a pair of opposite triangles are congruent by SSS in which a pair of triangles have two equal angles of 25 each and in another pair of opposite triangles have two equal angles of 65 each. By angle sum property we have two options of angle fromed between diagonals. Either it is of 130 or 50. 50 is an acute angle. So, it is a correct option.

questions.question: If a diagonal AC and BD of a quadrilateral ABCD bisect each other, then ABCD is a
questions.explanation: Two diagonals of quadrilateral form four triangles. Out of these four triangles two triangles of opposite to each other are congruent by SAS. By using CPCT property we can prove that both pair of opposite sides in a quadrilateral are parallel. A quadrilateral with both pair of opposite sides parallel is called parallelogram.

questions.question: In quadrilateral ABCD, if {tex}\angle {/tex}A = 60o and {tex}\angle {/tex}B : {tex}\angle {/tex}C : {tex}\angle {/tex}D = 2 : 3 : 7, then {tex}\angle {/tex}D is :
questions.explanation:
In quadrilateral, the sum of the all four angles equal to 360o. let angle B = 2x, angle C = 3x and angle D = 7x.
angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D = 360o
60 + 2x + 3x + 7x = 360
12x = 300o
x = 25o
So, angle D = 7x = 7(25o) = 175o

questions.question: If APB and CQD are 2 parallel lines, then the bisectors of the angles APQ, BPQ, CQP and PQD form, square only if
questions.explanation: The diagonals of a square bisect its angles. Opposite sides of a square are both parallel and equal in length. All four angles of a square are equal.

questions.question: The figure formed by joining the mid-points of the sides of a quadrilateral ABCD, taken in order, is a square only if

options.html: Diagonals of ABCD are equal and perpendicular

questions.explanation: A quadrilateral formed by joining the mid points of a square is a square. So, ABCD is a square. In Square, diagonals are equal and perpendicular.

questions.question: D and E are the mid-points of the sides AB and AC of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC and O is any point on the side BC, O is joined to A. If P and Q are the mid-points of OB and OC res, Then DEQP is
questions.explanation:
By mid point theorem, DE is parallel to BC. In triangle BOA,  DP parallel to OA and OA is parallel to QE in triangle AOC( mid point theorem) because D and P are mid points in triangle BOA and E and Q are mid points in triangle AOC.

So, DP is parallel to EQ. In quadrilateral DPQE, both pair of opposite sides are parallel. So, it become parallelogram.

questions.question: The bisectors of the angles of a Parallelogram enclose a
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a parallelogram. angle A + angle D = 180 (co-interior angles)
{tex}\frac {1}{2}{/tex} of (angle A + angle D) = 90.
Triangle formed by bisectors of angle A and angle D, have sum of two angles equals to 90 therefore, remaining angle is of 90. similarly, we can prove that other angles formed are of 90 each by bisectors of angles of ABCD. The quadrilateral formed by angle bisectors of ABCD has all angles equal to 90 (Vertically opposite angles). A quadrilateral with all right angles is a Rectangle.

questions.question: Rhombus is a quadrilateral

options.html: in which diagonals bisect opposite angles
in which diagonals are inclines at an angle of 120.
in which diagonals are inclines at an angle of 60{tex}^\circ {/tex}

questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a rhombus.
Join BD which forms two triangles ABD and DCB. In triangle ABD, AB = AD.
So, angle ADB = angle ABD ( angles opposite to equal sides are equal) ...(i)
But, angle ABD = angle BDC and angle ADB = angle CBD ( alternate angles) ....(ii)
So, from (i) and (ii)
angle ADB = angle ABD = angle BDC = angle CBD
therefore, diagonal BD bisects angle B and angle D.

questions.question: In Quadrilateral ABCD, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 110o, {tex}\angle B{/tex} = 75o and {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 35o. Find {tex}\angle D{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D = 360o (angle sum property of quadrilateral)
110 + 75o + 35o + angle D = 360o
angle D = 360o - 220o = 140o

questions.question: In Quadrilateral ABCD, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = (3x)o, {tex}\angle B{/tex} = (5x)o, {tex}\angle C{/tex} = (20x)o, {tex}\angle D{/tex} = (8x)o. Find the value of x?
questions.explanation:
angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D = 360 (angle sum property)
3x + 5x + 20x + 8x = 360
36x = 360
x = 10

questions.question: In Quadrilateral {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 38{tex}^\circ {/tex}, {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 3{tex}\angle A{/tex}, {tex}\angle D{/tex} = 4{tex}\angle A{/tex}. Find the value of {tex}\angle B{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D = 360o (angle sum property)
angle C = 3 ( 38) = 114o
angle D = 4 ( 38) = 152o
So, 38o + angle B + 114o + 152o = 360o
angle B = 360o - 304o = 56o

questions.question: In a Quadrilateral ABCD, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = {tex}\angle C{/tex}, {tex}\angle B{/tex} = 2{tex}\angle A{/tex}, {tex}\angle D{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\angle A{/tex}, Then {tex}\angle A{/tex}, {tex}\angle B{/tex}, {tex}\angle C{/tex} and {tex}\angle D{/tex} respectively are:
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex} A + {tex}\angle{/tex} B + {tex}\angle{/tex} C + {tex}\angle{/tex} D = 360o (angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex} A + 2 {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex} A + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of {tex}\angle{/tex} A = 360o 
{tex}\frac{9}{2}{/tex} of {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 360o  
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = 80o
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 2 (80o) = 160o, {tex}\angle{/tex} C ={tex}{80^ \circ }{/tex} and {tex}\angle{/tex} D = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of 80o = 40o

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: ABCD is a Parallelogram in which AB = 9.5 cm and its perimeter is 30 cm. Find the length of each side of the Parallelogram?
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of ABCD = AB + BC + CD + DA = 30
In a parallelogram, opposite sides are equal.
AB = CD = 9.5 and BC = DA = x
So, 9.5 + x + 9.5 + x = 30
2x = 30 - 19
x = 5.5
AB = 9.5 = CD and BC = DA = 5.5

questions.question: ABCD is a Parallelogram in which {tex}\angle BAO = 35^\circ {/tex}, {tex}\angle DAO = 40^\circ {/tex} and {tex}\angle COD = 105^\circ {/tex}. Find {tex}\angle ABO{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
Given, ABCD is a parallelogram having {tex}\angle{/tex}BAO = 35°, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAO = 40° and {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = 105°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}COD = {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB =  105° [vertically opposite angles]
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB, by angle sum property of triangle, 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO =  180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 105° + 35° + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO =  180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO =  40°

questions.question: In a rhombus ABCD, if {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 40o, then {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB =
questions.explanation:
Given ABCD is a rhombus. Diagonals bisect each other perpendicularly.
Hence {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 90°
Given {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB = 40°
AD||BC and BD is the transversal
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC (Alternate angles)
Hence in right angled {tex}\triangle{/tex}BOC,
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 90° + 40° + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}130° + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 180° - 130° = 50°
But {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 50°

questions.question: Diagonals of a Parallelogram ABCD intersect at O. If {tex}\angle BOC = 90^\circ {/tex}, {tex}\angle BDC = 50^\circ {/tex} then {tex}\angle OAB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle {/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle {/tex}COD = 180o (linear pair)
{tex}\angle {/tex}COD = 180o - 90o = 90o
In triangle DOC, {tex}\angle {/tex}DOC + {tex}\angle {/tex}DCO + {tex}\angle {/tex}ODC = 180o (angle sum property)
90o + {tex}\angle {/tex}DCO + 50 = 90o
{tex}\angle {/tex}DCO = 180o - 140o = 40
{tex}\angle {/tex}DCO = {tex}\angle {/tex}OAB = 40 (alternate angles)

questions.question: Which of the following is not true for the Parallelogram?

options.html: Opposite angles are bisected by the diagonals

questions.explanation: If opposite angles are bisected by diagonals in parallelogram, all four bisected angles become equal which leads to equal adjacent side. That is not true in case of parallelogram.

questions.question: The figure forms by joining the mid-points of the sides of a Rhombus, taken in order are:
questions.explanation:
In rhombus ,diagonals bisect each other at right angles. Which proves that all the angles of quadrilateral formed by joining the mid points of rhombus is equal to 90. A quadrilateral with both pair of opposite angles equal is a parallelogram

A parallelogram having one right angle is a rectangle.

questions.question: D and E are the mid-points of the sides AB and AC res. Of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}. DE is produced to F. To prove that CF is equal and parallel to DA, we need an additional information which is:
questions.explanation:
If DE = EF then triangle AED becomes congruent to triangle CEF by SSS congruence rule.
By CPCT, {tex}\angle{/tex}ECF = {tex}\angle{/tex}EAD which forms a pair of alternate angles.
Which proves that AD is parallel to CF

questions.question: If bisectors of {tex}\angle A{/tex} and {tex}\angle B{/tex} of a quadrilateral ABCD intersect each other at P, of {tex}\angle B{/tex} and {tex}\angle C{/tex} at Q, of {tex}\angle C{/tex} and {tex}\angle D{/tex} at R and of {tex}\angle D{/tex} and {tex}\angle A{/tex} at S, then PQRS is a

options.html: Quadrilateral whose opposite angles are supplementary

questions.explanation:
Let the half of the angles A ,B, C and D are denoted by a,b,c and d respectively.

a + b + angle APB = 180 and c + d + angle DRC = 180 ( angle sum property)

angle APB = angle QPS and angle DRC = angle QRS ( vertically opposite angles)

So, angle QPS + angle QRS = (180 - a - b) + ( 180 - c - d)

= 360 - ( a + b + c + d)

 = 360 - 1/2 of ( angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D)

 = 360 - 1/2 0f 360 = 180

therefore, angle QPS and angle QRS are supplementary.

questions.question: The Diagonals AC and BD of a Parallelogram ABCD intersect each other at point O. If {tex}\angle DAC = 32^\circ {/tex} and {tex}\angle AOB = 70^\circ {/tex}, then {tex}\angle DBC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 32o ( alternate angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}COB = 180o ( linear pair)
{tex}\angle{/tex}COB = 180 - 70o = 110o
In triangle BOC,
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBO = 180o ( angle sum property)
110o + 32 o+ {tex}\angle{/tex}CBO = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}CBO = 180o - 142o = 38o

questions.question: Given Rectangle ABCD and P, Q, R and S are the mid-points of the sides AB, BC, CD and DA respectively. If length of a diagonal of Rectangle is 8 cm, then the quadrilateral PQRS is a
questions.explanation:
A quadrilateral formed by joining midpoints of the sides of the rectangle is a Rhombus.
In Rhombus, all sides are equal.
In triangle ABC, P and Q are midpoints of the sides AB and BC respectively. By midpoint theorem, PQ is parallel to AC and PQ is half of the AC.
Let diagonal AC = 8 cm. So, PQ = 4 cm
Therefore, PQRS is a rhombus which all sides equal to 4 cm.

questions.question: Opposite angles of a Quadrilateral ABCD are equal. If AB = 4cm, find the length of CD.
questions.explanation:
A quadrilateral with both pair of opposite angles equal is a parallelogram.
In a parallelogram, opposite sides are equal.
So, AB = CD = 4 cm

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, EF is the line segment joining the mid-points of the sides AB and AC. BC = 7.2 cm, Find EF.
questions.explanation:
E and F are midpoints of sides AB and AC. By midpoint theorem, EF is parallel to BC and EF is {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of BC.
So, EF = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} of (7.2) = 3.6 cm

questions.question: E Divides AB in the ratio 1 : 3 and also, F divides AC in the ratio 1 : 3. EF = 2.8 cm, Find BC
questions.explanation:
Let AE = x and EB = 3x, AF = y and FC = 3y.
EF = 2.8 cm
AE + AF = 2.8 implies x + y = 2.8
BC = CF + FA + AE + EB
= 3y + y + x + 3x
= 4 (x + y) = 4 (2.8) = 11.2 cm

questions.question: Length of the line segment joining the mid-points of two sides of a triangle is ________ the third side of the Triangle.
questions.explanation: According to mid point theorem, A line segment joining mid points of any two sides of a triangle is parallel to third side and length of that line segment is half of the third side.

questions.question: ABCD is a Rectangle, diagonals AC and BD intersect each other at P. If {tex}\angle{/tex}APD = 52o, find {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB and {tex}\angle {/tex}DBA.
questions.explanation:
In Rectangle, diagonals are equal and bisect each other.
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}APD, AP = PD
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADP = {tex}\angle{/tex}PAD = x (angle opposite to equal sides are equal)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}APD, {tex}\angle{/tex} APD + {tex}\angle{/tex} PDA +{tex}\angle{/tex} DAP = 180o (angle sum property)
52o + x + x = 180{tex}^0{/tex} 
2x = 180o - 52o = 128o 
x = 64o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = 64o (alternate angles)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ADB,  {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB + {tex}\angle{/tex}DBA + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 180o (angle sum property)
64o + {tex}\angle{/tex}DBA + 90o = 180o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}DBA = 180o - 154o = 26o
