questions.question: The angle between two altitudes of a Parallelogram through the vertex of an obtuse angle of the Parallelogram of 60o. Find the angles of the Parallelogram
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a parallelogram and AP and CQ are the altitudes drawn from vertex A on sides DC and BC.
In quadrilateral APCQ, sum of the all angles = 360o 
So, 60o + 90o + {tex}\angle{/tex}C + 90o = 360o
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 360o - 240o = 120o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o (co-interior angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o - 120o = 60o 
In parallelogram, opposite angles are equal.
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 120o and {tex}\angle{/tex}B = {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 60o

questions.question: ABCD is a Trapezium in which AB{tex}\parallel {/tex} DC and {tex}\angle A = \angle B{/tex} = 45o. Find angles C and D of the Trapezium
questions.explanation:
AB is parallel to DC. 
angle A + angle D = 180o (co-interior angle)
angle D = 180o - 45o = 135o
Similarly by following same argument, angle C = 135o

questions.question: One Angle of a quadrilateral is of 108o and the remaining three angles are equal. Find each of the three equal angles.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a quadrilateral with angle A = 108o and angle B = angle C = angle D = x
Angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D = 360o (angle sum property)
108o + x + x + x = 360o
3x = 360o - 108o
3x = 252o
x = 84o

questions.question: Angles of a quadrilateral are in the ratio 3 : 4 : 4 : 7. Find all the angles of the quadrilateral.
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a quadrilateral with angle A = 3x, angle B = 4x , angle C = 4x and angle D = 7x
angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D = 360o (angle sum property)
3x + 4x + 4x + 7x = 360o
18x = 360o
x = 20o
angle A = 3 (20o) = 60o
angle B = angle C = 4 (20o) = 80o
angle D = 7 (20o) = 140o

questions.question: The angles of the quadrilateral are in the ratios 3 : 5 : 9 : 13. Find all the angles of the Quadrilateral
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD be a quadrilateral with angle A = 3x, angle B = 5x, angle C = 9x and angle D = 13x
Angle A + angle B + angle C + angle D = 360o ( angle sum property)
3x + 5x + 9x + 13x = 360o
30x = 360o
x = 12o
angle A = 3 (12o) = 36o
angle B = 5 (12o) = 60o
angle C = 9 (12o) = 108o
angle D = 13 (12o) = 156o

questions.question: P, Q, R are the mid- points of AB, BC, AC res, If AB = 10cm, BC = 8cm, AC = 12cm, Find the perimeter of {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR.
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABC, P, Q and R are the midpoints.
By midpoint theorem, PQ is parallel to BC and PQ = 1/2 of BC
QR is parallel to AB and QR = 1/2 of AB
PR is parallel to AC and PR = 1/2 of AC.
So, perimeter of triangle PQR = PQ + QR + PR = 1/2 of ( AB + BC + AC ) = 1/2 of ( 10 + 8 + 12 ) =1/2 of 30 = 15

questions.question: The Diagonals AC and BD of a Parallelogram ABCD intersect each other at the point O such that {tex}\angle DAC = 30^\circ {/tex} and {tex}\angle AOB = 70^\circ {/tex}. Then, {tex}\angle DBC{/tex}?
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle {/tex}DAC = {tex}\angle {/tex}ACB = 30o (alternate angles)
{tex}\angle {/tex}BOA + {tex}\angle {/tex}BOC = 180o (linear pair)
{tex}\angle {/tex}BOC = 180o - 70o = 110o
In triangle BOC, angle BOC + angle OCB + angle CBO = 180 (angle sum property)
110o + 30o + {tex}\angle {/tex}CBO = 180o
{tex}\angle {/tex}CBO = 180o - 140o = 40o = {tex}\angle {/tex}DBC

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a Rhombus. Then,
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a rhombus.
AB = BC = CD = DA
In Rhombus, diagonals bisect each other at right angles.
So, AO = CO and BO = DO
In triangle AOB , AO2 + BO2 = AB2 ( Pythagoras theorem)
(1/2 AC)2 + ( 1/2 BD)2 = AB2
AC2/4 + BD2/4 =  AB2
AC2 + BD2 = 4 AB2

questions.question: In a Trapezium ABCD, if AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD, then (AC2 + BD2) =?
questions.explanation:
Given: ABCD is a trapezium with AB {tex}\parallel{/tex} CD
Construction: Draw DE and CF {tex}\perp{/tex} to AB

Then in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAC is acute
{tex}\therefore{/tex} BC2 = AC2 + AB2 - 2 AF : AB ...(1)
and In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BDA
∠DBA is acute
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AD2 = BD2 + AB2 - 2 BE : AB ...(2)
Adding (1) and (2) we get
BC2 + AD2 = AC2 + BD2 + 2AB2 - 2AF·AB - 2BE·AB
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AC2 + BD2 = BC2 + AD2 - 2 AB [AB - AF - BE]
= BC2 + AD2 - 2AB [AB - (AE + EF) - (BF + EF)]
= BC2 + AD2 - 2AB [AB - (AE + EF + BF + EF)]
= BC2 + AD2 - 2AB [AB - (AB + CD)] ({tex}\because{/tex}EF = DC)
= BC2 + AD2 - 2AB [- CD)]
= AD2 + BC2 + 2AB {tex}\times{/tex} CD

questions.question: In given figure, ABCD is a parallelogram, M is the mid-point of BD and BM bisects {tex}\angle{/tex}B. Then, {tex}\angle{/tex}AMB =
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a parallelogram. BD is the diagonal and M is the mid point of BD. BD is a bisector of ∠B.
We know that, diagonals of the parallelogram bisect each other.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} M is the mid point of AC.
AB {tex}\parallel{/tex} CD and BD is the transversal,
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC ...(1) (Alternate interior angles)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC ...(2) (Given)
From (1) and (2), we get
{tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC
In Δ BCD,
{tex}\angle{/tex}BDC = {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC = CD ...(3) (In a triangle, equal angles have equal sides opposite to them)
AB = CD and BC = AD ...(4) (Opposite sides of the parallelogram are equal)
From (3) and (4), we get
AB = BC = CD = DA
{tex}\therefore{/tex} ABCD is a rhombus.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AMB = 90° (Diagonals of rhombus are perpendicular to each other)

questions.question: What is the length of PQ in a trapezium ABCD in which AB||DC and P and Q are mid-points on AD and BC respectively?
questions.explanation:
Join PD and Produce it to meet BA at G.

In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PCD and {tex}\triangle{/tex}APG,
{tex}\angle{/tex}DPC = {tex}\angle{/tex}GPA,
{tex}\angle{/tex}PDC = {tex}\angle{/tex}AGP
{tex}\therefore \triangle PCD \cong \triangle APG{/tex}
CD = AG and PD = PG
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BGD,
P is the mid - point of GD
Q is the mid -point of BD
Therefore, By mid - point theorem, PQ {tex}||{/tex} AB
and PQ = {tex}\frac 12{/tex}(GB)
but GB = GA + AB = CD + AB
{tex}\therefore P Q = \left( \frac { 1 } { 2 } \right) ( A B + C D ){/tex}

questions.question: In given figure, ABCD is a parallelogram and E is the mid-point of BC. DE and AB when produced meet at F. Then, AF =
questions.explanation:
By the congruency of triangles,
BEF and CED (AAS Rule) are congruent
So, DC = BF (CPCT) ...(1)
But DC = AB ...(2)
So AB = BF
but AF = AB + BF
So AF = 2AB

questions.question: In a parallelogram ABCD, if {tex}\angle{/tex}DAB = 75o and {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 60o, then {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC =
questions.explanation:
We know that the opposite angles of a parallelogram are equal. 
Therefore, {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = 75°....(i) 
(i) Now, in ∆ BCD, we have
{tex}\angle{/tex}CDB + {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCD = 180° [Since, sum of the angles of a triangle is 180°] 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CDB + 60° + 75° = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CDB + 135° = 180° 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CDB = (180° - 135°) = 45°.

questions.question: In Fig. the quadrilateral ABCD circumscribes a circle with centre O. If {tex}\angle AOB = 115^\circ {/tex}, then find {tex}\angle COD{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
{tex}{/tex}{tex}\because \angle AOB = \angle COD{/tex} (vertically opposite angle)
{tex}\therefore \angle COD = 115^\circ {/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a Rhombus. Find the value of x and y?
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a rhombus and a rhombus is also a parallelogram. A rhombus has four equal sides.
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = x
In {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC
x + x + 110{tex}^0{/tex} = 180{tex}^0{/tex} ..(angle sum property of triangle)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 180{tex}^0{/tex}-110{tex}^0{/tex} = 70{tex}^0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 35{tex}^0{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180°(Adjacent angles are supplementary)
But, {tex}\angle{/tex}C = x + y = 70°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 70° – x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 70° – 35° = 35°
Hence, x = 35°and y = 35°

tests.name: Quadrilaterals Test 05
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Quadrilaterals CBSE Online Test 05. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a Rhombus. Find the value of x and y ?
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a rhombus and a rhombus is also a parallelogram. A rhombus has four equal sides.
The diagonals of a rhombus are perpendicular bisector of each other.
So, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB, {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = 40°, {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 90 °and {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO = 180°- (40° + 90°) = 50 °
∴ x = 50°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD, AB = AD
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD = {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 50°
Hence, x = 50° and y = 50°

questions.question: In the figure, ABCD is a Rectangle. Find the values of x and y?
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a rectangle
The diagonals of a rectangle are congruent and bisect each other. Therefore, in  {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB, we have:
OA = OB
{tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA = 35°
x = 90° – 35° = 55° and {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180° – (35° + 35°) = 110°
y = {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 110° [Vertically opposite angles]
Hence, x = 55° and y = 110°

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a Rectangle. Find the values of x and y?
questions.explanation:
Given {tex}\angle AOB= 110^0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle DOC =110^0{/tex} vertically opposite angles

{tex}\triangle{/tex}DOC we have:
DO = OC 
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}ODC = {tex}\angle{/tex}OCD = y
now in {tex}\triangle ODC{/tex} 
y + y + 110{tex}^0{/tex} = 180{tex}^0{/tex} (angle sum property of triangle)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}2y = 180{tex}^0{/tex} -110{tex}^0{/tex}=70{tex}^0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y= 35{tex}^0{/tex} 
Also, x = 90° – y
x = 90° – 35° = 55°
Hence, x = 55° and y = 35°

questions.question: In Triangle ABC which is right angled at B. Given that AB = 9cm, AC = 15cm and D, E are the mid-points of the sides AB and AC respectively. Find the length of BC?
questions.explanation:
Applying pythagoras theorem in {tex}\triangle \mathrm { ABC }{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm { AC } ^ { 2 } = \mathrm { AB } ^ { 2 } + \mathrm { BC } ^ { 2 }{/tex} 
{tex}15 ^ { 2 } = 9 ^ { 2 } + \mathrm { BC } ^ { 2 }{/tex} 
{tex}225 = 81 + \mathrm { BC } ^ { 2 }{/tex} 
{tex}225 - 81 = \mathrm { BC } ^ { 2 }{/tex} 
{tex}\mathrm { BC } ^ { 2 } = 144{/tex} 
BC  = 12 cm

questions.question: Triangle ABC is right angled at B. Given that AC = 15cm, AB = 9cm and E and D are the mid-points of sides AC and AB res. Calculate the area of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ADE
questions.explanation:
As D and E are the midpoints of AB and AC.
So, by mid point theorem
DE = BC/2 = 12/2 = 6 cm 
AD = AB/2 = 9/2 = 4.5 cm 
Area of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ADE = 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} DE {tex}\times{/tex} AD
= 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 4.5 = 13.5 cm square

questions.question: M,N and P are the mid-points of AB, AC and BC res. If MN = 3 cm, NP = 3.5 cm and MP = 2.5 cm, calculate BC, AB and AC
questions.explanation:
AB = 7 cm ( by mid-point theorm )
AC = 5 cm ( by mid-point theorm )
BC = 6 cm ( by mid-point theorm )

questions.question: E and F are the mid-points of the sides AB and AC of a {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC. If AB = 6cm, BC = 5cm and AC = 6cm, Then EF is equal to
questions.explanation:
since E and F are the mid points of sides AB and AC respectively.
according to mid point theorem of triangle;
EF = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\times BC{/tex}
EF = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\times 5{/tex}

questions.question: In Parallelogram ABCD, bisectors of angles A and B intersect each other at O. The measure of {tex}\angle AOB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Given: ABCD is a parallelogram in which AO and BO are angle bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}A and {tex}\angle{/tex}B.
Now since ABCD is a parallelogram
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AD || BC
Now AD || BC and transversal AB intersect them
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180° ({tex}\therefore{/tex} sum of consecutive interior angle is 180°)
{tex} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{2}\angle A + \frac{1}{2}\angle B = {90^ \circ }{/tex} 
⇒ {tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 = 90°  ({tex}\therefore{/tex} AO and BO are angle bisectors)  ... (i)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB we have
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 90°  +{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180°  (from (i))
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180° – 90° = 90°

questions.question: D and E are the mid-points of the sides AB and AC. Of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC. If BC = 5.6cm, find DE.
questions.explanation:
By using Mid-Point theorem,
DE = Half of BC
Hence, DE = 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 5.6 = 2.8 cm

questions.question: E and F are the mid-points of sides AB and AC res. Of the {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC ; G and H are the mid-points of the sides AE and AF res. Of the {tex}\triangle {/tex}AEF. If GH = 1.8cm, Find BC
questions.explanation: BC = 4 {tex}\times{/tex}1.8 = 7.2 CM

questions.question: In Fig. ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral. If {tex}\angle BAC = 50^\circ {/tex} and {tex}\angle DBC = 60^\circ {/tex} then find {tex}\angle BCD{/tex}.
questions.explanation:
Here {tex}\angle BDC = \angle BAC = 50^\circ {/tex} (angles in same segment are equal)

In {tex}\Delta BCD,{/tex} we have
{tex} \angle BCD = 180^\circ - (\angle BDC + \angle DBC){/tex}
{tex} = 180^\circ - (50^\circ + 60^\circ ){/tex}
= 70o

questions.question: In Quadrilateral ABCD, {tex}\angle A + \angle C = 140^\circ {/tex}, {tex}\angle A:\angle C = 1:3{/tex} and {tex}\angle B:\angle D = 5:6{/tex}. Find the values of {tex}\angle {/tex}A, {tex}\angle {/tex}B, {tex}\angle {/tex}C and {tex}\angle {/tex}D?
questions.explanation:
given - {tex}\angle {/tex} A+ {tex}\angle {/tex}C=140{tex}^0{/tex}
and {tex}\angle {/tex} A: {tex}\angle {/tex} C = 1:3
and {tex}\angle B:\angle D = 5:6{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}\angle {/tex} A = {tex}\frac {1}{4}\times140^0 = 35^0{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}\angle {/tex} C = {tex}\frac {3}{4}\times140^0 = 105^0{/tex}
now according to angle sum property of quadrilateral
{tex}\angle {/tex} A +{tex}\angle {/tex}B+ {tex}\angle {/tex} C+ {tex}\angle {/tex} D = 360{tex}^0{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}35{tex}^0{/tex}+{tex}\angle {/tex} B+ 105{tex}^0{/tex} +{tex}\angle {/tex} D = 360{tex}^0{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}\angle B+\angle D{/tex} = {tex}360^0 -140^0=220^0{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}5x + 6x =  220{tex}^0{/tex}
{tex}\implies{/tex}x = 20{tex}^0{/tex}
so, {tex}\angle {/tex}  B = {tex}5\times20^0 = 100^0{/tex}
and {tex}\angle D = 6 \times 20^0 = 120^0{/tex}

questions.question: If bisector of {tex}\angle A{/tex} and {tex}\angle B{/tex} of a quadrilateral ABCD intersect each other at p , {tex}\angle B{/tex} and {tex}\angle C{/tex} at Q , {tex}\angle C{/tex} and {tex}\angle D{/tex} at R and, {tex}\angle D{/tex} and {tex}\angle A{/tex} at S then PQRS is a

options.html: Quadrilateral whose opposite angles are supplementary

questions.explanation:
Let's assume our quadrilateral ABCD as a parallelogram :

​we know
{tex}\angle{/tex}DCB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180° ( Co-interior angles of parallelogram are supplementary)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}DCB + {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 90°    ( Both sides divide by 2 )
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 =90°... (1)
In, ∆CQB we know
 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 +{tex}\angle{/tex}CQB = 180° ... (2)
From eq(1) and eq(2), We get
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CQB = 180° − 90°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}CQB = 90°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = 90°     (because {tex}\angle{/tex}CQB = {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR, vertically opposite angles )
Similarly, it can be shown
{tex}\angle{/tex}QPS = {tex}\angle{/tex}PSR ={tex}\angle{/tex}SRQ = 90°
So, quadrilateral PQRS is a rectangle.

questions.question: The diagonals AC and BD of a parallelogram ABCD intersect each other at the point O. If {tex}\angle DAC = 32^\circ {/tex} and {tex}\angle AOB = 70^\circ {/tex} then, {tex}\angle DBC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Let ABCD is the parallelogram, in which AC and BD are diagonals that intersect at O.
We have,{tex}\angle{/tex}DAC = 32°; {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB  = 70°[given]
Since ABCD is a ∥gm, so AB∥CD and AD∥BC.Since AD∥BC, and AC is a transversal, then {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC[alternate interior angles] so, {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 32° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB = 32°
Since, {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB and {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC form a linear pair, then {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC=180°
⇒70°+{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC=180°{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 180° − 70° {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC=110°
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BOC,we have, {tex}\angle{/tex}BOC + {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC =180°[angle sum property]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}110° + 32° + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}142° + {tex}\angle{/tex}OBC=180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 180°−142°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = 38°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 38°

questions.question: In a parallelogram ABCD if angle A = (3x - 20), angle B = (Y + 15), angle C = (x + 40) then find the value of x and y
questions.explanation:
Given, ABCD is a parallelogram.
So,
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\angle{/tex}C (Opposite angles of parallelogram are equal in size)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x − 20 = x + 40
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x − x = 40 + 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 60
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 30°
Thus, {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 − 20 = 90 − 20 = 70°
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180° (Sum of interior angles of parallelogram is 180°)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 70° + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180° − 70°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 110°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y + 15 = 110°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 95°
Hence, x = 30° and y = 95°
