questions.question: The median of a triangle divides it into two
questions.explanation:
The median of a triangle divides it into two triangles of different areas.
If in a triangle ABC, AD is a median, then
Area of triangle ABD = Area of triangle ACD.

questions.question: The area of the figure formed by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rhombus with diagonals 16 cm and 12 cm is
questions.explanation:
Since the figure obtained by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rhombus is rectangle, then
Side of rectange = PQ = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}AC [Mid-point theorem]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} PQ = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 8 cm
Similarly, QR = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 6 cm
Therefore, Area of rectangle PQRS = PQ {tex}\times{/tex} QR = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 48 cm2
The area of the figure formed by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rhombus with diagonals 16 cm and 12 cm is a rectangle with are 48 sq. cm.

questions.question: The figure obtained by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rectangle of sides 8 cm and 6 cm is
questions.explanation:
Since the figure obtained by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rectangle is a rhombus.
Then diagonals of the rhombus PQRS are QS and PR i.e., sides of rectangle ABCD.
Therefore, QS = 8 cm and PR = 6 cm
Now, Area of rhombus PQRS = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} QS {tex}\times{/tex} PR = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 24 cm2
The figure obtained by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rectangle of sides 8 cm and 6 cm is a rhombus with area 24 sq. cm.

questions.question: ABCD is quadrilateral whose diagonal AC divides it into two parts, equal in area, then ABCD

options.html: need not be any of (a), (b) or (c).

questions.explanation:
ABCD is quadrilateral whose diagonal AC divides it into two parts, equal in area, then ABCD can be either a rhombus or a rectangle or a parallelogram.

Therefore, it needs not be any of (a), (b) or (c).

questions.question: Two parallelograms are on equal bases and between the same parallels. The ratio of their areas is
questions.explanation:
Two parallelograms are on equal bases and between the same parallels. The ratio of their areas is 1 : 1.
Because when two parallelogram are on same base and between the same parallels, then the areas of both parallelograms are equal.

questions.question: The mid-point of the sides of a triangle along with any of the vertices as the fourth point make a parallelogram of area equal to
questions.explanation:
We know that, if D, E and F are respectively the mid-points of the sides BC, CA and AB of a {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, then all four triangles has equal area i.e..
ar({tex}\triangle{/tex}AFE) = ar({tex}\triangle{/tex}BFD) = ar({tex}\triangle{/tex}EDC) = ar({tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF) ..... (i)
{tex}\triangle{/tex} area of {tex}\Delta D E F=\frac{1}{4} \text { Area of } \Delta A B C{/tex} ..... (ii)

If we take D as the fourth vertex, then area of the paralleiogram AFDE
= Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}AFE + Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF
= Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF + Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF = 2 Area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}DEF [using Eq. (i)]
{tex}=2 \times \frac{1}{4} \text { Area of } \triangle A B C{/tex} [using eq. (ii)]
{tex}=\frac{1}{2} \text { Area of } \Delta A B C{/tex}

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, D, E, F are the mid-points of sides BC, CA and AB respectively. If ar({tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC) = 16 cm2, then ar (trapezium FBCE) =
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{DEF}}} \right) = {1 \over 4} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} 
Since {tex}\triangle {\rm{DEF}} \cong \triangle {\rm{BDF}} \cong \triangle {\rm{DCE}}{/tex}, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AFE}}} \right) = {1 \over 4} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{Trap}}{\rm{.FBCE}}} \right) {/tex}{tex}= {1 \over 4} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{Trap}}{\rm{.FBCE}}} \right) = {3 \over 4} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{Trap}}{\rm{.FBCE}}} \right) = {3 \over 4} \times 16 = 12{/tex} cm2

questions.question: If a triangle and a parallelogram are on the same base and between the same parallels, then the ratio of the area of the triangle to the area of the parallelogram is
questions.explanation:
If a triangle and a parallelogram are on the same base and between the same parallels, then the ratio of the area of the triangle to the area of the parallelogram is 1 : 2.
Area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Area of parallelogram
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of triangle : Area of parallelogram = 1 : 2

questions.question: Diagonals AC and BD of trapezium ABCD in which AB || DC, intersect each other at O. The triangle which is equal in area to triangle AOD is
questions.explanation:
Since triangles ABC and ABD are on the same base AB and between same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AOB}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}= {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AOB}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AOD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BOC}}} \right){/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, PQRS is a rectangle, If PS = 8 cm and SR = 4 cm, then the area of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is
questions.explanation:
In the given figure, AP{tex}\parallel {/tex}QR{tex}\parallel {/tex}BC

And SP = QR = BC = 8 cm

Also, PQ = AB = 4 cm                          [Since ABQP is a parallelogram]

Since PS is perpendicular to SB, therefore BC is also perpendicular to SB.

Therefore, {tex}\Delta {\rm{ABC}}{/tex} is a right angled triangle.

Hence, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2}{/tex} x AB x AC = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}x 4 x 8 = 16 cm2  area ({tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC) = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex}AB {tex}\times{/tex}AC = 16 cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a square. DEGH is a rectangle. Two equal parallelograms on the base DE are
questions.explanation:
In the Given figure,

If ABCD is a square and DEGH is a rectangle.
Then two equal parallelogram on the base DE are DEGH and DEFC.

questions.question: In the given figure, the area of quadrilateral ABCD is
questions.explanation:
In the given figure,

Area of quad. ABCD = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height = 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 42 cm2

questions.question: In the given figure if {tex}AD\parallel BC{/tex} then the triangle which is equal in area to {tex}\triangle COD{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since, theorem says that if there are two parallel lines in a quadrilateral, then the areas of opposite triangles formed by joining its diagonals are equal.
Therefore, in the given figure, if AD{tex}\parallel {/tex}BC, then the triangle which is equal in area to {tex}\triangle {\rm{COD}}{/tex} is {tex}\triangle {\rm{BOA}}{\rm{.}}{/tex}

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. If AB = 12 cm, AE 7.5 cm, CF = 15 cm, then AD is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here, Area of parallelogram ABCD with base AB = AB {tex}\times{/tex} AE = 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 7.5 = 90 sq. cm
Therefore, Area of parallelogram ABCD with base AD = 90 sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AD {tex}\times{/tex} CF = 90
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AD {tex}\times{/tex} 15 = 90
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AD = 6 cm

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram and E and F are mid-points of AD and BC respectively. P is any point on EF. If area of {tex}\triangle EFC = 8\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar(\triangle AEP + \triangle BFP){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Here, EFBA and EFCD are parallelograms of equal area. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABP}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFBP}}} \right){/tex} 
And {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EFC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFCD}}} \right){/tex} 
Therefore, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EFC}}} \right){/tex} 
And  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ECD}}} \right){/tex} 
Now, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) - \left[ {{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEP}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BFP}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}.....(i)
Since EC is a diagonal of parallelogram EFCD. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EFC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ECD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ECD}}} \right){\rm{ = area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFCD}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EFC}}} \right){/tex}.....(ii)
From eq.(i) and (ii), we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) - \left[ {{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEP}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BFP}}} \right)} \right] = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFCD}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EFC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EFC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEP}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BFP}}} \right) = 8{/tex} cm2

tests.name: Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles Test 02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles CBSE Online Test 02. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: PQRS is a parallelogram and A and B are any points on PQ and QR. If {tex}ar(\parallel ;PQRS) = 48\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar(\triangle PBS) + ar(\triangle ASR){/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Since triangle PBS and parallelogram PQRS are on the same base PS and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBS}}} \right){/tex}....(i)
Similarly, {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ASR}}} \right){/tex}....(ii)
Adding eq.(i) and (ii), we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBS}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ASR}}} \right) = 48{/tex} cm2

questions.question: A, B, C, D are mid-points of sides of parallelogram PQRS. If ar (PQRS) = 36 cm2, then ar (ABCD) =
questions.explanation:
Since area of the figure obtained by joining mid-points of adjacent sides of a parallelogram is half of the area of parallelogram.
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABCD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABCD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times36=18{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a trapezium in which AB || DC. If {tex}ar(\triangle ABD) = 24\;c{m^2}{/tex} and AB = 8 cm, then the height of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
In the given figure,

{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = 24{/tex} Sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} AB {tex}\times{/tex} Height of {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} Height of {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} = 24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  Height of {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} = 6 cm

questions.question: In the given figure if {tex}ar(\parallel ABCD) = 29\;c{m^2}{/tex} and AB = 5.8 cm, then the height of {tex}\parallel \;ABEF{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since parallelograms ABCD and ABEF are on the same base AB and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB {tex}\times{/tex} Height of {tex}\parallel {/tex}gm ABEF = 29
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 5.8 {tex}\times{/tex} Height of {tex}\parallel {/tex}gm ABEF = 29
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height of {tex}\parallel {/tex}gm ABEF = 5 cm

questions.question: {tex}AP\parallel BQ\parallel CR{/tex}. If {tex}ar(\triangle AQC) = 17\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar(\triangle PBR){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since triangles ABQ and PBQ are on the same base BQ and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABQ}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBQ}}} \right){/tex} .....(i)
And {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BCQ}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BQR}}} \right){/tex} ....(ii)
Adding eq.(i) and (ii), we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABQ}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BCQ}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBQ}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BQR}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AQC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBR}}} \right) = 17{/tex} cm2

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a parallelogram. If {tex}ar(\triangle BAP) = 10\;c{m^2}{/tex} and{tex}ar(\triangle CPD) = 30\,c{m^2},{/tex}then {tex}ar(\parallel \;ABCD){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
In the given figure,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABP}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PCD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 10 + 30{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 80{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a square. P and Q are mid-point of AB and DC respectively. If AB = 8 cm, then {tex}ar\;(\triangle BPD){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since P is the mid-point of AB. Therefore,
BP = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}AB = 4 cm
And, height of the triangle BPD = AD = 8 cm
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BPD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 4 \times 8 = 16{/tex} cm2

questions.question: PQRS is a parallelogram. If X and Y are mid-points of PQ and SR and diagonal SQ is joined, then {tex}ar(\parallel \;XQRY):ar(\triangle QSR){/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Since parallelogram PQRS and triangle AQS are on the same base QS and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{ar}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQS}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}= {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{ar}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right){/tex} ..............(i)
Also, if X and Y are mid-points of PQ and SR and diagonal SQ is joined, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ XQRY}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}= {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right){/tex} .............(ii)
From eq.(i) and (ii), we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ XQRY}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ XQRY}}} \right):{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQS}}} \right) = 1:1{/tex}

questions.question: ABCD and ABEF are parallelograms. M is any point of EB. If {tex}ar(\parallel \;ABCD) = 28\;c{m^2},{/tex}then {tex}ar(\triangle FAM){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since parallelogram ABCD and ABEF are on the same base AB and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) = 28{/tex} sq. cm
Again, since triangle FAM and parallelogram ABEF are on the same base AF and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{FAM}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 28 = 14{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. Through A a line AEF is drawn to meet DC produced at F. If {tex}ar(\triangle DCE) = 13\;sq{/tex} units then {tex}ar\;(\triangle BEF){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Construction: Join AC.

Since triangle ABC and ABF are on the same base and between same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABF}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BEF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEC}}} \right){/tex}                    ...............(i)
Also, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{DCE}}} \right){/tex}                   ...............(ii)
From eq.(i) and (ii), we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{DCE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BEF}}} \right) = 13{/tex} cm2

questions.question: PQR is a triangle. S is any point on a line through P parallel to QR. If T is any point on a line through R parallel to SQ, then the three triangles equal in area are
questions.explanation:
Since triangles PQR and SQR are on the same base QR and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQR}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SQR}}} \right){/tex} ................(i)
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SQR}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SRT}}} \right){/tex} .................(ii)
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SRT}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{QRT}}} \right){/tex} ...................(iii)
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{QRS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{QST}}} \right){/tex} .................(iv)
From eq.(i), (ii), (iii) and (iv) we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQR}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SQR}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{QST}}} \right){/tex}

questions.question: ABCD is a trapezium with parallel sides AB = a cm and DC = b cm. E and F are the mid-points of the non-parallel sides. The ratio of ar (ABFE) to ar (EFCD) is
questions.explanation:
Area of trapezium ABCD = {tex}{1 \over 2}\left( {a + b} \right)h{/tex} 
Since {tex}{\rm{EF}}\parallel {\rm{AB,}}{/tex} and EF lies half way between AB and CD.
Therefore, perpendicular distance between AB and EF and between DF and CD are each {tex}{1 \over 2}h.{/tex} 
The length EF lies half way between that of AB and that of CD.
Therefore, EF = {tex}{1 \over 2}\left( {a + b} \right){/tex} 
Therefore, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABFE}}} \right){/tex}{tex} = {1 \over 2}\left[ {a + {1 \over 2}\left( {a + b} \right)} \right] \times {1 \over 2}h {/tex}{tex}= {1 \over 8}\left( {3a + b} \right)h{/tex} 
And {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{EFCD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2}\left[ {{1 \over 2}\left( {a + b} \right) + b} \right] {/tex}{tex}\times {1 \over 2}h = {1 \over 8}\left( {a + 3b} \right)h{/tex} 
Therefore, {tex}{{{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABFE}}} \right)} \over {{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFCD}}} \right)}}{/tex}= {tex}{{{1 \over 2}\left[ {a + {1 \over 2}\left( {a + b} \right)} \right] \times {1 \over 2}h = {1 \over 8}\left( {3a + b} \right)h} \over {{1 \over 2}\left[ {{1 \over 2}\left( {a + b} \right) + b} \right] \times {1 \over 2}h = {1 \over 8}\left( {a + 3b} \right)h}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{{{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABFE}}} \right)} \over {{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFCD}}} \right)}} = {{3a + b} \over {a + 3b}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABFE}}} \right):{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFCD}}} \right){/tex} = (3a + b): (a + 3b)

questions.question: In quadrilateral PQRS, M is the mid-point of PR. If ar (SMQR) is {tex}18\;c{m^2},{/tex} then ar (PQMS) is
questions.explanation:
Since M is mid-point of PR, therefore, QM is the median of triangle PQR.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQM}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{QMR}}} \right){/tex} ..................(i)
SImilarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PSM}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SMR}}} \right){/tex} ..................(ii)
Adding eq.(i) and (ii), we have
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQM}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PSM}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{QMR}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SMR}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{SQRM}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQMS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQMS}}} \right) = 18{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram formed by drawing lines parallel to diagonals of quadrilateral PQRS through its vertices. If {tex}ar\;(quad\;PQRS) = 15\;c{m^2},\;{/tex}then {tex}\;ar\;(\parallel ABCD){/tex}is
questions.explanation:
Since triangle PQR and parallelogram PRBA are on the same base PR and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQR}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PRBA}}} \right){/tex} ....(i)
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRS}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PDCR}}} \right){/tex}....(ii)
Adding eq.(i) and (ii), we have
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQR}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRS}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PRBA}}} \right){1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PDCR}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{PQRS}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 15{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 30{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a rectangle with O as any point in its interior. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle AOD) = 3\;c{m^2}{/tex} and {tex}\;ar\;(\triangle BOC) = 6\;c{m^2},{/tex} then area of rectangle ABCD is
questions.explanation:
According to question,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AOD}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BOC}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}= {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 3 + 6 = 9{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 18{/tex} cm2

tests.name: Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles CBSE Online Test 03. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In the given figure ABCD is a parallelogram and its area is {tex}64\;c{m^2}.{/tex} If P is any point in the interior of {tex}\parallel \;ABCD,{/tex} then {tex}ar(\triangle APD) + ar(\triangle PBC){/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
According to question,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{APD}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 64 = 32{/tex} cm2

questions.question: PQRS is a trapezium with {tex}PQ\;\parallel \;SR.{/tex} A line parallel to PR intersects PQ at X and QR at Y. If {tex}ar(\triangle PYR) = 5\;c{m^2},{/tex}then {tex}ar\;(\triangle PXS){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since triangle PXS and and PXR are on the same base PX and between the same parallels, the
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PXS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PXR}}} \right){/tex}.....(i)
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRY}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PXR}}} \right){/tex}.....(ii)
From eq.(i) and (ii), we have
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PXS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRY}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PXS}}} \right) {/tex} = 5 cm2

questions.question: D and E are mid-points of BC and AD respectively. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle ABC) = 10c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle EBC){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since D is the mid-point of BC. Then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADC}}} \right) = 5{/tex} sq. cm
Also, E is the mid-point of AD, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BED}}} \right) = 2.5{/tex} sq. cm  ....(i)
And, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDC}}} \right) = 2.5{/tex} sq. cm .....(ii)
Adding eq. (i) and (ii), we have
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BED}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDC}}} \right) = 5{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EBC}}} \right) = 5{/tex} cm2

questions.question: Points A, B, C, and D are collinear. AB = BC = CD. {tex}XY\parallel AD.{/tex} If P and M lie on XY and {tex}ar(\triangle MCD) = 7\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle APB)\;{/tex}and {tex}\;ar\;(\triangle APD){/tex} respectively are
questions.explanation:
Since triangles MCD and PAB are on the same base and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{MCD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PAB}}} \right) = 7{/tex} sq. cm
Now, since AB = BC = CD, then
AB = {tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex}AD and BC = {tex}{2 \over 3}{/tex}AD
Then, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{APB}}} \right) = {1 \over 3} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{APD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 3} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{APD}}} \right) = 7{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{APD}}} \right) = 21{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: If E and F are mid-points of sides AB and CD respectively and {tex}ar(\parallel \;ABCD) = 36\;c{m^2},\;{/tex}then {tex}ar\;(APD)\;:\;ar\;(DEF){/tex}
questions.explanation:
Since E and F are mid-points of CD and AB respectively. Then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ AEFD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 36 = 18{/tex} sq. cm
Now, in parallelogram AEFD, DE is diagonal.
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{DEF}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ AEFD}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 18 = 9{/tex} sq. cm
Since triangle ADP and parallelogram ABCD are on the same base and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADP}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 18{/tex} sq. cm
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADP}}} \right){\rm{ : area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} = 18 : 2 = 2 : 1

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}BC\parallel AE,\;CD\parallel BE,{/tex} and {tex}\;ar\;(\triangle BED) = 6\;c{m^2},{/tex} than {tex}\;ar\;(\triangle ABC){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since triangles BDE and and BEC are on the same base BE and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BDE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BEC}}} \right) = 6{/tex} sq. cm
Since triangles BEC and and ABC are on the same base BE and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BEC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = 6{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: In the given figure If {tex}ar\;(\parallel \;ABEF)\; = \;ar(\parallel \;ABCD) = 50\;c{m^2},{/tex} AFGH is a parallelogram and points E, B, G and H are collinear points, then {tex}ar\;(\parallel \;AFGH){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since parallelograms ABEF and ABCD are on the same base and between two parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 50{/tex} sq. cm
Since parallelograms ABEF and AFGH are on the same base and between two parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ AFGH}}} \right) = 50{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: PQRS is a parallelogram. A and B are any points on PQ and RQ respectively. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle SBR) = 16\;c{m^2}\;and\;ar\;(\triangle PBQ) = 8\;c{m^2},{/tex} then the area of {tex}\triangle RAS{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since triangle BPS and parallelogram PQRS are on the same base SP and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{PBS}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right) = 2 \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBS}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SBR}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBQ}}} \right) = 2 \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}16 + 8 = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBS}}} \right) = 24{/tex} sq. cm
Since {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{RAS}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBQ}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQRS}}} \right){/tex} 
Therefore, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{RAS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBQ}}} \right) = 24{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. P is any point on CD. If ar ({tex}\triangle{/tex}DPA) = 15 cm2 and ar ({tex}\triangle{/tex}APC) = 20 cm2, then ar ({tex}\triangle{/tex}APB) =
questions.explanation:
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{ADC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{ADP}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{APC}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}= {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}{\rm{15 + 20}} = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right)=35{/tex} sq. cm ...(i)
Since triangles APB and parallelogram ABCD are on the same base AB and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = 35{/tex} sq. cm [From eq.(i)]

questions.question: M and N are the mid-points of sides DC and AB respectively, of a rectangle ABCD. If{tex}ar\;(rectangle\;ABCD) = 48\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle EMC){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since M and N are mid-points of DC and AB respectively. Then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{MCBN}}} \right) = {{48} \over 2} = 24{/tex} sq. cm
Now, MD is diagonal, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BMC}}} \right) = {{24} \over 2} = 12{/tex} sq. cm
Now, by ASA congruency, {tex}\triangle {\rm{BMC}} \cong \triangle {\rm{DMC}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{DMC}}} \right) = 12{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} DM {tex}\times{/tex} DE = 12
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} MC {tex}\times{/tex} DE = 12 [Given: DM = MC]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EMC}}} \right) = 12{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: ABCD is a rectangle in which AB = 8 units and AD = 3 units. If DCEF is a parallelogram, then the area of {tex}\triangle EFG{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Area of rectangle ABCD = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 24 sq. cm
Since rectangle ABCD and parallelogram DCEF are on the same base CD and between the same parallels, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABCD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ DCEF}}} \right) = 24{/tex} sq. cm
Also, triangle EFG and parallelogram DCEF are on the same base EF and between the same parallels, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EFG}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ DCEF}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 24 = 12{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: ABCD is a quadrilateral. A line through D, parallel to AC meets BC produced at E. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle ABE) = 36\;c{m^2},{/tex} then the ar (quad ABCD) is
questions.explanation:
Here, AD = EC  [Opposite sides of vertically opposite angles]
According to question, AC is a median, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ACE}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 36 = 18{/tex} sq. cm
Since diagonal of a quadrilateral divides it into two triangles of equal area. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABCD}}} \right) = 2 \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = 2 \times 18 = 36{/tex} cm2

questions.question: In given figure ABCD and AGEF are parallelograms. If {tex}ar(\parallel \;AGEF) = 27\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle ADG) + ar(\triangle GCB){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since triangle AGB and parallelogram AGEF are on the same base AG and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGB}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ AGEF}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 27 = 13.5{/tex} sq. cm
Also, since triangle AGB and parallelogram ABCD are on the same base AG and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGB}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 13.5{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 27{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADG}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{GBC}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGB}}} \right) = 27{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADG}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{GBC}}} \right) + 13.5 = 27{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADG}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{GBC}}} \right) =13.5{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: ABCD is a trapezium in which {tex}AB\parallel DC.{/tex} A line through A parallel to BC meets diagonal BD at P. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle BPC) = 5\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle ABD){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since triangles BPC and BAC are on the same base BC and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BPC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BAC}}} \right) = 5{/tex} sq. cm
Again, since triangles ABD and BAC are on the same base BC and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BAC}}} \right) = 5{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: PQRS and ADEQ are rectangles. {tex}RE\parallel AP.{/tex} If {tex}ar\;(ACPQ) = 25\;c{m^2}{/tex} and {tex}ar(ABEP) = 10\;c{m^2},{/tex}then ar (PQRS) is
questions.explanation:
Since ABEP and ARFP are on the same base AP and between the same parallels, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABEP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ARFP}}} \right) = 10{/tex} sq. cm
Also, since ARSC and ARFP are on the same base AR and between the same parallels, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ARSC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ARFP}}} \right) = 10{/tex} sq. cm
Therefore, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABFP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ARSC}}} \right) = 10{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{PQRS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ACPQ}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ARSC}}} \right) = 25 + 10 = 35{/tex}cm2

tests.name: Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles CBSE Online Test 04. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. O is any point on diagonal BD. If {tex}ar(\triangle DOP) = 8\;c{m^2},{/tex} {tex}ar(\triangle BOS) = 3\,c{m^2}{/tex} and {tex}ar(\triangle APS) = 6\,c{m^2},{/tex}then {tex}ar(\parallel \;ABCD){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since PS is a diagonal of parallelogram ASOP. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ASP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SOP}}} \right) = 6{/tex} sq. cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ASOP}}} \right) = 6 + 6 = 12{/tex} sq. cm
Now, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ASOP}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{SOB}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{POD}}} \right){/tex} = 12 + 3 + 8 = 23 sq. cm
Since BD is a diagonal of parallelogram ABCD, therefore
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 2 \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle{\rm{ABD}}} \right) = 2 \times 23 = 46{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a rectangle and EFGH is a trapezium DE = CH. If {tex}ar\;(ABCD) = 26\,c{m^2},{/tex} then ar (EFGH) is
questions.explanation:
In triangles FED and CHG,
{tex}\angle {\rm{FDG}} = \angle {\rm{GCH}} = {90^ \circ }{/tex} 
DE = CH [Given]
{tex}\angle {\rm{EFD}} = \angle {\rm{CGH}}{/tex} [Angle opposite to equal side]
{tex}\triangle {\rm{FED}} \cong \triangle {\rm{CHG}}{/tex} [By ASA congruency]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\triangle {\rm{FED}} = {\rm{area}}\triangle {\rm{CHG}}{/tex} .........(i)
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\Delta {\rm{PGO}} = {\rm{area}}\Delta {\rm{FOA}}{/tex} .........(ii)
Since triangles CHG and OHG are on the same base GH and betweent the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHG}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{OHG}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHG}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PHG}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{OHG}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PHG}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PGO}}} \right){/tex} ..........(iii)
From eq.(i), (ii) and (iii), we have
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{FOA}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2\times{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHP}}} \right) = 2 \times{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{FOA}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHG}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{rect}}{\rm{.ABGH}}} \right){/tex} .............(iv)
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDF}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{rect}}{\rm{.ABGH}}} \right){/tex} ...............(v)
Adding eq. (iv) and (v), we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHG}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{rect}}{\rm{.ABFG}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{trap}}{\rm{.EFGH}}} \right){\rm{ = area}}\left( {{\rm{rect}}{\rm{.DCGF}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{CHG}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{trap}}{\rm{.EFGH}}} \right){\rm{ = area}}\left( {{\rm{rect}}{\rm{.DCGF}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{ABGF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{rect}}{\rm{.ABCD}}} \right) = 26{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD, ABEF and AGHF are parallelograms. If {tex}ar\;(ABCD) = 23\;c{m^2},{/tex} then ar (FGH) is
questions.explanation:
Since parallelograms ABCD and ABEF are on the same base AB and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) = 23{/tex} sq. cm
Since parallelograms AGHF and ABEF are on the same base AF and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ AGHF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABEF}}} \right) = 23{/tex} sq. cm
Now, since triangle FGH and parallelogram AGHF are on the same base and between the same parallels, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{FGH}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ AGHF}}} \right) = {{23} \over 2} = 11.5{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram in which DC is produced to P such that DC = CP. AP intersects BC at Q. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle BQD) = 3\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar(\parallel \;ABCD){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since triangles BQD and BQA are on the same base BQ and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BQD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BQA}}} \right) = 3{/tex} sq. cm
In triangles ABQ and CQP,
{tex}\angle {\rm{AQB}} = \angle {\rm{CQP}}{/tex} [Vertically opposite angles]
AB = CP [Since CP = DC and DC = AB]
BQ = CQ [Given]
Therefore, {tex}\triangle {\rm{ABQ}} \cong \triangle {\rm{CQP}}{/tex} [By SAS congurancy]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area }}\triangle {\rm{ABQ}} = {\rm{area }}\triangle {\rm{CQP = 3}}{/tex} sq. cm
Similarly using SAS criterion of congurancy, {tex}\triangle {\rm{CQP}} \cong \triangle {\rm{DCQ}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area }}\triangle {\rm{CQP}} = {\rm{area }}\triangle {\rm{DCQ = 3}}{/tex} sq. cm
Now, BD is diagonal of parallelogram ABCD
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 2 \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BCD}}} \right) = 2 \times \left( {3 + 3} \right) = 12{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: PQRS is a parallelogram whose diagonals PR and SQ intersect at O. A line segment through O meets PQ at A and SR at B. If {tex}ar\;(PQRS) = 25\;c{m^2},{/tex} then ar (SBAP) is
questions.explanation:
Since BP is a digonal of parallelogram PQRS. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 25 = 12.5{/tex} cm2
Now, in triangles POA and BOR,
{tex}\angle {\rm{POA}} = \angle {\rm{BOR}}{/tex} [Vertically opposite angles]
OP = OR   [Diagonals of parallelogram bisect each other]
OA = OB   [O is the mid-point of diagonals]
Therefore, {tex}\triangle {\rm{POA}} \cong \triangle {\rm{BOR}}{/tex}   [By SAS congruency]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area }}\triangle {\rm{POA}} = {\rm{area }}\triangle {\rm{BOR}}{/tex}                ...............(i)
Now, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{PRS}}} \right) = 12.5{/tex} cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRS}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{OBR}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{OBR}}} \right) = 12.5{/tex}cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRS}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{OBR}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{OPA}}} \right) = 12.5{/tex}cm2 [From eq.(i)]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{SBAP}}} \right) = 12.5{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD and ABFE are parallelograms as shown in the figure. If {tex}ar\;(ABCD) = 24\;c{m^2}{/tex} and {tex}ar\;(ABFE) = 18\;c{m^2},{/tex} then ar (EFCD) is
questions.explanation:
Construction: Produced AB to G, cuts CF at G.
Since parallelogram ABCD and CDHG are on the same base CD and between the same parallels, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ CDHG}}} \right) = 24{/tex} cm2
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABFE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ EFGH}}} \right) = 18{/tex} cm2
Therefore, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{EFCD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{EDHG}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{EFGH}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{EFCD}}} \right) = 24 + 18 = 42{/tex} cm2

questions.question: PQRS is a trapezium. A is any point on PQ and {tex}AB\parallel QR.{/tex} If {tex}ar\;(\triangle PBQ) = 17\,c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle ASR){/tex}is
questions.explanation:
Since triangles ABQ and ABR are on the same base AB and between the same parallels, therefore
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABQ}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABR}}} \right){/tex}              .........(i)
Since triangles, ABS and ABP are on the same base AB and between the same parallels, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABP}}} \right){/tex}              .........(ii)
Adding eq.(i) and (ii),
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABQ}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABP}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABR}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABS}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQB}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ASR}}} \right) = 17{/tex} cm2

questions.question: PQRS is a trapezium. A line drawn parallel to QP through R meets a line parallel to RP drawn through S at X. If ar (PQRS) is 22 {tex}c{m^2}{/tex} and {tex}ar\;(\triangle PQR) = 8\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle PXR){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {{\rm{PQRS}}} \right) - {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQR}}} \right){/tex} = 22 - 8 = 14 cm2
Since {tex}{\triangle {\rm{PRS}}}{/tex} and {tex}{\triangle {\rm{PRX}}}{/tex} are on the same base PR and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRS}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PRX}}} \right){/tex} = 14 cm2

questions.question: ABCD and FECG are parallelograms equal in area. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle AQE) = 12\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\parallel \;FGBQ){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Given: {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AQE}}} \right) = 12{/tex} cm2
Since AE is a diagonal of AQED, then
Area of AQED = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 24 cm2
Now, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}= {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ FGCE}}} \right){/tex} [Given]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of AQED + Area of QBCE = Area of QBCE + Area of FGBQ
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of AQED = Area of FGBQ
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of FGBQ = 24 cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. P is the mid-point of AB. BD and CP intersect at Q,. CQ : QP = 3 : 1. If {tex}ar(\triangle PBQ) = 10\,c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\parallel \;ABCD){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Given: CQ : QP = 3 : 1
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BQC}}} \right) = 3 \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBQ}}} \right) = 3 \times 10 = 30{/tex} cm2
Then, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBQ}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{QBC}}} \right) = 10 + 30 = 40{/tex} cm2
Since CP is the median of triangle ABC, then
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PBC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{APC}}} \right) = 40{/tex} cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = 40 + 40 = 80{/tex} cm2
Since AC is a diagonal of parallelogram ABCD, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right) = 2 \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = 2 \times 80 = 160{/tex} cm2

questions.question: Medians of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC intersect at G. If ar ({tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC) = 27 cm2, then ar ({tex}\triangle{/tex}BGC) =
questions.explanation:
According to quesiton,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADC}}} \right){/tex} ........(i)
And, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{GBD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{GDC}}} \right){/tex} ..........(ii)
Subtracting eq.(ii) from eq.(i), we get
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGB}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGC}}} \right){/tex} 
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGB}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BGC}}} \right){/tex} 
Therefore, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGB}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle{\rm{BGC}}} \right) {/tex} {tex}= {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGC}}} \right){/tex} 
But {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGB}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BGC}}} \right) {/tex}{tex}+ {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AGC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{3}} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BGC}}} \right) = {{27} \over 3} = 9{/tex} cm2

questions.question: In the given figure, QA = AB = BC = CR. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle PQR) = 24\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle PAR){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Draw a line parallel to QR, passes through P.
Since {tex}\triangle {\rm{PQA}},\triangle {\rm{PAB}},\triangle {\rm{PBC}}{/tex} and {tex}\triangle {\rm{PCR}}{/tex} are on the equal bases and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}ar\triangle {\rm{PQA}} =ar \triangle {\rm{PAB}} = ar\triangle {\rm{PBC}} = ar\triangle {\rm{PCR}}{/tex} 
But {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQA}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PAB}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{PBC}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PCR}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQR}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}{\rm{4}} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQA}}} \right) = 24{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PQA}}} \right) = 6{/tex} cm2
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PAR}}} \right) = 6 \times 3 = 18{/tex} cm2

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC if D and E are mid-points of BC and AD respectively such that ar({tex}\triangle{/tex}AEC) = 4 cm2, then ar({tex}\triangle{/tex}BEC) =
questions.explanation:
Since E is the mid-point of AD.
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ECD}}} \right) = 4{/tex} cm2
Similarly, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ECD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDB}}} \right) = 4{/tex} cm2
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BEC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BED}}} \right) {/tex}{tex}+ {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDC}}} \right) = 4 + 4 = 8{/tex} cm2

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. M is any point on AD. P is the mid-point of BM. If the area of parallelogram {tex}ABCD = 28\;c{m^2},{/tex} then the area of {tex}\triangle MPC{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
ABCD is a parallelogram. M is any point on AD. P is the mid-point of BM. If the area of parallelogram {tex}ABCD = 28\;c{m^2},{/tex} then the area of {tex}\triangle MPC{/tex} is
Triangle BMC and parallelogram ABCD are in the same base BC and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BMC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BMC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 28 = 14{/tex} cm2
In triangles BPC and MPC,
MP = BP [Given]
PC = PC [Common]
{tex}\angle {\rm{PCM}} = \angle {\rm{BCP}}{/tex}[Angles opposite to equal sides]
Therefore, {tex}\triangle {\rm{BPC}} \cong \triangle {\rm{MPC}}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{ar }}\triangle {\rm{BPC}} \cong {\rm{ar }}\triangle {\rm{MPC}}{/tex} 
Now, {tex}{\rm{ar }}\triangle {\rm{BMC}} = 14{/tex} cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{ar }}\triangle {\rm{MPC}} + {\rm{ar }}\triangle {\rm{MPC}}{/tex} = 14 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2\times{\rm{ar }}\triangle {\rm{MPC}}{/tex} = 14 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{ar }}\triangle {\rm{MPC}}{/tex} = 7 cm2

questions.question: P is any point on the base BC of {tex}\triangle ABC.{/tex} D is the mid-point of BC. DE is drawn parallel to PA, If {tex}ar\;(\triangle ABC) = 12\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle EPC){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
According to question,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADC}}} \right){/tex} = {tex}{{12} \over 2} = 6{/tex} cm2
Since triangles AED and PED are on the same base and between the same parallels. Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AED}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PED}}} \right){/tex} 
In the figure,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EPC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EPD}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EPC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle{\rm{AED}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EDC}}} \right){/tex}[Triangles EPD and AED are on the same base and between the same parallels]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{EPC}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADC}}} \right) = 6{/tex} cm2

tests.name: Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles Test 05
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Areas of Parallelograms and Triangles CBSE Online Test 05. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: ABC is a triangle in which D is the mid-point of BC. E and F are mid-points of DC and AE respectively. If {tex}ar\;(\triangle ABC) = 16\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(\triangle DEF){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Given: {tex}ar\;(\triangle ABC) = 16\;c{m^2},{/tex} 
Since AD is median of triangle ABC, and median of triangle divided it into two triangles of equal area, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADC}}} \right) = {/tex} {tex}{{16} \over 2} = 8{/tex} cm2
Now, since AE is median of triangle ADC, and median of triangle divided it into two triangles of equal area, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{AEC}}} \right) = {/tex} {tex}{{8} \over 2} = 4{/tex} cm2
Now, again since DF is median of triangle ADE, and median of triangle divided it into two triangles of equal area, therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADF}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{DEF}}} \right) = {/tex} {tex}{{4} \over 2} = 2{/tex} cm2

questions.question: If AD is median of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} and P is a point on AC such that {tex}ar\;(\triangle ADP):ar(\triangle ABD){/tex} = 2 : 3, then {tex}ar\;(\triangle PDC):ar(\triangle ABC){/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Given: {tex}{{{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADP}}} \right)} \over {{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right)}} = {2 \over 3}{/tex} 
Then, {tex}{{{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PDC}}} \right)} \over {{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right)}} = 1 - {2 \over 3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{{{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PDC}}} \right)} \over {{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABD}}} \right)}} = {1 \over 3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}{{{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PDC}}} \right)} \over {{1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right)}} = {1 \over 3}{/tex} [Median of a triangle divides it into two triangles of equal area]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{{{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PDC}}} \right)} \over {{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right)}} = {1 \over 6}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{PDC}}} \right):{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABC}}} \right) = 1:6{/tex}

questions.question: The altitude of a parallelogram is twice the length of the base and its area is 1250 cm2. The lengths of the base and the altitude respectively are :
questions.explanation:
Let the length of the base of the parallelogram be x cm, then its altitude will be 2x cm.
According to question,
Area of parallelogram = 1250 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height = 1250 cm2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x {tex}\times{/tex} 2x = 1250 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x2 = 1250 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 625
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 25 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = 25 cm
Then Altitude, 2x = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 25 = 50 cm

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram one of whose diagonals is AC. Then, which of the following is true?
questions.explanation:
In the given figure,
Area of triangle ADC = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} DC {tex}\times{/tex} AE
And, Area of triangle CBA = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} AB {tex}\times{/tex} AE   [Since AE is the height of triangle CBA also as it is between two parallel lines]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} DC {tex}\times{/tex} AE  [Since AB = DC, opposite sides of a parallelogram are equal]
Therefore, {tex}ar\;(\triangle ADC) = ar(\triangle CBA){/tex}

questions.question: In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram and EFCD is rectangle. Now which of the following is correct option?
questions.explanation:
If a parallelogram and a rectangle are on the same base and between the same parallels, then area of parallelogram and that of rectangle are equal.
Therefore, according to question,
{tex}ar\;(\parallel ABCD)\; = \;ar\;(rect.\;EFCD){/tex}

questions.question: The figure obtained by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rectangle of sides 8 cm and 6 cm is :
questions.explanation:
We know, that the figure obtained on joining the midpoints of a rectangle is a rhombus.

Let ABDE be a rectangle in which AB = 8 cm and BD = 6 cm.
And F, G, H and I are the mid-points of the sides AB, BD, DE and AE respectively. FGHI is a rhombus.
Now, the diagonals of the rhombus FGHI are FH and GI.
FH = AB = 8 cm and GI = BD = 6 cm
Area of rhombus FHGI = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex}FH {tex}\times{/tex} GI = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 24 cm2
Therefore, the figure obtained by joining the mid-points of the adjacent sides of a rectangle of sides 8 cm and 6 cm is a rhombus with area 24 cm2.

questions.question: In the figure if area of parallelogram ABCD is {tex}30\;c{m^2},{/tex} then {tex}ar\;(ADE) + ar(BCE){/tex}is equal to
questions.explanation:
In the given figure, parallelogram ABCD and triangle ABE are on the same base and between the same parallels.
Therefore, {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times 30 = 15{/tex} cm2
Now Area of parallelogram ABCD =  {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADE}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BCE}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 30 = {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADE}}} \right) + 15 + {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BCE}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADE}}} \right){\rm{ + area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BCE}}} \right){/tex} = 30 - 15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ADE}}} \right){\rm{ + area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BCE}}} \right){/tex} = 15 cm2

questions.question: In the figure, parallelogram ABEF and rectangle ABCD have the same base AB and equal area. If AB = x, BC = y and BE = z, then :
questions.explanation:
Since the area of parallelogram ABEF and rectangle ABCD are equal and they have the same base AB, hence heights (altitudes) are equal.
Now. Perimeter of parallelogram ABEF = 2(AB + BE) .........(i)
And Perimeter of rectangle ABCD = 2(AB + CD) ..........(ii)
In triangle AFD, {tex}\angle {\rm{ADF}} = {90^ \circ }{/tex} and angle AFD is an acute angle.
Therefore, AF > AD
Similarly, BE > BC {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z > y   [Side opposite to greater angle]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2z > 2y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2z + 2x > 2y + 2x     [Adding 2x both sides]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2(z + x) > 2(y + x)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + z > x + y
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y < x + z

questions.question: In the figure, ABCD is a parallelogram, if area of {tex}\triangle AEB{/tex} is {tex}16\;c{m^2},{/tex} then area of {tex}\triangle BFC{/tex} is :
questions.explanation:
Given: Area of triangle ABE = 16 cm2
Since Parallelogram ABCD and {tex}\triangle {\rm{ABE}}{/tex} are on the same base and between two parallels.
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} .......... (i)
Also,
Since Parallelogram ABCD and {tex}\Delta {\rm{BFC}}{/tex} are on the same base and between two parallels.
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BFC}}} \right) = {1 \over 2} \times {\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ ABCD}}} \right){/tex} ..........(ii)
From eq.(i) and (ii), we have
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{ABE}}} \right) = {\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BFC}}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\triangle {\rm{BFC}}} \right) = 16{/tex} cm2

questions.question: In the figure, PQRS and PQLM are parallelograms and X is any point on side QL. The area of {tex}\triangle PMX{/tex} is equal to:
questions.explanation:
In the given figure,
Since Parallelogram PQLM and triangle PMX are on the same base PM.
Therefore,
{tex}{\rm{area}}\left( {\Delta {\rm{PMX}}} \right) = {\frac 12}\left[ {{\rm{area}}\left( {\parallel gm{\rm{ PQLM}}} \right)} \right]{/tex}

questions.question: In the figure, the area of parallelogram ABCD is :
questions.explanation:
In the given figure, Base of parallelogram ABCD = AB and its corresponding height = BM
Also Base of parallelogram ABCD = DC and its corresponding height = DL
Therefore
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of paralellogram ABCD = DC {tex}\times{/tex} DL

questions.question: In the figure, PQRS is a parallelogram, {tex}PM \bot RS{/tex} and {tex}RN \bot PS.{/tex} If PQ = 12 cm, PM = 6 cm and RN = 8 cm, then the length of PS is equal to :
questions.explanation:
According to question,
Area of parallelogram PQRS = PQ {tex}\times{/tex} PM
= 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 72 cm2
Also,
Area of parallelogram PQRS = PS {tex}\times{/tex} RN
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 72 = PS {tex}\times{/tex} 8
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} PS = {tex}{{72} \over 8}{/tex} = 9 cm

questions.question: Two adjacent sides of a parallelogram are 24 cm and 18 cm. If the distance between the longer sides is 12 cm, then the distance between the shorter sides is :
questions.explanation:
Let two adjacent sides of a parallelogram be AB = 24 cm and AD = 18 cm and Height AE = 12 cm.

Then Area of parallelogram ABCD = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding Height = AB {tex}\times{/tex} AE
= 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 288 cm2
Now, taking BC = 18 cm as base, then
Area of Parallelogram ABCD = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding height
288 = 18 {tex}\times{/tex} AF
AF = {tex}{{288} \over {18}}{/tex} 
AF = 16 cm.
Therefore the distance between the shorter sides is 16 cm.

questions.question: The area of the parallelogram ABCD in the figure is :
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= AB {tex}\times{/tex} BD
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
= 12 cm2

questions.question: If the sum of the parallel sides of a trapezium is 7 cm and distance between them is 4 cm, then area of the trapezium is:
questions.explanation:
Given: Sum of the parallel sides of a trapezium is 7 cm and distance between them is 4 cm.
Area of the trapezium = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}(Sum of parallel sides) {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
= 14 cm2

tests.name: Circles Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Circles CBSE Online Test 01. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\angle BPC = {19^o},{/tex} arc AB = arc BC = arc CD. Then, the measure of {tex}\angle APD{/tex} is
questions.explanation: Equal arcs subtend equal angles at the centre and the angle subtended by them at the circumference would be double the angle subtended by them at the centre. As the angle subtended at centre were same so the angle subtended at the circumference would also become same. Thus each arc would make an angle of {tex}{19^o}{/tex}. Thus the total length of all the three angles would be thrice {tex}{19^o}{/tex} that is {tex}{57^o}{/tex}.

questions.question: The given figures show two congruent circles with centre O and O’. Arc AXB subtends an angle of {tex}{75^o}{/tex} at the centre and arc A’YB’ subtends an angle of {tex}{25^o}{/tex} at the centre O’. Then, the ratio of arcs AXB to A ‘YB’ is
questions.explanation:
Since, circles are congruent thus we can consider the two arcs as in the same circle. Now the length of the arcs is directly proportional the angle subtended by the arcs. Therefore the lengths of the given arcs would be same in ratio as the ratio of the given angles. Hence the required ratio is:
{tex}{75\over 25}={3\over 1}{/tex}

questions.question: Greatest chord of a circle is called its
questions.explanation: Since diameter is the longest segment that can be drawn in a circle(touching the circle at both ends), therefore it is the longest possible chord also.

questions.question: Angle formed in minor segment of a circle is
questions.explanation:
Proof: BA is the major arc and angle ADB is the angle formed by it in the alternate segment.
Therefore 2{tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = m (arc BA)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 360 - m (arc AB)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}ADB = 360 - {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB (since (arc AB) = angle AOB )
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}ADB > 360 - 180 (since {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB < 180)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB > 90°
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ADB is obtuse.

questions.question: Number of circles that can be drawn through three non-collinear points is
questions.explanation: Only 1 circle can be drawn from three non-collinear points.

questions.question: AB is a chord of a circle with radius r. If P is any point on the circle such that {tex}\angle {/tex}APB is a right angle, then AB is equal to
questions.explanation: Since the angle subtended by the diameter or the semicircle is a right angle, therefore the AB is nothing but the diameter. Hence, it is equal to 2r, r being the radius.

questions.question: Two chords AB and CD intersect at right angles. If {tex}\angle BAC = {40^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle ABD{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Let AB and CD intersect at O. Then In the triangle ACO,
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o
40o + {tex}\angle{/tex}C + 90o = 180o
130o + {tex}\angle{/tex}C  = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 50o 
As per the theorem, Angles in the same segment of a circle are equal. Hence, {tex}\angle ABD=\angle C{/tex} = 50o

questions.question: In the given figure if OA = 5 cm, AB = 8 cm and OD is perpendicular to AB, then CD is equal to
questions.explanation:
AC = 4 cm and In triangle ACO,
AC2 + OC2 =AO2
42 + OC2 = 52
16 + OC2 = 25
OC2 = 25 - 16
OC2 = 9
OC = 3
Now OD being the radius is 5 cm and OC is 3 cm.
So, CD = OD - OC = 5 - 3 = 2 cm

questions.question: X is a point on a circle with centre O. If X is equidistant from the two radii OP, OQ, then arc PX : arc PQ is equal to
questions.explanation:
Since, PX = XQ 
2PX = PQ
PX : PQ= 1 : 2

questions.question: If ABCD is a cyclic trapezium in which AD{tex}\parallel {/tex} BC and {tex}\angle B = {60^o}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle BCD{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Since ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral
{tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o
60o + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}D = 120o
Now since AD is parallel to BC 
{tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}C + 120o = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}C = 60o

questions.question: If a straight line APQB is drawn to cut two concentric circles, then
questions.explanation:
Let OD is perpendicular to AB. Then AD = DB.
Also DP = DQ
Therefore, AP = AD - PD
= BD - DQ
= BQ
Hence, AP = BQ

questions.question: If AB = 12 cm, BC = 16 and AB is perpendicular to BC, then the radius of the circle passing through the points A, B and C is
questions.explanation:
Since AB is perpendicular to BC, therefore ABC is a right-angled triangle right angled at B. As clear from the figure, AC would act as the diameter 
{tex}AB^2+ BC^2 = AC^2{/tex} 
{tex}12^2+ 16^2=AC^2{/tex} 
{tex}AC=20{/tex} 
Since AC is diameter so radius = 10 cm.

questions.question: The value of x in the given figure is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle CDB = {110^0\over 2}=55^0{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\angle ADB = 90^0{/tex}  (Angle in a semicircle)
So, {tex}\angle ADC=x=90^0-55^0=35^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle BAC = {25^o}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle BOC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation: Angle made at centre by an arc is double the angle made by it on any point on the circumference.

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle ADC = {118^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle BDC{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle ADB+\angle BDC=118^0 {/tex} 
{tex}90^0+\angle BDC=118^0 \Rightarrow \angle BDC=28^0{/tex}

General Instructions:

  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
