If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: ABCD is a parallelogram. A circle passes through A and D and cuts AB at E and DC at F. If {tex}\angle BEF = {80^o}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle ABC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle AEF+80^0=180^0{/tex} (Linear Pair)
{tex}\angle AEF=100^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle ADF+\angle AEF=180^0{/tex} (Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral)
{tex}\angle ADF=180^0-100^0=80^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle ADF=\angle ABC=80^0{/tex}(Opposite angles of a parallelogram)

questions.question: If a chord of a circle is equal to its radius, then the angle subtended by this chord in major segment is
questions.explanation:
Since the chord is equal to the radius therefore, it will form an equilateral triangle inside the circle with the third vertex being the centre of the circle.
So the chord will make an angle of 60o at the centre. As the angle made by the chord at any other point of the circumference would be half.
So, we have that angle made at the major segment would be 30o.

questions.question: In the given figure PQ and RS are two equal chords of a circle with centre O. OA and OB are perpendiculars on chords PQ and RS, respectively. If {tex}\angle AOB = {140^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle PAB{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABO, AO = BO
So, {tex}\angle BAO=\angle ABO=x {/tex} 
{tex}x+x+140^0=180^0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow 2x=40^0 {/tex} 
{tex}x=20^0{/tex} 
Now {tex}\angle QAO+\angle BAO+\angle PAB=180^0{/tex} 
Substituting the values we get:-
{tex}90^0+20^0+\angle PAB=180^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle PAB=70^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure PQ = QR = RS and {tex}\angle PTS = {75^o}{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle QOR{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle PTS = {75^o}{/tex} 
Now {tex}\angle PTQ=\angle QTR=\angle RTS {/tex} ( Equal chords would make equal angles at centre and thus equal angles at the circumference)
{tex}\angle QTR={75^0\over3}=25^0{/tex} 
So, {tex}\angle QOR=25^0\times2=50^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle ABC = {50^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle BDC = {40^o}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle BCA{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle BDC=\angle BAC=40^0{/tex} 
In triangle ABC, 
{tex}\angle A+\angle B+\angle C=180^0{/tex}   ( angles of a triangle)
{tex}50^0+40^0+\angle C=180^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle C=90^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, AC is a diameter of the given circle and {tex}\angle BCD = {75^o}{/tex}. Then, {tex}\angle EAF - \angle ABC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle B =90^0{/tex} (Angle in semicircle)
{tex}\angle BAD=180^0-75^0=105^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle EAF=\angle BAD=105^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle EAF-\angle ABC=105^0-90^0=15^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle. {tex}\angle OAB{/tex} and {tex}\angle OCB{/tex} are {tex}{40^o}{/tex} and {tex}{30^o}{/tex} respectively. Then, the measure of {tex}\angle AOC{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
OA = OB = OC = Radius 
so, {tex}\angle OAB{/tex} = {tex}\angle ABO{/tex} = 40°
and, {tex}\angle OCB{/tex} = {tex}\angle ABO{/tex} = 30°
Thus, {tex}\angle ABC{/tex} = 30 + 40 = 70°
So, {tex}\angle AOC{/tex} = 70 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 140° {Angle subtended by arc at centre is twice of angle subtended at circumference}

questions.question: If {tex}\angle OAB = {40^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABC,
{tex}\angle A + \angle B + \angle C = 180^0{/tex} 
{tex}40^0+90^0+\angle C = 180^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle C = 50^0{/tex}

questions.question: BC is a diameter of the circle and {tex}\angle BAO = {60^o}{/tex}. Then {tex}\angle ADC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABO,
OA = OB, {tex}\angle A = \angle B=60^0{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\angle ABO= \angle ADC=60^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle and {tex}\angle AOC = {130^o}{/tex}. Then {tex}\angle ABC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
In major segment, {tex}\angle AOC = 360^0-130^0=230^0{/tex} 
So, {tex}\angle ABC={230^0\over2}=115^0{/tex} (Angle made by an arc at the centre is double the angle made by it on any other point on the circumference of the same segment)

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle CAB = {50^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle ABC = {70^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle ADB{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
In triangle ABC, {tex}\angle A +\angle B + \angle C=180^0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle C = 60^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle ACB=\angle ADB=60^0{/tex} (Angle made by the same chord are equal)

questions.question: ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral such that AB is a diameter of the circle circumscribing it and {tex}\angle ADC = {140^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle BAC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
In the given quadrilateral,

 {tex}\angle ABC + \angle ADC = 180^0{/tex}

{tex}140^0+\angle ABC=180^0{/tex}

{tex}\angle ABC = 40^0{/tex}

Since, AB is diameter so ABCD lies in semi-circle.

thus, {tex}\angle BCA= 90^0{/tex}

In triangle, ABC,

{tex}\angle A +\angle B + \angle C=180^0{/tex}

{tex}\angle BAC = 180^0-40^0-90^0 =180^0-130^0=50^0{/tex}

{tex}\angle BAC = 50^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle ABC = {45^o}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle AOC{/tex} =
questions.explanation: The angle made by an arc at the centre is double the angle made by it on any other point on the circumfrence.

questions.question: O is the centre of the given circle. If {tex}\angle APB = {120^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle DBC = {25^o}{/tex}, then the measure of {tex}\angle ADB{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Now, {tex}\angle APB + \angle CPB =180^0{/tex} (Linear Pair)
{tex}120^0+\angle CPB=180^0{/tex} 
{tex}\angle CPB=60^0{/tex} 
Now from angle sum property, we can calculate the values of {tex}\angle PCB{/tex} and we find that {tex}\angle PCB = 95^0{/tex} 
Since, {tex}\angle PCB=\angle ADB=95^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle. If {tex}\angle DBA = {35^o}{/tex} , then the measure of {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Join OA.
Now, in triangle AOB, from angle sum property we can find that {tex}\angle AOB = 110^0{/tex} 
Now, {tex}2\angle ACB=\angle AOB={110^0\over2}=55^0{/tex}

tests.name: Circles Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Circles CBSE Online Test 03. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.

  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle CDB = {40^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle PAC{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since {tex}\angle CDB=\angle CAB{/tex} 
So, {tex}\angle CAB=40^0{/tex} 
Now {tex}\angle PAC+\angle CAB=180^0{/tex} [Linear Pair]
Hence, {tex}\angle PAC = 140^0{/tex}

questions.question: The given figure shows two intersecting circles. If {tex}\angle ABC = {75^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle PAD{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since {tex}\angle PQC+\angle PBC=180^0{/tex} (Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral)
{tex}\angle PQC=180^0-75^0=105^0{/tex} 
Again, {tex}\angle DQP+\angle PQC=180^0{/tex} (Linear Pair)
so, {tex}\angle DQP=75^0{/tex} 
Also, {tex}\angle PAD+\angle DQP=180^0{/tex} (Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral)
{tex}\angle PAD=105^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, chords AB and CD intersect at P. If {tex}\angle DPB = {88^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle DAP = {46^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle ABC{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle PCB=46^0{/tex} (Angles of same arc)
{tex}\angle CPB=180^0-88^0=92^0{/tex}  (Linear Pair)
So, {tex}\angle PBC=180^0-46^0-92^0=42^0{/tex} (Using angle sum property in triangle PCB)

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle ABE is a straight line. If {tex}\angle DBE = {95^o}{/tex} then {tex}\angle AOD{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle ABD=180^0-95^0=85^0{/tex}(Linear Pair)
Since, {tex}\angle AOD = 2\angle ABD=2 \times 85^0=170^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, if chords AB and CD of the circle intersect each other at right angles, then, x + y =
questions.explanation:
{tex}y=\angle ACP{/tex}  (Angles of same arc)
{tex}\angle APC=180^0-90^0=90^0{/tex}  ({tex}\angle APC, \angle CPB {/tex} are linear pair)
Thus from triangle APC,
{tex}x+y+\angle APC=180^0{/tex} 
Hence, {tex}x+y=90^0{/tex}

questions.question: The region between chord and either of the arc is called
questions.explanation: The area in pink and blue are called segment .i.e. area between chord and either of the arc.

questions.question: In the given, AB is side of regular five sided polygon and AC is a side of a regular six sided polygon inscribed in the circle with centre O. AO and CB intersect at P, then {tex}\angle APB{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here AC is side of hexagon, so it will subtend 60° angle at centre and also sides & radius are equal.
Thus,  AC = OC = OA  and {tex}\angle COA = \angle OAC = \angle ACO{/tex} = 60° 
AB is side of pentagon, so it would subtend angle of {tex}\frac{360}{5}{/tex} = 72° angle at centre.
so, {tex}\angle BOP {/tex} = 72°
SO, {tex}\angle COB = 72+60= 132°{/tex} 
Also since, OC = OB, {tex}\angle OCP = OBP{/tex} 
{tex}\triangle COB{/tex}  {tex}\angle COB + \angle OBC + \angle OCB {/tex} = 180°
2{tex}\angle OBC{/tex} = 180 - ( 132) = 48°
{tex}\angle OBC{/tex} =24°
NOW, {tex}​​​​\triangle{/tex} BOP {tex}​​​​\angle BOP + ​​​​\angle OPB +​​​​\angle PBO{/tex} =180°
{tex}​​​​\angle OPB{/tex} = 180 - (24 + 72) =180 - 96 = 84°
Now, 
{tex}​​​​\angle APB {/tex} and{tex}​​​​\angle OPB{/tex}  lie on straight line, so they are supplementry angles.
{tex}​​​​\angle APB {/tex} = 180 - {tex}​​​​\angle OPB{/tex} = 180- 84 =96°

questions.question: In a given figure, chords AD and BC intersect each other at right angles at point P. If {tex}\angle DAB = {35^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle ADC{/tex} =
questions.explanation:
From triangle APB, {tex}\angle ABP=180^0-90^0-35^0=55^0{/tex} 
Thus, {tex}\angle ADC=55^0{/tex} ({tex}\angle ABC=\angle ADC{/tex})

questions.question: What fraction of the whole circle is minor arc RP in the given figure?
questions.explanation:
Complete the cyclic quadrilateral PQRS, with S being a point on a point on the major arc. Then {tex}\angle S=60^0{/tex} (Opposite angles of a cyclic quadrilateral)
Then Major {tex}\angle POR=120^0{/tex} 
Thus fraction the minor arc {tex}=\frac{120^0}{360^0}=\frac{1}{3}{/tex}

questions.question: In the given circle, O is the centre and {tex}\angle BDC = {42^o}{/tex}. Then, {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}\angle BDC = BAC= {42^o}{/tex} {Angles in same segment are equal}
now, since AC is diameter so, ABC forms  a semi-circle, thus {tex}\angle ABC = {90^o}{/tex} 
So, in triangle ABC {tex}\angle BAC + \angle ABC +\angle ACB = {180^o}{/tex} 
{tex}\angle ACB{/tex} = 180 - (90 + 42)
= 180° - 132°
= 48°

questions.question: In the given figure, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD and O is the centre of the circle. If {tex}\angle ADC = {25^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle AEB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Here, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD and {tex}\angle ADC = {25^o},{/tex} 
So, {tex}\angle DAB = {25^o},{/tex} ( opposite interior angles are equal)
Now, {tex}\angle ADC = {25^o},{/tex} so, {tex}\angle AOC {/tex} = 50° ( Angle subtended by arc AC at centre is twice the angle subtended at circumference)
Similarly, {tex}\angle DAB = {25^o}{/tex} , So, {tex}\angle DOB = {50^o}{/tex}( Angle subtended by arc BD at centre is twice the angle subtended at circumference)
{tex}\angle AOB{/tex}  + {tex}\angle DOB{/tex} + {tex}\angle AOC {/tex} = 180° (All lie in straight line)
{tex}\angle AOB{/tex} = 180 - 50 - 50 = 80°
Now, {tex}\angle AEB{/tex} = 40° ( Angle subtended by arc AB at centre is twice the angle subtended at circumference)

questions.question: In the given figure, {tex}\angle OPQ = {30^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle ORQ = {57^o}{/tex}. Then, the measure of {tex}\angle POR{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle OPQ=\angle OQP=30^0{/tex} ( Angles of isosceles triangle OPQ )
Also, in triangle OPQ,  
{tex}\angle{/tex}O + {tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}O + 30o + 30o = 180o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}O = 180o - 60o = 120o
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ = 120o ....(1)
Again, in triangle ORQ
{tex}\angle{/tex}R = {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 57o 
And {tex}\angle{/tex}O + {tex}\angle{/tex}R + {tex}\angle{/tex}Q = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}O + 57o + 57o = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}O + 180o - 114o = 66o
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}ROQ = 66o ...(2)
From(1) and (2), we get :- 
{tex}\angle{/tex}POR = {tex}\angle{/tex}POQ - {tex}\angle{/tex}ROQ
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}POR = 120o - 66o = 54o

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle and {tex}\angle SPQ = {50^o}{/tex}. Then, the measure of {tex}\angle SRQ{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}OQP = {tex}\angle{/tex}OPQ = 50o 
{tex}\angle{/tex}POQ = 80o (From angle sum property)
{tex}\angle{/tex}SOQ = 180o - 80o = 100o (From linear pair)
Completing the cyclic quadrilateral, QRSL, ( L being any point on the circumference) 
{tex}\angle{/tex}SLQ = 50o
From cyclic quadrilateral, we have
{tex}\angle{/tex}SRQ = 180o - 50o = 130o

questions.question: In the given figure, M, A, B and N are points on a circle having centre O. AN and MB cut at Y. If {tex}\angle NYB = {50^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle YNB = {20^o},{/tex} then reflex {tex}\angle MON{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
In triangle NYB,
{tex}\angle{/tex}N + {tex}\angle{/tex}Y + {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 180o - 50o - 20o = 110o  
Complete the cyclic quadrilateral, MBNX, where X being any point on the circumference in the major segment, we have:- 
{tex}\angle{/tex}MXN = 80o - 110o = 70o
So, minor {tex}\angle{/tex}MON = 70o {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 140o 
Hence, reflex {tex}\angle{/tex}MON = 360o - 140o = 220o

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral, {tex}\angle CBQ = {48^o}{/tex} and a = 2b. Then, b is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}\angle ABC {/tex} is supplementary to {tex}\angle CBQ{/tex} 
So, {tex}\angle ABC {/tex} = 180 - 48 = 132°
Since, ABCD is cyclic quadrilateral, {tex}​​​​\angle B + ​​​​\angle D = 180°{/tex} {opposite angles are supplementery}
So, {tex}\angle D {/tex} = 180 - 132 = 48°
Now, in triangle PDC, {tex}\angle P +\angle D +\angle C{/tex} = 180° 
= a + 48° + (48°+ b) =180° {since, {tex}\angle C{/tex} is external angle to B and b, and sum of two opposite interior angles is equal to external angle}
= 3b + 96 = 180°
= 3b = 180 - 96 = 84
b = 28°

tests.name: Circles Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Circles CBSE Online Test 04. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a quadrilateral inscribed in circle with centre O. CD is produced to E. If {tex}\angle ADE = {95^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle OBA = {30^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle OAC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here, {tex}\angle ADC {/tex} & {tex}\angle ADE{/tex}  are supplementary to each other,
so, {tex}\angle ADC {/tex} = 180 - 95 = 85° 
Also, ABCD is also a cyclic quadrilateral so,  {tex}\angle ADC {/tex} and {tex}\angle ABC {/tex} are supplementary
so, {tex}\angle ABC {/tex} + {tex}\angle ADC {/tex} = 180°
{tex}\angle ABC {/tex} = 180 - 95 = 85°
angle subtended at centre is double the angle suntended at circumference.
so, {tex}\angle AOC{/tex} = 170°
Now, in triangle AOC, OA = OC( Radii) so,{tex}\angle OAC = OCA{/tex} 
{tex}​​\angle OCA + ​​\angle OAC + ​​\angle AOC =180°{/tex} 
2{tex}\angle OAC{/tex} = 180 - 170 = 10° 
{tex}\angle OAC{/tex} = 5°

questions.question: In the given figure, ABCD is a cyclic quadrilateral in which {tex}\angle BAD = {75^o},{/tex} {tex}\angle ABD = {58^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle ADC = {77^o}{/tex} , AC and BD intersect at P. the measure of {tex}\angle DPC{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since AD acts as a chord also, So, {tex}\angle ABD=\angle ACD=58^0{/tex} 
Again as CD also acts as a chord also, therefore, 
{tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC
Now, {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ABD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DBC
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}ADC + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = 180o - 77o = 103o
And therefore
{tex}\angle{/tex}DBC = 103o - 58o = 45o
Hence, {tex}\angle{/tex}DAC = 45o
Since,  
{tex}\angle{/tex}DAC = 45o
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}CAB = 75o - 45o = 30o 
But, {tex}\angle CAB = \angle BDC {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle BDC=30^0{/tex} 
Now, In triangle CPD,  
{tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 58o + {tex}\angle{/tex}P + 30o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}P = 180o - 30o - 58o = 92o

questions.question: AD is diameter (50 cm) of a circle, O being the centre and AB is a chord (48 cm). Let the centre of AB be denoted by M, then find OM
questions.explanation:
Join OM. OM will be perpendicular to AB. Since the line joining the midpoint of a chord to the centre is always perpendicular to the chord.
AB = 48 cm, So, AM = {tex}{48\over2}=24cm{/tex}  (M is the midpoint of AB)
And OA = {tex}{50\over2}=25{/tex}   (O is the midpoint of AD)
Now, applying pythagoras theorem, we get:-
OA2 = AM2 + OM2
252 + OM2
OM2 = 252 - 242
OM2 = 625 - 576 = 49
OM = 7 cm

questions.question: The given figure shows two congruent circles with centre O and O’ intersecting at A and B. If {tex}\angle AO'B = {50^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle APB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since both the triangles are congruent,
So, OA = O'A, 
OB = O'B
AB = AB  (Common)
Hence, {tex}\triangle AOB \cong\triangle AO'B{/tex} 
Thus, {tex}\angle AOB=\angle AO'B=50^0{/tex} 
Since, PB is a straight line, therefore:- 
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOP + {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}AOP = 180o -  50o = 130o 
Again, In triangle OPA,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}P = {tex}\angle{/tex}A
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}P + {tex}\angle{/tex}O = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}P + 130o = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow\angle P={50^0\over2}=25^0{/tex}
Thus, {tex}\angle OPA=25^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure if {tex}\angle CAB = {49^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle ADC = {43^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Here, 
{tex}\angle CAB = {49^o}{/tex} = {tex}\angle BDC {/tex} { Angles in same segment are equal}
So, {tex}\angle BDA = 43°+ 49°{/tex} = 92°
Now, ACBD is a cyclic quadrilateral, so, sum of opposite angles is 180°
{tex}\angle BDA + \angle ACB {/tex} = 180°
{tex}\angle ACB{/tex} = 180 - 92 = 88°

questions.question: P is a point on the diameter AB of a circle and CD is a chord perpendicular to AB. If AP = 4 cm and PB = 16 cm, the length of chord CD is
questions.explanation:
Join AC, BC. Let CD = 2x. Then CP = x
Now, in triangle ACP,  
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AC2 = AP2 + PC2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AC2 = 42 + x2 ....(1)
And BC2 = CP2 + BP2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BC2 = x2 + 162 ...(2)
Again, in triangle ABC,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AB2 = BC2 + AC2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 202 = x2 + 42 + x2 + 162
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 400 = 2x2 + 16 + 256
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 128 = 2x2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x2 = 64
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}x = 8 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 16 cm
CD = 16 cm

questions.question: In the given figure, if {tex}\angle AOB = {80^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle ABC = {30^o}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle CAO{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
In triangle AOB, OA = OB  ( Radii )
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = {tex}\angle{/tex}OBA
Now,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABO + {tex}\angle{/tex}AOB = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2{tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = 80o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB = 50o
Now, {tex}\angle CAB={1\over2}\angle AOB={80^0\over2}=40^0{/tex} 
Again, in triangle ABC, 
{tex}\angle{/tex}CAB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC + {tex}\angle{/tex}BCA = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}CAB + 30o + 40o = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}CAB = 110o 
Thus,
{tex}\angle{/tex}CAO = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAB - {tex}\angle{/tex}OAB
=110o - 50o = 60o
Hence, proved.

questions.question: In the given figure, AB is a diameter of the circle APBR. APQ and RBQ are straight lines. If {tex}\angle A = {35^o}{/tex} and , then the measure of {tex}\angle PBR{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle APB=\angle BPQ=90^0{/tex} 
Now, 
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}APB,
{tex}\angle{/tex}BAP + {tex}\angle{/tex}APB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABP = 180o
35o + 90o + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABP = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}ABP = 55o 
Again, 
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BPQ
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BPQ + {tex}\angle{/tex}PQB + {tex}\angle{/tex}PBQ = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 90o + 25o + {tex}\angle{/tex}PBQ = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}PBQ = 65o
Since, RBQ is a straight line,
{tex}\angle{/tex}RBA + {tex}\angle{/tex}ABP + {tex}\angle{/tex}PBQ = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}RBA + 55o + 65o = 180o
{tex}\angle{/tex}RBA = 60o
Finally, 
{tex}\angle{/tex}PBR = {tex}\angle{/tex}ABP + {tex}\angle{/tex}RBA
= 55o + 60o = 115o

questions.question: In the given figure, AD is the diameter of the circle and AE = DE. If {tex}\angle ABC = {115^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle CAE{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since, AE = DE,
therefore, {tex}\angle DAE =\angle ADE=45^0{/tex}  (In {tex}\Delta AED, \angle E =90^0{/tex} And other two angles are equal.)
Now, BADC is a cyclic quadrilateral, 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 115o + {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CDA = {tex}\angle{/tex}D = 65o 
Also, {tex}\angle ACD=90^0{/tex}  ( Angle in a semicircle )
So, we have:- 
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ACD,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}CDA = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD + 90o + 65o = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD = 25o 
Finally, 
{tex}\angle CAE=\angle CAD+\angle DAE=25^0+45^0=70^0{/tex}

questions.question: AOB is the diameter of the circle. If {tex}\angle AOE = {150^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle CBE{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Here, AOB is diameter,
so, {tex}\angle BOE{/tex} = 180 - 150 = 30° {Angles lie in straight line}
Now, OE & OB are radius so, OE = OB .i.e{tex}\angle OEB = ​​\angle OBE{/tex} 
In {tex}\triangle BOE{/tex}, {tex}\angle BOE{/tex} + {tex}\angle OBE+ \angle BEO{/tex} =180°
=  30 + 2{tex}​​\angle OBE{/tex} =180°
= 2{tex}​​\angle OBE{/tex}  = 180 - 30 = 150°
= {tex}​​\angle OBE{/tex} = 75°
Now, {tex}​​\angle OBE{/tex} & {tex}\angle CBE {/tex}  lie on staright line
so, {tex}​​\angle OBE{/tex} + {tex}\angle CBE {/tex} =180°
{tex}\angle CBE {/tex} = 180  - 75 = 105°

questions.question: In the given figure, P and Q are centers of two circles intersecting at B and C. ACD is a straight line. Then, the measure of {tex}\angle BQD{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle APB{/tex} = 150°, so, {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} = 75° {Angle subtended by an arc at centre is twice the angle subtended at any point on circumference}
Now, ACD is straight line, so, {tex}\angle ACB + \angle DCB = 180°{/tex} 
{tex}\angle DCB {/tex} = 180 - 75 = 105° 
Now, angle subtended by arc BD on centre is twice of {tex}\angle DCB {/tex} = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 105 = 210°
Now, {tex}\angle BQD{/tex} = 360°- 210° = 150°

questions.question: The figure shows two circles which intersect at A and B. The centre of the smaller circle is O and it lies on the circumference of the larger circle. If {tex}\angle APB = {70^o},{/tex} then the measure of {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Since, AB is a chord and makes {tex}\angle APB=70^0{/tex} at the circumference, so {tex}\angle AOB=140^0 {/tex} 
Now, as AOBC is a cyclic quadrilateral then, sum of opposite angles must be 1800. 
{tex}\angle{/tex}AOB + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 140o + {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 40o

questions.question: AOB is a diameter of the circle and C, D, E are any three points on the semicircle. Then {tex}\angle AED + \angle BCD{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Join OE, OD, OC.
We get four triangles, namely, {tex}\triangle AOE,\triangle EOD,\triangle DOC,\triangle COB{/tex} 
Now, all these triangles are isosceles triangles.
So, 
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 = {tex}\angle{/tex}2
{tex}\angle{/tex}3 = {tex}\angle{/tex}4
{tex}\angle{/tex}5 = {tex}\angle{/tex}6
{tex}\angle{/tex}7 = {tex}\angle{/tex}8
Now, required angle {tex}\angle AED + \angle BCD= \angle 2+\angle3+\angle 6+\angle7{/tex} 
Now sum of all the angles must be {tex}720^0{/tex}  (as they are the angles of four triangles {tex}\therefore Sum=180^0 \times 4=720^0{/tex})
Therefore,
{tex}\angle{/tex}1 + {tex}\angle{/tex}2 + {tex}\angle{/tex}3 + {tex}\angle{/tex}4 + {tex}\angle{/tex}5 + {tex}\angle{/tex}6 + {tex}\angle{/tex}7 + {tex}\angle{/tex}8 + {tex}\angle{/tex}9 + {tex}\angle{/tex}11 + {tex}\angle{/tex}12 = 720o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2 \angle 2+2 \angle 3+2 \angle 6+2 \angle 7+\angle 9+\angle 10+\angle 11+\angle 12=720^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2(\angle 2+\angle 3+\angle 6+\angle 7)+\angle 9+\angle 10+\angle 11+\angle 12=720^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2(\angle 2+\angle 3+\angle 6+\angle 7)+180^{0}=720{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2(\angle 2+\angle 3+\angle 6+\angle 7)=540^{\circ}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\angle 2+\angle 3+\angle 6+\angle 7)=\frac{540^{\circ}}{2}=270^{\circ}{/tex}  
Thus, the required angle is equal to {tex}270^0{/tex}

questions.question: AOB is the diameter of the circle. ABCD is a cyclic trapezium in which AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} DC. If {tex}\angle BED = {65^o}{/tex}, then {tex}\angle BDC{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
AB is diameter and ABCD is semi-circle, so {tex}\angle ADB{/tex} =90°
Also, {tex}\angle BED= \angle BAD =65°{/tex} {Angles in same segment}
so, in {tex}\triangle ABD {/tex}, {tex}\angle BAD+\angle ABD+\angle ADB{/tex}= 180°
= 65 + 90 + {tex}\angle ADB{/tex} = 180°
{tex}\angle ADB{/tex}  = 180 - 155 = 25°
Now, since, AB {tex}\parallel {/tex} CD 
So, {tex}\angle ADB{/tex} = {tex}\angle BDC{/tex} = 25°{alternate interior angles}

questions.question: in the given figure, AD is a diameter of the circle with centre O. Chords AB, BC and CD are equal. If {tex}\angle DEF = {110^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle FAB{/tex} is equal to
questions.explanation:
Here, given AB = BC = CD
Now, equal chords subtend equal angles at centre.so, {tex}\angle AOC= \angle BOC = \angle COD{/tex} 
Also, they lie in straight line so, {tex}\angle AOC+ \angle BOC +\angle COD{/tex} = 180°
{tex}\angle AOB= \angle BOC = \angle COD{/tex} = 60°
In {tex}\triangle AOB {/tex} 
{tex}AO=OB, ​​​​\angle OAB=​​​​\angle OBA{/tex} 
Since, {tex}\angle AOB=60°,{/tex} {tex}\angle OAB=​​​​\angle OBA =60°{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\angle DOE = \angle AOB =60°{/tex} ( vertically opposite angle)
In {tex}\triangle DOE, OD=OE {/tex}(radius)
so, {tex}\angle ODE= \angle OED {/tex} 
{tex}\triangle DOE, \angle DOE+ \angle ODE+ \angle OED=180°{/tex} 
2 {tex}\angle ODE= \angle OED {/tex} = 180 - 60 = 120°
{tex}\angle ODE= \angle OED {/tex} = 60°
given was, {tex}\angle DEF = {110^o},{/tex} so, {tex}\angle OEF =110-60=50°{/tex} 
Now, in {tex}\triangle EOF {/tex} OE = OF
So, {tex}\angle OEF = \angle OFE= 50°{/tex} 
In, {tex}\triangle EOF {/tex} {tex}\angle FOE = 180-(50+50)=80°{/tex} 
Now, {tex}\angle DOE+ \angle FOE + \angle AOF =180°{/tex} ( All lie on same straight line)
So, {tex}\angle AOF = 180-(80+60)=40°{/tex} 
Now, in {tex}\triangle AOF {/tex}  AO = FO
SO, {tex}\angle OFA= \angle OAF{/tex} 
In{tex}\triangle AOF {/tex}, {tex}2​​​​​\angle OAF +\angle FOA =180°{/tex} 
{tex}2​​​​​ \angle OAF +\angle FOA =180°{/tex} 
{tex}​​​​\angle OAF = 90-20=70°{/tex} 
So, {tex}\angle FAB= \angle FAO + \angle OAB{/tex} 
= 70° + 60° = 130°

tests.name: Circles Test 05
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Circles CBSE Online Test 05. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In a figure, O is the centre of the circle with AB as diameter. If {tex}\angle AOC = {40^o},{/tex} the value of x is equal to
questions.explanation:
OA = OC ( radii ) 
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}OAC = {tex}\angle{/tex}OCA = x
Again, In {tex}\triangle{/tex}OAC
{tex}\angle{/tex}OAC + {tex}\angle{/tex}OCA + {tex}\angle{/tex}AOC = 180o 
x + x + {tex}\angle{/tex}AOC = 180o
x + x + 40o = 180o
2x = 140o 
x = 70o

questions.question: For what value of x in the figure, points A, B, C and D are concyclic?
questions.explanation:
If the quadrilateral ABCD is concyclic, then, 
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o
80o + 81o + x = 180o
x = 10o

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle. If {tex}\angle CAB = {40^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle CBA = {110^o},{/tex} the value of x is :
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o - 40o - 110 = 30o
Since AB is a chord and angle made by a chord at the centre is twice the angle made by it on any point on the circumference, therefore:-
{tex}x=2 \times 30^0=60^0{/tex}

questions.question: Angle inscribed in a semicircle is :
questions.explanation: Angle inscribed in a semicircle is a right angle. Its a given theorem.

questions.question: In the given figure, O is the centre of the circle. If {tex}\angle QPR{/tex} is {tex}{50^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle QOR{/tex} is :
questions.explanation:
Angle made by a chord at the centre is twice the angle made by it on any point on the circumference. Therefore,
{tex}\angle QOR=2\angle QPR=50^0\times 2=100^0{/tex}

questions.question: Arc ABC subtends an angle of {tex}{130^o}{/tex} at the centre O of the circle. AB is extended to P. Then {tex}\angle CBP{/tex} equals :
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle AEC={130^0\over2}=65^0{/tex}
{tex}\angle{/tex}CBP = 180o - {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC
=180o - (180o - {tex}\angle{/tex}AEC)
={tex}\angle{/tex}AEC

questions.question: In the figure, O is the centre of eh circle and {tex}\angle AOB = {80^o}{/tex}. The value of x is :
questions.explanation:
Angle made by a chord at the centre is twice the angle made by it on any point on the circumference.
{tex}x={\angle AOB\over2}={80^0\over2}=40^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the figure, O is the center of the circle. If {tex}\angle OAB = {40^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} is equal to :
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}AOB,
{tex}\angle{/tex}A = {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 40o
And {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}O = 180o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}O = 100o 
So, {tex}\angle ACB={100^0\over2}=50^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the figure, if {tex}\angle DAB = {60^o},{/tex} {tex}\angle ABD = {50^o},{/tex} then {tex}\angle ACB{/tex} is equal to :
questions.explanation:
In, {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD
{tex}\angle{/tex}D = 180o - {tex}\angle{/tex}A - {tex}\angle{/tex}B
=180o - 110o = 70o
Since angles made by same chord at any point of circumference are equal so, {tex}\angle ACB=\angle ADB=70^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the figure, O is the centre of the circle. If {tex}\angle ABC = {20^o}{/tex} , then {tex}\angle AOC{/tex} is equal to :
questions.explanation:
Angle made by a chord at the centre is twice the angle made by it on any point of the circumference.
So, {tex}\angle AOC = 2\angle ABC=2\times 20^0=40^0{/tex}

questions.question: In the given figure, a circle is centred at O. The value of x is :
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACO = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAO = 20o (because OA = OC)
{tex}\angle{/tex}OBC = {tex}\angle{/tex}OCB = 35o (because OB = OC)
{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 55o 
x = 2{tex}\angle{/tex}ACB = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 55o = 110o

questions.question: In the given figure, O s the centre of circle, {tex}\angle BAO = {68^o},{/tex} AC is diameter of circle, then measure of {tex}\angle BCO{/tex} is :
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}B = 90o (Angle in a semicircle)
Now, in {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o 
68o + 90o + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180o

questions.question: In the figure, if {tex}\angle{/tex}SPR = 73°, {tex}\angle{/tex}SRP = 42° then {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR is equal to :
questions.explanation: {tex}\angle{/tex}PQR = {tex}\angle{/tex}PSR = 180° - 73° - 42° = 65°

questions.question: In the figure, O is the centre of the circle. If {tex}\angle OPQ = {25^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle ORQ = {20^o}{/tex}, then the measures of {tex}\angle POR{/tex} and {tex}\angle PQR{/tex} are respectively :
questions.explanation:
Here, given
,  
OP = OQ and OR = OQ (Radius of circle)
So, {angles opposite to equal sides are also equal}
Hence,  
PQR = 25o + 20o = 45o 
and PQR = 2  PQR = 2  45o = 90o 
{Angle subtended by same sides on centre is double the angle at opposite vertex}

questions.question: In the figure, O is the centre of the circle with {tex}\angle AOB = {85^o}{/tex} and {tex}\angle AOC = {115^o},{/tex} Then {tex}\angle BAC{/tex} is :
questions.explanation:
{tex}\angle{/tex}BOC = 360o - (85o + 115o) = 160o 
So, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = {tex}\frac{160^\circ}{2}{/tex} = 80o

tests.name: Constructions Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Constructions CBSE Online Test 01. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is possible to construct an angle of _______.
questions.explanation:
With the help of a ruler and compass, it is not possible to construct an angle which is not a multiple of 15o.
As we know, 37.5o is a multiple of 15o. Hence, we can construct an angle of 37.5o with the help of a ruler and compass.

questions.question: It is not possible to construct an angle of ________ with the help of a ruler and a compass.
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is not possible to construct an angle which is not a multiple of 15o and as in these option, 35o is not a multiple of 15o,so we can not construct an angle of 35o.

questions.question: The construction of {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC in which AB = 5 cm, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 75o is not possible when difference of BC and AC is equal to
questions.explanation: The construction of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is not possible as the difference of other two sides of a triangle should be less than its base i.e AB should be greater than BC - AC.

questions.question: The construction of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, given that BC = 5 cm, {tex}\angle B {/tex} = 60o is not possible when the difference of AB and AC is equal to________.
questions.explanation: The construction of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is not possible when difference of the other two sides of a triangle is greater than its base i.e BC < AB - AC.

questions.question: The construction of a triangle ABC, given that BC = 3 cm, {tex}\angle C = {60^0}{/tex} is possible when the difference of AB and AC is equal to ____.
questions.explanation: The construction of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} is possible when difference of other two sides is less than its base i.e BC> AB-AC.

questions.question: The construction of a triangle ABC, given that BC = 6 cm, {tex}\angle B = {45^0}{/tex} is not possible when difference of AB and AC is equal to:
questions.explanation: The construction of {tex}\triangle ABC {/tex} is not possible when the difference of other two sides is greater than its base.

questions.question: In the figure, a {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is constructed when its perimeter and its two base angles are given. In this construction line segment XY is drawn equal to :
questions.explanation: As the perimeter of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC is given so we will construct a line XY of its perimeter i.e AB + BC + CA.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, given that BC = 3 cm, {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 60o is possible when difference of AB and AC is equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and difference of other two sides are given, the difference of other two sides should be less than its base.
As in this case AB - AC < BC, So we can construct this triangle.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} in which AB = 4 cm, {tex}\angle A = {60^0}{/tex} is not possible when difference of BC and AC is equal to ____.
questions.explanation: To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and difference of other two sides are given is not possible when the difference of other two sides is greater than its base.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, given AB = 5.2 cm, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 45o is possible when the sum of AC and BC is equal to ________.
questions.explanation: To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and sum of other two sides are given is possible when the sum of other two sides is greater than its base.

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is possible to construct an angle of ________.
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, we can construct an angle which is a multiple of 15o. Since 7.5o is multiple of 15o, so, we can construct it.

questions.question: Which of the following angles cannot be constructed with the help of a ruler and a compass?
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, we can not construct an angle which is not a multiple of 15o. Since {tex}30\frac{1}{ 2}^\circ {/tex} is not a multiple of 15o, so, we can not construct it.

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is not possible to construct an angle of:
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is not possible to construct an angle which is not a multipe of 15o. Since 40o is not a multiple of 15o, so, we cannot construct it.

questions.question: To construct the perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB, We draw two equal arcs as taking centres A and B with radius _____.
questions.explanation: Because if we take radius less than or equal to {tex}AB\over2{/tex},then the arcs  may not intersect on both the sides.

questions.question: Two or more circles are congruent if they have same ________.
questions.explanation: Two circles are congruent if they are of same size. The size can be measured as the radii, diameter. So, if two circles have same radii or diameter, then those circles are congruent.

questions.question: Two or more circles are concentric if they have same ________.
questions.explanation: Concentric circles are those which have same centres but different radii or diameters.

questions.question: If two circles touches internally then distance between their centers is equal to_________.
questions.explanation:
Consider, two circles with Centre A and B touches internally at point P.
Now distance between their Centre i.e. AB can be given as, AB = AP - BP, where, AP = radius of circle A;  BP = radius of circle B
Thus, we can say that the distance between the centers of two circles touching internally is equal to the difference of their radii.

questions.question: Radius of a circle is ________ to tangent.
questions.explanation:
Consider a circle with Centre O and radius 'r', AB is the tangent on circle O touching the circle at point P. Join OP such that OP becomes the radius of circle O. Now let us take a point Q, other than P, on the tangent AB. Join OQ.  Since Q is a point on tangent AB, other than the point of contact P, so Q will be outside the circle.
Let OQ intersect the circle at R.
Then OQ = OR + RQ
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} OQ > OR
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} OQ > OP (OR = OP = RADIUS)
Thus, OP is the shortest distance among all line segments which is thus perpendicular from O on AB.
Hence, OP is perpendicular to AB.

questions.question: Two radii of same circle are always ________ to each other.
questions.explanation:
The radius of any circle is defined as the distance of any point on the circumference of the circle from the centre of the circle. Since the centre of any circle is only one. 
Thus, any radius will always be drawn from the centre, hence, both radii will meet and inclined at an angle.

questions.question: A quadrilateral can be drawn if the measures of its ________.

options.html: three sides and a diagonal are given
four sides and one diagonal are given
four angles and one side are given

questions.explanation: At first, we will have to draw the diagonal and then by taking endpoints of diagonals as the centre we can draw the arcs corresponding to the length of the four sides given. Therefore, the measures of four sides and one diagonal is necessary for the construction of a quadrilateral.

questions.question: The point in the plane of a triangle which is at equal perpendicular distances from the three sides of the triangle is its __________.
questions.explanation:
As we know that, Incentre is the point of intersection of the angle bisectors of the triangle and it is also the Centre point of the Incircle of the triangle which touches the three sides of the triangle internally.
 
Now, as shown in the figure above, the perpendicular distance of the incentre of the triangle from the sides of the triangle is the radii of the incircle which are always equal.
Hence, the incentre of a triangle is at equal perpendicular distance from three sides of a triangle.

questions.question: The orthocentere of an obtuse triangle lies ________.

options.html: on the smallest side of the triangle
on the greatest side of the triangle

questions.explanation:
This is so because orthocentre of a triangle is the point of intersection of the three altitudes of a triangle and as we know, the three altitudes of an obtuse triangle meet outside the triangle.
Thus, the orthocentre of an obtuse triangle is outside the triangle.

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and compass. It is possible to construct an angle of _________.
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and compass, we can only construct an angle which is a multiple of 15o and as 22.5o is a multiple of 15o, So, we can construct it.

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is not possible to construct an angle of ________.
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, we cannot construct an angle which is not a multiple of 15o and as 70o is not a multiple of 15o, So, we cannot construct it.

questions.question: The construction of a triangle ABC in which AB = 2.5 cm, {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 45o is not possible when difference of BC and AC is equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and difference of other two sides are given, the difference of other two sides must be less than the base of the triangle.
But in this case BC - AC > AB, So, we can not construct it.

questions.question: The construction of  {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, given that BC = 5 cm, {tex}\angle B{/tex} = 45o is not possible when difference of AB and AC is equal to _________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and difference of other two sides are given, the difference of other two sides should be less than its base.
But, here, AB - AC > BC, So we can not construct it.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, given that BC = 4 cm, {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 60o is possible when difference of AB and AC is equal to
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle, whose base, base angle and difference of other two sides are given, the difference of other two sides must be less than its base.
As in this case, AB - AC < BC, so, it is possible to construct it.

questions.question: To construct a right triangle whose base is 12 cm and sum of its hypotenuse and other side is 18 cm. We draw line segment AB of 12 cm. Draw a ray AX making 90o with AB. The next step is:

options.html: Cut a line segment AD of 18 cm on AX
Cut a line segment BD of 18 cm on AB
Cut a line segment AD of 18 cm on AB
Cut a line segment BD of 18 cm

questions.explanation: We will cut the line segment of length equal to the hypotenus of the triangle i.e. 18 cm . Now the line segment AD thus formed will be the hypotenus of the triangle.

questions.question:
By following these steps of construction,

  1. Draw a line segment PQ = 11 cm
  2. At P construct an angle of 60o and at Q, an angle of 45o,
  3. Bisect these angles. Let the bisectors of these angles intersect at a point A.
  4. Draw perpendicular bisectors DE of AP to intersect PQ at B and FG of AQ to intersect PQ at C.
  5. Join AB and AC. {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} has been obtained.

Which of the following option is correct for this {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}?
questions.explanation: We can say that AB + BC + CA = 11cm, because the steps given above are the steps to construct a triangle when perimeter of a triangle and its two angles are given.

questions.question: In the construction of the bisector of a given angle, as shown in the figure below {tex}\triangle BEF \cong \triangle BDF{/tex} by which congruence criterion?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle BEF \;and \;\triangle BDF{/tex},
BE = BD (lines formed by same arc)
BF = BF (common)
EF = DF ( sides from same arc)
hence, {tex}\Rightarrow \triangle BEF \cong\triangle BDF{/tex} (BY SSS)

questions.question: In the construction of the perpendicular bisector of a given line segment, as shown in the figure below {tex}\triangle{/tex}PBM {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle{/tex}PMB by which congruence criterion?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}PMA and {tex}\triangle{/tex}PMB,
PM = PM ( common )
{tex}\angle{/tex}PMA = {tex}\angle{/tex}PMB ( each 90°)
MA = MB (perpendicular bisector PM bisects the side AB in two equal parts)
{tex}\implies{/tex}{tex}\triangle PMA{/tex} {tex}\cong{/tex} {tex}\triangle PMB{/tex} ( By SAS criteria)

questions.question: On a ray AB with initial point A, taking A as centre and some radius, draw an arc of a circle, which intersects AB, say at a point D. Taking D as centre and with the same radius as before, draw an arc intersecting the previously drawn arc, say at a point E. Draw the ray AC passing through E. Then, the measure of {tex}\angle CAB{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation: 60o

questions.question: If a 60o angle is bisected and then each new angle is bisected, what will be measure of each angle that is constructed?
questions.explanation:
If a 60o angle is bisected, then two angles each  of {tex}{{60^0}\over2} = {30^0}{/tex} are formed. This two angles are further bisected, thus forming four angles each of {tex}{{30^0}\over 2} = {15^0} {/tex}.
Hence the four angles formed wil be of 15o each.

questions.question: In the following below PR is the perpendicular bisector of a line segment AB = 16 cm, then which of the following is true?
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}POA and {tex}\triangle{/tex}POB,
PO = PO ( COMMON)
{tex}\angle{/tex}POA = {tex}\angle{/tex}POB ( EACH 90°)
OA = OB ( Perpendicular bisector PR divides the line AB into two equal parts OA and OB)
{tex}\implies \triangle POA \cong \triangle POB{/tex} ( By SAS)
Hence, PA = PB (By C.P.C.T)

questions.question: An external bisector of an angle measuring 70o will divide the angle into two angles measuring
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\angle ABC{/tex} = 70o, side BC is extended to point N  and MB be the external bisector of {tex}\angle ABC{/tex}.
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle ABN {/tex} = 180o - 70o = 110o 
Now, MB is the external bisector of {tex}\angle ABC{/tex}, which divides {tex}\angle ABN{/tex} in two equal parts.
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle ABM = \angle MBN = {{110^0}\over 2}{/tex}= 55o

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, which of the following information is needed to construct it if it is known that measure of {tex}\angle B{/tex} = 60o and BC = 6 cm :
questions.explanation: To construct a triangle, we need measurements of its base, base angle and sum or difference of other two sides i.e to construct a {tex}\triangle ABC {/tex}, we need BC, {tex}\angle B{/tex} and AB - AC or AB + AC.

questions.question: Which of these triangles are possible to construct by knowing only its altitude?
questions.explanation:
As we know that the the altitude of an equilateral triangle is given as {tex}{\sqrt {3} \over 4} {(side)^2}{/tex}. 
If the altitude of equilateral triangle is given then we can determine its side by using above formula and further construct it.

questions.question: To construct a {tex}\triangle {/tex} ABC in which BC = 10 cm and {tex}\angle B{/tex} = 60o and AB + AC = 14 cm, then the length of BD used for construction ________.
questions.explanation: To construct a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} whose base BC = 10cm, base angle {tex}\angle B{/tex} = 60o and sum of other two sides i.e. AB + AC = 14 cms are given, we draw a ray of angle 60o from initial point of base i.e. B and draw an arc of length equal to the sum of other two sides i.e. 14 cm. The line segment thus formed is named as BD which is of length 14 cm.

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and a compass it is not possible to construct an angle of ________.
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, we can not construct an angle which is not a multiple of 15o and as we know 50o is not a multiple of 15o, so, we can't construct it.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, given that BC = 3 cm, {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 30o is possible when sum of AB and AC is equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, whose, base BC = 3 cm, base angle {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 30o and sum of other two sides i.e. AB + AC is given, the sum of other two sides should be more than its base i.e. AB + AC should be greater than BC. 
As 3.2 > 3, so, we can construct the {tex}\triangle ABC {/tex}.

questions.question: In the following figure, AD = AC + BC. Find the relation between {tex}\angle{/tex}1 and {tex}\angle{/tex}2:
questions.explanation:
As,  AD = AC + BC (Given) ...(i)
Also, AD = AC + CD (From figure) ...(ii)
From eq. (i) and (ii), we get,  BC = CD
Now in {tex}\triangle{/tex}DCB,
BC = CD (Proved above)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}1 = {tex}\angle{/tex}2 (Equal angles of eaual sides are equal)

questions.question: In figure, LM is an arc of a circle having radius = r. If AL = AM = AN = LM = r And {tex}LN = \frac 1 2LM.{/tex} Then {tex}\angle CAB = {/tex}
questions.explanation: By the rules of construction, we came to know that the angle so formed by arc LM is of 60o and it is given that LN = {tex}\frac {1} {2}{/tex} LM, such that we can say that line CA is the bisector of angle so formed by arc LM. Therefore {tex}\angle C A B=\frac{60^{\circ}}{2}=30^0{/tex}.

questions.question: In figure, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 90°. {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD and {tex}\angle{/tex}DAE  = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAE. Here, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE = _______.
questions.explanation:
It is Given that, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAC = 90° Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAD = {tex} {\angle BAC\over 2} = {{90^0}\over2}{/tex} = 45° ,
and {tex}\angle{/tex}DAE = {tex}\angle{/tex}CAE =  {tex}{\angle DAC\over2}={{45^0}\over2}={22{1\over2}^0}{/tex},
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE = {tex}\angle{/tex}BAD + {tex}\angle{/tex}DAE = 45° + {tex} {22{1\over2}^0}={67{1\over2}^0}{/tex}

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is not possible to construct an angle of _________.
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is not possible to construct an angle which is not a multiple of 15o and as here 80o is not a multiple of 15o, so, we can not construct it.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC, given that BC = 3 cm, {tex}\angle C{/tex} = 60o, is possible when difference of AB and AC is equal to ________.
questions.explanation: To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and difference of other two sides are given, then in that case, the difference of other two sides should be less than the base. i.e.. AB - AC should be less than BC.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: It is not possible to construct a triangle whose sides are ________.
questions.explanation: To construct a triangle with given three sides is only possible when sum of the two sides is greater than the third side. But in this case, 3 cm + 3 cm is not greater than the third side i.e. 6 cm, so we can not construct it.

questions.question: It is not possible to construct a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} with BC = 5 cm, {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 75o and AB + AC equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and sum of other two sides are given, the sum of other two sides should be more than its base.
But in this case, AB + AC <  BC, so it is impossible to construct the {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, when AB + AC = 4.5 cm.

questions.question: With the help of a ruler and a compass, it is possible to construct an angle of:
questions.explanation: With the help of a ruler and a compass, we can construct an angle which is the multiple of 15o and as here, 37.5o is a multiple of 15o, so, we can construct it.

questions.question: Using a ruler and a compass, it is not possible to construct an angle of ________.
questions.explanation: Using a ruler and a compass, we cannot construct an angle which is not a multiple of 15o and as we know, {tex}{32\frac{1}{2}^o}{/tex} is not a multiple of 15o, so, we can not construct it.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} with AB = 4 cm and {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 60o is not possible when difference of BC and AC is equal to_____.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and difference of other two sides are given, the difference of other two sides must be less than its base.
But in this case, BC - AC > AB, so, we cannot construct it.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} with AB = 6 cm and {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 60o is not possible when sum of BC and CA is equal to _________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose base, base angle and sum of other two sides are given, the sum of other two sides should be more than its base.
But here, BC + CA < AB, so, we cannot construct it.

questions.question: It is possible to construct a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} whose sides are ________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose three sides are given, the sum of two sides should always be greater than the third side.
As here, (3 + 4) > 5; (3 + 5) > 4; (4 + 5) > 3
So, we can construct this triangle.

questions.question: The sides BC, CA and AB of {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC are produced in order to form exterior angles {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE and {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF. {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD + {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE + {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF is_____.
questions.explanation:
Let {tex}\angle{/tex}ACD, {tex}\angle{/tex}BAE, {tex}\angle{/tex}CBF be {tex}\angle{/tex}a, {tex}\angle{/tex}b, {tex}\angle{/tex}c respectively.
As, the sum of angles in a triangle is 180° i.e. x + y + z = 180°
From the figure, we have,
{tex}\angle{/tex}a + x = 180° [LINEAR PAIR],
{tex}\angle{/tex}b + y = 180° [LINEAR PAIR],
{tex}\angle{/tex}c + z = 180° [LINEAR PAIR]
Adding the above three equations, we get,
{tex}\angle{/tex}a + x + {tex}\angle{/tex}b + y + {tex}\angle{/tex}c + z = 180° + 180° + 180°
({tex}\angle{/tex}a + {tex}\angle{/tex}b + {tex}\angle{/tex}c) + (x + y + z) = 540°
({tex}\angle{/tex}a + {tex}\angle{/tex}b + {tex}\angle{/tex}c) = 180° = 540°
{tex}\angle{/tex}a + {tex}\angle{/tex}b + {tex}\angle{/tex}c = 360°

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, if {tex}\angle{/tex}2 = 55° and {tex}\angle{/tex}5 = 55°, the lines m and n are
questions.explanation: For the parallel lines, the corresponding or alternate angles should be equal, but here {tex}\angle{/tex}2 and {tex}\angle{/tex}5 are neither corresponding nor alternate so, the lines m and n cannot be parallel.

questions.question: In the adjoining figure, the values of {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}E + {tex}\angle{/tex}F is______.
questions.explanation:
In the given figure, ABC and DEF are two triangles .
As we know, som of angles of a triangle are 180°.
i.e. In {tex}\triangle {/tex}ABC, {tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C = 180°,
In {tex}\triangle {/tex}DEF, {tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}E + {tex}\angle{/tex}F = 180°,
Adding above two equations ,we get,
{tex}\angle{/tex}A + {tex}\angle{/tex}B + {tex}\angle{/tex}C + {tex}\angle{/tex}D + {tex}\angle{/tex}E + {tex}\angle{/tex}F = 360°

questions.question: The {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC and {tex}\angle{/tex}BDC are right angles. If AD = 8 cm and DC = 32 cm the length of BD is________.
questions.explanation:
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABD , (AB)2 = (BD)2 + (AD)2 ...(i)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}BDC, (BC)2 = (BD)2 + (DC)2 ...(ii)
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, (AB)2 + (BC)2 = (AC)2
Putting eq. (i) and (ii) in above eq., we get,
2(BD)2 + (AD)2 + (DC)2 = (AC)2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (BD)2 = 256 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BD = 16cm

questions.question: If D is a mid point of the hypotenuse AC of a right {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC, BD is equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
Let us produce BD to E such that BD = DE. Join EC such that EC is parallel and equal  to AB and {tex}\angle {/tex}ECB = 90o.
In {tex}\triangle{/tex}ABC and {tex}\triangle{/tex}ECB 
AB = EC, 
 {tex}\angle{/tex}ABC = {tex}\angle{/tex}ECB  (each 90o)
BC = BC (common)
{tex}\therefore\;\triangle ABC\cong\triangle ECB{/tex} ( BY SAS)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AC = BE (by C.P.C.T)
{tex}\Rightarrow\;\frac{1}{2}AC = \frac{1}{2} BE{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}BD=\frac{1}{2}AC{/tex}

questions.question: In {tex}\triangle {/tex} ABC if {tex}\angle B = \angle C{/tex} = 30o, which of the following is the longest side?
questions.explanation:
As, {tex}\angle B=\angle C={30^0}{/tex} (Given)
Therefore, AB = AC (opposite sides of two equal angles are equal)
Since two sides are equal, and the triangle is an obtuse angle isosceles triangle, therefore, the base BC will be the longest side of the triangle.

questions.question: In a {tex}\triangle {/tex} ABC if {tex}\angle{/tex}A = 50o and {tex}\angle{/tex}B = 70o then the shortest and the largest sides of the triangles are ________.
questions.explanation:
As we know, the shortest side is equal to the shortest angle and the longest side is equal to the longest angle.
Thus, {tex}\angle A{/tex} is shorter here, therefore, BC will be the shortest side and {tex}\angle B{/tex} is longer thus AC will be the longest side.

questions.question: What is the supplementary angle of 108o?
questions.explanation:
Supplementary angles are those angles whose sum are 180o. 
So,  180o + xo = 180o
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} xo = 180o - 108o = 72o
Thus the supplementary of 108o is 72o.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A rotating ray after making a complete rotation coincides with its initial position. The angle thus formed is ________.
questions.explanation: If a rotating ray after making a complete rotation coincides with the initial position, then, the angle thus formed will be of {tex}{360^0}{/tex} and this angle is known as Complete angle.

questions.question: If two lines are parallel, the perpendicular distance between them ________.
questions.explanation:
Parallel lines are those lines which do not intersect each other or meet each other in a plane. 
Thus to keep two lines parallel, the perpendicular distance between them is to be kept constant.

questions.question: How many angles are formed by a transversal with a pair of lines?
questions.explanation: This can easily be done by counting the number of angles given in the figure.

questions.question: How many pairs of corresponding angles are formed when a transversal intersects a pair of lines?
questions.explanation:
Corresponding angles are pair of angles that:

  • have distinct vertex points
  • lie on same side of transversal.
  • one angle is interior and other exterior.

When a transversal intersects a pair of lines then 4 such pairs are formed.

questions.question: How many pairs of alternate angles are formed when a transversal intersects a pair of lines?
questions.explanation:
Let a line L intersects two lines AB and BC  thus 8 angles will be formed by the transversal.
{tex}\angle 3=\angle 6{/tex} (alternate angles)
{tex}\angle 4=\angle5{/tex} (alternate angles)
Hence two pairs of alternate angle are formed.

questions.question: Instruments used to draw a pair of parallel lines are ________.
questions.explanation:
A ruler and a set square can be used to draw parallel lines as described below:
STEP 1: Position an edge of the set square against the ruler and draw a line along with one of the other edges.
STEP 2: Slide the set square into a new position while keeping the ruler fixed at the same position.
STEP 3: Draw the line along the same edge as in Step 1.
The two lines thus drawn are parallel to each other.

questions.question: Example for a pair of parallel lines is ________.
questions.explanation: The stumps of a cricket match are so arranged that the perpendicular distance between the stumps is equal at every point. Hence, the stumps are parallel lines.

questions.question: Among the following, find the set of measures which can form triangle.
questions.explanation: As the sum of the interior angles of a triangle should be 180o and in this case 65o + 85o + 30o = 180o so this set of measures form a triangle.

questions.question: A triangle divides the plane into _________ parts.
questions.explanation: As the triangle have 3 sides, so it divide the plane into three parts.

questions.question: Can 90o, 90o and 10o form a triangle?
questions.explanation: As we know that, the sum of the interior angles of a triangle should be always equal to 180o but here 90o + 90o + 10o = 190o > 180o, so, we cannot construct this triangle.

questions.question: Can 7 cm, 5 cm and 3 cm form a triangle?
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose three sides are given, the sum of the two sides must always be greater than the third side. As in this case,
(7 + 5) > 3; (3 + 5) > 7; (3 + 7) > 5;
So, we can construct this triangle.

questions.question: If lines AB, AC, AD and AE are parallel to a line m, then ________.

options.html: A, B, C, D, E are collinear points
AB & AC are perpendicular and AD & AE are parallel
AB & AC are parallel and AD & AE are perpendicular
A, B, C, D, E are non-collinear points

questions.explanation: If lines AB, AC, AD, AE are parallel to a line m, then either all the lines should be parallel to each other or the points should be collinear. Since all the lines originate from point A thus they cannot be parallel, hence, points A, B, C, D, E are collinear.

questions.question: An angle greater than 180o but less than 360o is called ________.
questions.explanation: An angle whose measure is greater than 180o but less than 360o is called Reflex Angle.

questions.question: If two parallel lines are intersected by a transversal, the bisectors of the interior angles form a ________.
questions.explanation:
Let, Two parallel lines AB and CD are intersected by a transversal line L at P and R respectively.
PQ, RQ, RS and PS are bisectors of {tex}\angle{/tex}APR, {tex}\angle{/tex}PRC, {tex}\angle{/tex}PRD, {tex}\angle{/tex}BPR respectively.
Since, {tex}AB \parallel CD{/tex} and L is a transversal
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle APR=\angle PRD{/tex} (alternate interior angles)
{tex}\Rightarrow {1\over2}\angle APR={1\over2}\angle PRD {/tex}  
{tex}\Rightarrow \angle QPR= \angle PRS{/tex} 
But these are alternate interior angles.
{tex}\therefore{/tex} PQ || RS
Similarly, QR || PS
{tex}\therefore{/tex} PQRS is a parallelogram.
Also, {tex}\angle{/tex}APR + {tex}\angle{/tex}BPR= 180o (Linear pair )
{tex}\Rightarrow {1\over2} \angle APR + {1\over2}\angle BPR{/tex} = 90o 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}QPR + {tex}\angle{/tex}SPR = 90o  {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\angle{/tex}QPS = 90o
Hence, PQRS is a rectangle.

questions.question: The construction of a {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex}, given that BC = 6 cm, {tex}\angle B = {45^0}{/tex} is not possible when the difference of AB and AC is equal to ________.
questions.explanation:
To construct a triangle whose base, base angle, and difference of the other two sides are given, the difference of the other two sides should be less than its base.
But here, AB - AC > BC, So we cannot construct it.

questions.question: The area of a triangle with base 8 cm and height 10 cm is
questions.explanation:
Area = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= {tex}{1 \over 2} \times 8 \times 10{/tex} 
= 40 cm2

questions.question: The area of a triangle is 150 cm2 and its sides are in the ratio 3 : 4 : 5. What is its perimeter?
A triangle ABC in which AB = AC = 4 cm and {tex}\angle A{/tex} = 90o, has an area of
questions.explanation:
According to question, in given right-angled triangle AB and AC are Base and Perpendicular respectively.
Therefore,
Area = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times 4 \times 4{/tex} 
= 8 cm2

questions.question: If the side of an equilateral triangle is 4 cm, then its area is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{/tex}a2
= {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4} \times 4 \times 4{/tex}
= {tex}4\sqrt 3 {/tex} cm2

questions.question: If the perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 24 m, then its area is
questions.explanation:
Side = {tex}{{24} \over 3}{/tex} = 8m
Area = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4} \times 8 \times 8{/tex} 
= {tex}16\sqrt 3 {/tex} m2

questions.question: The length of each side of an equilateral triangle having an area of {tex}25\sqrt 3 {/tex} cm2 is
questions.explanation:
Area = {tex}25\sqrt 3 {/tex} sq. cm
{tex}25\sqrt 3 {/tex} = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{/tex}a2  
a2 =  {tex}{{25 \times 4 \times \sqrt 3 } \over {\sqrt 3 }}{/tex} = 100
Side = 10 cm

questions.question: If the area of an equilateral triangle is {tex}36\sqrt 3 \;c{m^2}{/tex} , then the perimeter of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
{tex}36\sqrt 3 {/tex} = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{/tex}a2
a2  = {tex}{{36\sqrt 3 \times 4} \over {\sqrt 3 }}{/tex}= 144
a = 12 cm
Perimeter = {tex}3 \times 12{/tex} = 36 cm

questions.question: The edges of a triangular board are 6 cm, 8 cm and 10 cm. The cost of painting it at the rate of 70 paise per cm2 is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{6 + 8 + 10} \over 2} = 12{/tex} cm
A = {tex}\sqrt {12\left( {12 - 8} \right)\left( {12 - 10} \right)\left( {12 - 6} \right)} {/tex} 
= 24 cm2
cost = 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.70 = ₹16.80

questions.question: The perimeter of a rhombus is 20 cm. If one of its diagonals is 6 cm, then its area is
questions.explanation:
Side = {tex}{{20} \over 4} {/tex} = 5 cm
Half diagnoal = {tex}\sqrt {{5^2} - {3^2}} {/tex} = 4 cm
Diagnoal = 4 {tex} \times{/tex} 2 = 8 cm
Area = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times 6 \times 8 = 24{/tex}cm2

questions.question: The area of a rhombus is 96 cm2. If one of its diagonals is 16 cm, then the length of its side is
questions.explanation:
Area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonal
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 96 = {tex}{1 \over 2}\left( {16 \times {d_2}} \right){/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{d_2} = {{96 \times 2} \over {16}}{/tex}= 12 cm
Since diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at a right angle.
Therefore, the side of the rhombus is the hypotenuse of a triangle.
Side = {tex}\sqrt {{8^2} + {6^2}}{/tex} = 10 cm

questions.question: If the area of an isosceles right triangle is 8 cm2, what is the perimeter of the triangle?
questions.explanation:
Let each of the two equal sides of an isosceles right triangle be a cm
Then, third side = {tex}a \sqrt{2}{/tex}m
Area of {tex}\Delta=\frac{1}{2} \times 2 \times 2{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 8=\frac{a^{2}}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a2 = 16
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a = 4 cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Perimeter
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} a + a + a {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} = 4 + 4 + 4 {tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} = 8 + 4{tex}\sqrt{2}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: The area of an isosceles triangle having base 24 cm and length of one of the equal sides 20 cm is
questions.explanation:
s =  {tex}\frac{(24+20+20)}{2}{/tex} = 32 cm 
Area = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {32\left( {32 - 24} \right)\left( {32 - 20} \right)\left( {32 - 20} \right)} {/tex} 
= 192 sq. cm

questions.question: The perimeter of an isosceles triangle is 32 cm. the ratio of the equal side to its base is 3 : 2. Then area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Let each of the equal sides of given triangle be x cm. Then the third side is (32 - 2x) cm
According to quesiton, {tex}{x \over {32 - 2x}} = {3 \over 2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 96 - 6x
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8x = 96
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 12 cm
Therefore, the sides are 12 cm, 12 cm and 8 cm
Area = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {16\left( {16 - 12} \right)\left( {16 - 12} \right)\left( {16 - 8} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {16 \times 4 \times 4 \times 8} {/tex} 
= {tex}32\sqrt 2 {/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: If the perimeter and base of an isosceles triangle are 11 cm and 5 cm respectively, then its area is
questions.explanation:
Let each of the equal sides be x cm. Then
x + x + 5 = 11
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x = 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3 cm
s = {tex}\frac{3 + 3 + 5} {2} = \frac{11}{2}{/tex} cm
Area = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{\frac{11}{2}\left(\frac{11}{2}-3\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-3\right)\left(\frac{11}{2}-5\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{\frac{11}{2} \times \frac{5}{2} \times \frac{5}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{5}{4}\sqrt {11} {/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: The area of a regular hexagon of side 4 cm is
questions.explanation:
Area of regular hexagon = {tex}{{3\sqrt 3 } \over 2}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{{3\sqrt 3 } \over 2} \times 4 \times 4{/tex} 
= {tex}24\sqrt 3 {/tex} sq. cm
