questions.question: If the base and the corresponding altitude of a parallelogram are 60 cm and 24 cm respectively, then the area of the parallelogram is
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} corresponding height
= 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 = 1440 sq. cm

questions.question: The area of a triangle whose sides are 12 cm, 16 cm and 20 cm is
questions.explanation:
Here, s = {tex}{{12 + 16 + 20} \over 2} = 24{/tex} cm

Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {24\left( {24 - 12} \right)\left( {24 - 16} \right)\left( {24 - 20} \right)} {/tex}

= 96 sq. cm

questions.question: The perimeter and area of a triangle whose sides are of lengths 3 cm, 4 cm and 5 cm respectively are
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of triangle = 3 + 4 + 5 = 12 cm
Now, s = {tex}{{3 + 4 + 5} \over 2}{/tex}= 6 cm
Area = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {6\left( {6 - 3} \right)\left( {6 - 4} \right)\left( {6 - 5} \right)}{/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{6 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1}{/tex}
= 6 sq cm

questions.question: The area of a triangle whose sides are 15 cm, 8 cm and 19 cm is
questions.explanation:
{tex}{{15 + 8 + 19} \over 2} {/tex} = 21 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {21\left( {21 - 15} \right)\left( {21 - 8} \right)\left( {21 - 19} \right)} {/tex} = {tex}\sqrt{21 \times 6 \times 13 \times 2}{/tex} 
= {tex}6\sqrt {91} {/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: Each of the equal sides of an isosceles triangle is 2 cm greater than its height. If the base of the triangle is 12 cm, then its area is
questions.explanation:
Let the height of the isosceles triangle be x cm.
Then length of equal side = (x + 2) cm
Since altitude of isosceles triangle bisects the base. Then in a right angled triangle,
(x + 2)2 = x2 + 62
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 + 4x = 36 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 8 cm
Now, area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 48 sq.cm

questions.question: If the height of a parallelogram having 500 cm2 area is 20 cm, then its base is of length
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 500 = Base {tex}\times{/tex} 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = 25 cm

questions.question: Given the product of diagonals of a rhombus ABCD is 2500 cm2, its area is
questions.explanation:
Area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonals
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2500
= 1250 sq. cm

questions.question: The area of a right-angled triangle if the radius of its circumcircle is 3 cm and altitude drawn to the hypotenuse is 2 cm. is
questions.explanation:
Since in a right-angled triangle, the circumcentre is the mid-point of the hypotenuse, then
Hypotenuse = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 6 cm
Now, Area of right-angled triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Altitude
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 6 sq. cm

questions.question: Area of an isosceles triangle ABC with AB = a = AC and BC = b is
questions.explanation:
Here s = {tex}\frac{a + a + b}{2} = \frac{2a + b} {2}{/tex} 
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{\frac{2 a+b}{2}\left(\frac{2 a+b}{2}-a\right)\left(\frac{2 a+b}{2}-a\right)\left(\frac{2 a+b}{2}-b\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt{\frac{2 a+b}{2}\left(\frac{b}{2}\right)\left(\frac{b}{2}\right)\left(\frac{2 a-b}{2}\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{b}{4}\sqrt {4{a^2} - {b^2}} {/tex}

questions.question: The area of quadrilateral ABCD whose diagonals are perpendicular and of lengths 12 cm, 8 cm is
questions.explanation:
Since in the quadrilateral ABCD, the diagonals are perpendicular.
Area of quadrilateral = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonals
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 48 sq. cm

questions.question: An isosceles right triangle has area 8 cm2. The length of its hypotenuse is
questions.explanation:
Area of isosceles triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times {/tex} Base {tex}\times {/tex} Height
Since in an isosceles triangle, Base and Height are equal.
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times {/tex} Base {tex}\times {/tex} Base
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = Height = 4 cm
Hypotenuse = {tex}\sqrt {{4^2} + {4^2}} {/tex} = {tex}\sqrt {32} {/tex} cm

questions.question: The perimeter of an equilateral triangle is 60 m. The area is
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of equilateral triangle = 60 m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 {tex}\times{/tex} side = 60 m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} side = 20 m
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex}
= {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}20 \times 20{/tex}
= {tex}100\sqrt 3 {/tex} sq.m

questions.question: The area of an equilateral triangle with side {tex}2\sqrt 3 {/tex} cm is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex}

= {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4} \times 2\sqrt 3 \times 2\sqrt 3 {/tex}

= {tex}3\sqrt 3 {/tex}

= 3 x 1.732 = 5.196 sq. cm

questions.question: The length of each side of an equilateral triangle having an area of {tex}9\sqrt 3 {/tex} {tex}c{m^2}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}9\sqrt 3 {/tex} sq cm
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2} = 9\sqrt 3 {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Slide)2 = 36
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 6 cm

questions.question: If the area of an equilateral triangle is {tex}16\sqrt 3 \;c{m^2}{/tex}, then the perimeter of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2} = 16\sqrt 3 {/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2} = 64{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 8 cm
Perimeter of equilateral triangle = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} side
= 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 24 cm

tests.name: Herons Formula Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Herons Formula CBSE Online Test 03. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 35 cm, 54 cm and 61 cm respectively, and its area is {tex}420\sqrt 5 {/tex} cm2. The length of its longest altitude is
questions.explanation:
Since longest altitude is drawn opposite to the shortest side in a triangle.
Area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}420\sqrt 5 = {1 \over 2} \times 35{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height = {tex}{{420\sqrt 5 \times 2} \over {35}} = 24\sqrt 5 {/tex} cm

questions.question: The difference of semi-perimeter and the sides of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} are 8, 7 and 5 cm respectively. Its semi-perimeter ‘s’ is
questions.explanation:
Given: s - a = 8 cm, s - b = 7 cm and s - c = 5 cm
Adding all equations,
s - a + s - b + s - c = 8 + 7 + 5
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3s - (a + b + c) = 20 [s ={tex}\frac{a+b+c}{2}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3s - 2s = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s = 20 cm

questions.question: The perimeter of a rhombus is 20 cm. One of its diagonals is 8 cm. Then area of the rhombus is
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of Rhombus = 20 cm

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} side = 20 cm {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 5 cm
Since diagonals of rhombus bisect each other at perpendicular, therefore
OC = {tex}{8 \over 2}{/tex} = 4 cm
In triangle OBC,
OB = {tex}\sqrt {{5^2} - {4^2}} = \sqrt {25 - 16} {/tex} = 3 cm
AC = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 6 cm
Area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonal
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 24 sq cm

questions.question: If one side and one diagonal of a rhombus and 20 m and 24 m, then its area =
questions.explanation:
Since diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angle.

OB = {tex}{{24} \over 2}{/tex}= 12 m
In triangle OBC, OC = {tex}\sqrt {{{20}^2} - {{12}^2}} = \sqrt {400 - 144} {/tex} = 16 m
AC = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 16 = 32 m
Area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonals
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 32
= 384 sq. m

questions.question: The product of difference of semi-perimeter & respective sides of {tex} \triangle ABC{/tex} are given as 13200 {tex}{m^2}{/tex}.The area of {tex}\triangle ABC{/tex} , if its semi-perimeter is 132 m, is given by
questions.explanation:
Given: {tex}\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right) = 13200{/tex} m and s = 132 m

Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {13200 \times 132} {/tex}

= 1320 sq. m

questions.question: The area of an isosceles right angled triangle of equal side 30 cm, is given as
questions.explanation:
Area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 30 = 450 sq. cm

questions.question: The area and length of one diagonal of a rhombus are given as 200 cm2 and 10 cm respectively. The length of other diagonal is
questions.explanation:
Area of rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonal
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 200 = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} d2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} d2 = {tex}{{200 \times 2} \over {10}}{/tex}= 40 cm

questions.question: The area of one triangular part of a rhombus ABCD is given as 125 cm2. The area of rhombus ABCD is
questions.explanation:
Since diagonals of a rhombus divide it into 4 triangles of equal area. Therefore,
Area of rhombus = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} Area of triangle
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 125 = 500 sq. cm

questions.question: Each side of an equilateral triangle measures 10 cm. Then the area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral trianlge = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{{1.732} \over 4} \times 10 \times 10{/tex} 
= 43.3 sq. cm

questions.question: The area of a parallelogram whose base is 32 m and the corresponding altitude is 6 m is
questions.explanation:
Area of parallelogram = Base {tex}\times{/tex} Corresponding altitude
= 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 192 sq. m

questions.question: The diagonal of a rhombus are 24 cm and 10 cm. Then its perimeter is
questions.explanation:
Since diagonals of a rhombus bisect each other at right angle.

OB = {tex}{{24} \over 2}{/tex}= 12 cm and OC = {tex}{{10} \over 2}{/tex}= 5 cm
In triangle OBC,
BC = {tex}\sqrt {{{12}^2} + {5^2}} = \sqrt {144 + 25} {/tex} = 13 cm
Perimeter of rhombus = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} side = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 13 = 52 cm

questions.question: The adjacent sides of a parallelogram are 20 cm and 15 cm in length. Then the ratio between the corresponding altitudes is
questions.explanation:
Since the adjacent sides and corresponding altitudes of a parallelogram are in proportion.
Therefore, {tex}{{15} \over {20}} = {3 \over 4}{/tex} 
Thus, the required ratio is 3 : 4.

questions.question: In a four-sided field, the length of the longer diagonal is 128 m. The lengths of the perpendiculars from the opposite vertices upon this diagonal are 22.7 m and 17.3 m. Then area of the field is
questions.explanation:
According to the question,
Area of the field = {tex}{1 \over 2} \times 128 \times 17.3 + {1 \over 2} \times 128 \times 22.7{/tex} 
= {tex}{1 \over 2} \times 128\left( {17.3 + 22.7} \right){/tex} 
= 2560 sq. m

questions.question: The area of a quadrilateral whose diagonals measure 48 m and 32 m respectively and bisect each other at right angles is
questions.explanation:
According to the question,
Area of given quadrilateral = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonals
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 48 {tex}\times{/tex} 32 
= 768 sq. m

questions.question: The diagonals of a rhombus measure 4 cm and 6 cm respectively. Its area in sq. cm is
questions.explanation:
Area of Rhombus = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Product of diagonals
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 
= 12 sq. cm

questions.question: The cost of turfing a triangular field at the rate of Rs. 45 per 100 m2 is Rs. 900. If the double the base of the triangle is 5 times its height, then its height is
questions.explanation:
Cost of turfing a triangular field at the rate of Rs.45 per 100 = Rs.900
{tex}{{{\rm{Area }} \times 45} \over {100}} = 900{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area = 2000 sq. m
According to question,
2 {tex}\times{/tex} Base = 5 {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = {tex}{{{\rm{Height }} \times 5} \over 2}{/tex}
Area of triangle = 2000 sq. m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height = 2000
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{{{\rm{Height }} \times 5} \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Height = 2000
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Height)2 = 1600
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height = 40 m

questions.question: The length of each side of an equilateral triangle of area {tex}4 \sqrt{3}{/tex} cm2, is
questions.explanation:
If side of an equilateral triangle is 'a', then its
Area = {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^{2}{/tex} 
Now {tex}\frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^{2}{/tex} = 4{tex}\sqrt{3}{/tex}
a2 = 4​​​​2
a = 4cm

questions.question: The area of an isosceles triangle having base 2 cm and the length of one of the equal sides 4 cm, is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}\frac{4 + 4 + 2}{2}{/tex}= 5 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {5\left( {5 - 4} \right)\left( {5 - 4} \right)\left( {5 - 2} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {5 \times 1 \times 1 \times 3} {/tex}=
= {tex}\sqrt {15} {/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: The edges of a triangular board are 6 cm, 8 cm and 10 cm. The cost of painting it at the rate of 9 paise per {tex}\;c{m^2}{/tex} is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{6 + 8 + 10} \over 2}{/tex}= 12 cm

Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {12\left( {12 - 6} \right)\left( {12 - 8} \right)\left( {12 - 10} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {12 \times 6 \times 4 \times 2} {/tex}

= 24 sq. cm

Therefore, the cost of painting it at the rate of Rs.0.09 per sq. cm = 24 x 0.09 = Rs.2.16

questions.question: The area of an equilateral triangle of side a is {tex}4\sqrt 3 {/tex} cm2. Its height is given by
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}\frac{\sqrt 3 } {4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4}(\text { Side })^2=4 \sqrt{3}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Side)2 = 42  
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 4 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}4\sqrt 3 {/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} Height 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Height = {tex}2\sqrt 3 {/tex} cm

questions.question: If side of a scalene {tex}\triangle {/tex} is doubled then area would be increased by
questions.explanation:
Area of triangle with sides a, b, c (A) = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}
New sides are 2a, 2b and 2c
Then {tex}s' = {{2a + 2b + 2c} \over 2} = a + b + c{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} s' = 2s .....(i)
New area = {tex}\sqrt {s'\left( {s' - 2a} \right)\left( {s' - 2b} \right)\left( {s' - 2c} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {2s\left( {2s - 2a} \right)\left( {2s - 2b} \right)\left( {2s - 2c} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}4\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}
= 4A
Increased area = 4A - A = 3A
% of increased area = {tex}{{3{\rm{A}}} \over {\rm{A}}} \times 100{/tex} = 300%

questions.question: If each side of a {tex}\triangle {/tex} is halved then its perimeter will be decreased by
questions.explanation:
Perimeter of triangle with sides a, b and c is P = a + b + c. .....(i)
New sides are {tex}{a \over 2},{b \over 2},{c \over 2}{/tex}
New perimeter = {tex}{{a + b + c} \over 2} = {{\rm{P}} \over 2}{/tex}. (From eq.(i))
Decreased perimeter = P - {tex}{{\rm{P}} \over 2}{/tex}= {tex}{{\rm{P}} \over 2}{/tex}
% of decreased perimeter = {tex}{{{\rm{P/2}}} \over {\rm{P}}} \times 100{/tex} = 50%

questions.question: Length of perpendicular drawn on smallest side of scalene triangle is
questions.explanation: Length of the perpendicular drawn on the smallest side of the scalene triangle is largest.

questions.question: Length of perpendicular drawn on longest side of a scale {tex}\triangle {/tex} is
questions.explanation: Length of the perpendicular drawn on the longest side of a scale is{tex}\triangle {/tex} smallest.

questions.question: Semiperimeter of scalene triangle of side k, 2k and 3k is
questions.explanation: Semiperimeter of scalene triangle of side k, 2k and 3k = {tex}{{k + 2k + 3k} \over 2}{/tex} = 3k

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 5 cm, 12 cm and 13 cm. then its area is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{5 + 12 + 13} \over 2}{/tex}= 15 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {15\left( {15 - 5} \right)\left( {15 - 12} \right)\left( {15 - 13} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {15 \times 10 \times 3 \times 2} {/tex} 
= 30 sq. cm
= 0.003 sq. m

questions.question: If the sides of a triangle are doubled, then its area
questions.explanation:
Area of triangle with sides a, b and c.
(A) = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
New sides are 2a, 2b and 2c 
s' = {tex}{{2a + 2b + 2c} \over 2}{/tex}= a + b + c = 2s .....(i)
New Area = {tex}\sqrt {s'\left( {s' - 2a} \right)\left( {s' - 2b} \right)\left( {s' - 2c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {2s\left( {2s - 2a} \right)\left( {2s - 2b} \right)\left( {2s - 2c} \right)} {/tex} [From eq.(i)]
= {tex}4\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= 4A
Therefore, the new area will be four times the old area.

questions.question: The area of a right angled triangle is 20 m2 and one of the sides containing the right triangle is 4 cm. Then the altitude on the hypotenuse is
questions.explanation:
Area of right angle triangle = 20 sq. m
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 20
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = 10 cm
Then, Hypotenuse = {tex}\sqrt {{{10}^2} + {4^2}} = 2\sqrt {29} {/tex} m
If the altitude drawn to the hypotenuse of a right angle triangle, then the length of required altitude = {tex}\frac{10 \times 4}{2 \sqrt{29}}=\frac{20}{\sqrt{29}}{/tex}cm

questions.question: Area of an equilateral triangle of side 10 cm is :
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {10} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}25\sqrt 3 {/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: The length of the sides of a triangle are 5x, 5x and 8x. The area of the triangle is :
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{5x + 5x + 8x} \over 2}{/tex} = 9x cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {9x\left( {9x - 5x} \right)\left( {9x - 5x} \right)\left( {9x - 8x} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {9x \times 4x \times 4x \times x} {/tex} 
= 12x2 sq. cm

questions.question: The area of the the triangle having sides 1 m, 2 m and 2 m is:
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}\frac{1 + 2 + 2}{2} = \frac{5}{2}{/tex}m
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{5}{2}\left(\frac{5}{2}-1\right)\left(\frac{5}{2}-2\right)\left(\frac{5}{2}-2\right)}{/tex} 
= {tex}=\sqrt{\frac{5}{2} \times \frac{3}{2} \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{1}{2}}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{\sqrt {15} }{4}{/tex} sq. m

questions.question: The sides of a triangular flower bed are 5 m, 8 m and 11 m. the area of the flower bed is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{5 + 8 + 11} \over 2}{/tex} = 12 m
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {12\left( {12 - 5} \right)\left( {12 - 8} \right)\left( {12 - 11} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {12 \times 7 \times 4 \times 1} {/tex} 
= {tex}4\sqrt {21} {/tex} sq. m

questions.question: The length of the sides of a triangle are 5 cm, 7 cm and 8 cm. Area of the triangle is :
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{5 + 7 + 8} \over 2}{/tex}= 10 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {10\left( {10 - 5} \right)\left( {10 - 7} \right)\left( {10 - 8} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {10 \times 5 \times 3 \times 2} {/tex} 
= {tex}10\sqrt 3 {/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: Area of an equilateral triangle of side 2 cm is :
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( 2 \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{\sqrt 3 }{/tex} sq. cm

questions.question: The base of a right triangle is 8 cm and hypotenuse is 10 cm. Its area will be :
questions.explanation:
Perpendicular = {tex}\sqrt {{{10}^2} - {8^2}} = \sqrt {100 - 64} {/tex} = 6 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Height
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 24 sq. cm

questions.question: An isosceles right triangle has area 8{tex}c{m^2}{/tex}. The length of its hypotenuse is :
questions.explanation:
Area of isosceles right triangle = 8 sq. cm
{tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} Base {tex}\times{/tex} Base = 8    [Since in isosceles right triangle, base and perpendicular are same]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Base)2 = 16
 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Base = 4 cm
Hypotenuse = {tex}\sqrt {{4^2} + {4^2}} = \sqrt {16 + 16} {/tex} = {tex}\sqrt {32} {/tex} cm

questions.question: Area of the given triangle is
questions.explanation:
In the given triangle,
Base (BC) = {tex}\sqrt {{{13}^2} - {{12}^2}} = \sqrt {169 - 144} {/tex} = 5 cm
Area of triangle ABC = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} BC {tex}\times{/tex} AB
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 12
= 30 sq. cm

questions.question: Each equal side of an isosceles triangle is 13 cm and its base is 24 cm Area of the triangle is :
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{13 + 13 + 24} \over 2}{/tex}= 25 cm

Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {25\left( {25 - 13} \right)\left( {25 - 13} \right)\left( {25 - 24} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {25 \times 12 \times 12 \times 1} {/tex}

= 60 sq. cm

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are in ratio 3 : 4 : 5. If the perimeter of the triangle is 84 cm, then area of the triangle is :
questions.explanation:
Let the sides be 3x, 4x and 5x.
Then according to quesiton, 3x + 4x + 5x = 84
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = 84
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 7
Therefore, the sides are 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 21, cm, 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 28 cm and 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 35 cm
s = {tex}{{21 + 28 + 35} \over 2}{/tex} = 42 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {42\left( {42 - 21} \right)\left( {42 - 28} \right)\left( {42 - 35} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {42 \times 21 \times 14 \times 7} {/tex} 
= 21 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 294 sq. cm

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 4 cm, 8 cm and 6 cm. The length of the perpendicular from the opposite vertex to the longest side is :
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}\frac{4 + 8 + 6} {2}{/tex}= 9 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {9\left( {9 - 4} \right)\left( {9 - 8} \right)\left( {9 - 6} \right)} {/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt {9 \times 5 \times 1 \times 3} {/tex}
= {tex}3\sqrt {15} {/tex} sq. cm
Area of triangle taking base as longest side = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} h
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{2} \times 8 \times h = 3\sqrt {15} {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}h = \frac{3}{4}\sqrt {15} {/tex} cm

questions.question: Each side of an equilateral triangle is 2x cm. If {tex}x\sqrt 3 = \sqrt {48} ,{/tex} then area of the triangle is :
questions.explanation:
Here , {tex}x\sqrt 3 = \sqrt {48} {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\sqrt {16} {/tex}  
Side = 2x
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {2x} \right)^2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt 3 {x^2}{/tex} sq. cm
= {tex}\sqrt 3 {\left( {\sqrt {16} } \right)^2} = 16\sqrt 3 {/tex}

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are x, y and z. If x + y = 7 m, y + z = 9 m, and z + x = 8 m, then area of the triangle is :
questions.explanation:
Adding given three equaitons,
2x + 2y + 2z = 24 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x + y + z = 12
Therefore, s = {tex}\frac{12}{2}{/tex} = 6 m
Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {6\left( {6 - x} \right)\left( {6 - y} \right)\left( {6 - z} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {6\left( {12 - 6 - x} \right)\left( {12 - 6 - y} \right)\left( {12 - 6 - z} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {6\left( {y + z - 6} \right)\left( {x + z - 6} \right)\left( {x + y - 6} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {6\left( {9 - 6} \right)\left( {8 - 6} \right)\left( {7 - 6} \right)} {/tex} 
= {tex}\sqrt {6 \times 3 \times 2 \times 1} {/tex} 
= 6 sq. m

questions.question: If the area of an equilateral triangle is {tex}16\sqrt 3{/tex} cm2 then the perimeter of the triangle is :
questions.explanation:
Area of equilateral triangle = {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}{{\sqrt 3 } \over 4}{\left( {{\rm{Side}}} \right)^2} = 16\sqrt 3 {/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (Side)2 = 64 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Side = 8 cm
Perimeter of equilateral triangle = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} side = 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 = 24 cm

questions.question: The base of a right triangle is 8 cm and hypotenuse is 10 cm. Its area will be
questions.explanation:
Perpendicular = {tex}\sqrt {{{10}^2} - {8^2}} = \sqrt {100 - 64} = \sqrt {36} {/tex} = 6 cm
Area of triangle = {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}x Base x Perpendicular
= {tex}{1 \over 2}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
= 24 sq cm

questions.question: The sides of a triangle are 56 cm, 60 cm and 52 cm long. Then the area of the triangle is
questions.explanation:
s = {tex}{{56 + 60 + 52} \over 2} = {{168} \over 2} = 84{/tex}cm

Area of triangle = {tex}\sqrt {s\left( {s - a} \right)\left( {s - b} \right)\left( {s - c} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {84\left( {84 - 56} \right)\left( {84 - 60} \right)\left( {84 - 52} \right)} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {84 \times 28 \times 24 \times 32} {/tex}

= {tex}\sqrt {12 \times 7 \times 7 \times 4 \times 12 \times 2 \times 16 \times 2} {/tex}

= 12 x 7 x 2 x 2 x 4

= 1344 sq. cm

tests.name: Surface Areas and Volumes Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Surface Areas and Volumes CBSE Online Test 01. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The total surface area of a cube is 96 cm2. Find its lateral surface area.
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of cube = 6a2 (let a be a side of cube)
So, 6a2 = 96,
a2 = {tex}{96\over 6}{/tex} =16
a = 4cm
Now, lateral surface area of cube is 4a2
So, 4a2 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} (4)2
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 16
= 64cm2

questions.question: The lateral surface area of a cube is 256 {tex}{m^2}{/tex} The volume of the cube is
questions.explanation:
LSA of cube = 4 × side²
256 = 4 side²
Side² = 256/4
= 64
Side = 8m
Volume of cube = side3 = 83               
= 8×8×8        
= 512m3

questions.question: The difference between the total surface area of a cube of side 4 cm and its lateral surface area is
questions.explanation:
TSA of cube - LSA of cube
= 6a2 - 4a2
= 2a2
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
= 32 cm2

questions.question: The volume of a cube whose diagonal is {tex}8\sqrt 3{/tex} cm is
questions.explanation:
Diagonal of cube = {tex}{ \sqrt{3} }{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (edge or side)
8{tex}{ \sqrt{3} }{/tex} = {tex}{ \sqrt{3} }{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} edge
edge = 8 cm
Volume of cube = edge3
= 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 8 
= 512 cm3

questions.question: The number of planks of dimensions (5m {tex}\times{/tex} 25 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 10 cm) that can be placed in a pit which is 20 m long, 6 m wide and 80 cm deep is
questions.explanation:
Volume of planks = 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 500 {tex}\times{/tex} 10
= 125000 cm3
Volume of pit = 2000 {tex}\times{/tex} 600 {tex}\times{/tex} 80
= 96000000 cm3
Number of plank ={tex}{Volume \ of \ pit \over volume\ of \ plank}{/tex} 
= {tex}{96000000 \over 125000}{/tex} 
= 768

questions.question: The number of 6 m cubes that can be formed from another cuboid measuring 18 m {tex}\times{/tex} 12 m {tex}\times{/tex} 9 m is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cube = a3 = 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 = 216
Volume of cuboid = l {tex}\times{/tex} b {tex}\times{/tex} h
= 18 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
Number of cube = {tex}\frac{\text{volume of cuboid}}{\text{volume of cube}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1944}{216}{/tex} = 9

questions.question: The length of the longest rod that can be placed in a room 12 m long, 9 m broad and 8 m high is
questions.explanation:
Diagonal of cuboid = {tex}\left.\sqrt{( L^{2}+B^{2}+H^{2}}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\left.\sqrt{( 12^{2}+9^{2}+8^{2}}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\sqrt{289}{/tex}
= 17 m
Thus, the longest rod that could be placed would be along diagonal .i.e 17 m.

questions.question: The edge of a cube whose volume is equal to the volume of a cuboid of dimensions 36 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 75 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 80 cm is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cube = volume of cuboid
Volume of cuboid = 36 {tex}\times{/tex} 75 {tex}\times{/tex} 80
Edge3 = 216000 cm3
Edge = (216000)1/3
= 60 cm

questions.question: A rectangular pit of dimensions 30 m {tex}\times{/tex} 15 m {tex}\times{/tex} 12 m is dug and the earth taken out is disposed of in a carrier which can carry a maximum load of 540 m3 of earth. The least number of rounds the carrier had to make to dispose off the earth dug out is
questions.explanation:
Volume of pit = 30 {tex}\times{/tex} 15 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 = 5400 m2 
Number of round =  Volume of pit {tex}\div{/tex} volume of carrier
= 5400 {tex}\div{/tex} 540 = 10

questions.question: A granary is in the shape of a cuboid of size 16 m {tex}\times{/tex} 12 m {tex}\times{/tex} 9 m. If a bag of grain occupies a space of 0.48 m3, then the maximum number of bags that can be stored in the granary is
questions.explanation:
Volume of granary = 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 9 = 1728m3
Volume of one bag = 0.48 m3
Number of bags = volume of granary {tex}\div{/tex} volume of bag
= (1728) {tex}\div{/tex} 0.48
= 3600

questions.question: When a cuboid of dimensions 30 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 30 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 42.6 cm is melted and converted into cubes of edge 3 cm, then the number of cubes formed is
questions.explanation:
Volume of Cuboid = 30 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 30 cm {tex}\times{/tex} 42.6 cm 
= 38340 cm3
Volume of cube = 33 = 27 cm
Number of cubes formed = {tex}{Volume\ of \ cuboid \over volume\ of\ cube }{/tex} 
= {tex}{38340 \over 27}{/tex} 
= 1420

questions.question: The volume of a right circular cylinder is 2310 cm3. If the radius of its base is 7 cm, then its height is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
2310 = {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} h
h = {tex}{2310 \over 22 ×7}{/tex} 
h = 15 cm

questions.question: If a square paper of side 25 cm is rolled to form a cylinder, then its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
If the square of side 25 cm is rolled to make cylinder then, base of cylinder will have circumference = 25 cm i.e. 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r = 25 and also, height of cylinder would be equal to side of square = 25cm
And, Curved surface area of cylinder is = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
 = 25 {tex}\times{/tex} 25
= 625 cm2

questions.question: The curved surface area of a well of diameter 3.5 m and depth 10 m is
questions.explanation:
The curved surface area of  well = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{3.5 \over 2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 10
= {tex}{22×3.5×10 \over 7}{/tex} 
= 110 m2

questions.question: The curved surface area of a cylinder whose circumference of the base is 22 m and height 3 m is
questions.explanation:
The curved surface area of cylinder = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
Given, 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r = 22 height = 3
So CSA = 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 66 m2

tests.name: Surface Areas and Volumes Test 02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Surface Areas and Volumes CBSE Online Test 02. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: If the outer diameter of a pipe 21 m long is 1 m, then its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
CSA of pipe = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 0.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 21 (diameter = 1m, r = 0.5m)
= 66 m2

questions.question: The cost of cementing the inner curved surface of a 14 m deep well of radius 2 m at the rate of ₹2 per m2 is
questions.explanation:
CSA of well = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 14
= 176 m2
Cost of cementing 1 m2 = ₹2
So cost of cementing 176 m2 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 176
= ₹352

questions.question: The diameter of the base of a cylinder of curved surface area 88 cm2 and height 14 cm is
questions.explanation:
CSA of cylinder = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
88 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r {tex}\times{/tex} 14
r = {tex}{88×7\over 2×22×14}{/tex} 
r =  1 cm
Hence Diameter of base = 2 cm.

questions.question: The total surface area of a right circular cylinder of height 4 cm and radius 3 cm is
questions.explanation:
Total surface area = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh + 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
 = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r(4 + 3)
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r(7)
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 3
= 132 cm2

questions.question: If the lateral surface area of a cylinder is 132 cm2 and its height is 7 cm, then its base diameter is
questions.explanation:
LSA of cylinder = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
132 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r {tex}\times{/tex} 7
r = {tex}\frac{132}{44}{/tex}
= 3 cm
So, the diameter would be 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 = 6 cm

questions.question: The circumference of the base of a right circular cylinder is 44 cm. If its whole surface area is 968 cm2 then the sum of its height and radius is
questions.explanation:
Given,
The circumference of the base of a right circular cylinder = 44 cm
2{tex}\pi{/tex}r = 44
r = 44 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{7}{22 \times 2}{/tex}
r = 7 cm
Whole surface area = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh + 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
968 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7(h + r)
h + r = 22 cm

questions.question: The curved surface area of a right circular cylinder is 4400cm2. If the circumference of its base is 110 cm, then its height is
questions.explanation:
The circumference of its base = 110 cm
2{tex}\pi{/tex}r = 110
r = {tex}\frac{35}{2}{/tex}
CSA = 4400 cm2
2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh = 4400
h = {tex}\frac{4400}{2 \pi r}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4400}{110}{/tex}
= 40 cm

questions.question: A cylindrical piece of maximum volume has to be cut out of an iron cube of edge 4 cm. then, the maximum volume of the iron cylinder is
questions.explanation:
So here, radius would be half of edge of cube, or diameter of rod = edge of cube
r = 2 cm
h = 4 cm
So, volume = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
= {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
= 16{tex}\pi{/tex} cm3

questions.question: If each bag containing rice occupies 2.1 m3 of space, then the number of full bags which can be emptied into a drum of radius 4.2 m and height 3.5 m is
questions.explanation:
Number of bags = {tex}\frac{\text{Volume of drum}}{\text{volume of one bag}}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{2 \pi rh}{2.1}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{(2 \times \frac{22}{7} \times 4.2 \times 3.5)}{2.1}{/tex}
= 92

questions.question: If the radius of the base of a right circular cylinder is halved, keeping the same height, then the ratio of the volume of the reduced cylinder to the volume of the original cylinder is
questions.explanation:
Volume of reduced cylinder : Volume of original cylinder
{tex}\pi (\frac{r}{2})^2 h{/tex} : {tex}\pi (r)^2 h{/tex} 
1 : 4

questions.question: In a cylinder, if radius is halved and height is doubled, the curved surface area will be
questions.explanation:
CSA of original cylinder = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
CSA of new cylinder = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{r}{2}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2h
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh

questions.question: The radii of two cylinder are in the ratio 2 : 3 and their heights are in the ratio of 5 : 3. The ratio of their volumes is
questions.explanation:
volume of cylinder1 : volume of cylinder2
= {tex}\pi{/tex}×(2r)² × 5h : {tex}\pi{/tex}×(3r)² ×3h
= 20:27

questions.question: The volume of a sphere of diameter 42 cm is
questions.explanation:
Since, diameter is 42 cm
Radius would be 21 cm
Volume of sphere = {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r3
= {tex}\frac{4}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (21)3
= 38808 cm3

questions.question: The surface area of a sphere of radius 3.5 cm is
questions.explanation:
Given r = 3.5 cm
Surface area of sphere= 4{tex} {\pi r^2}{/tex} 
= 4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex} {22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 3.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 3.5
= 154 cm2

questions.question: The volume of a sphere is numerically equal to its surface area, then its diameter is
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere = surface area of sphere
{tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3{/tex} = {tex}4 \pi r^2{/tex}
r = 3 units
So, diameter = 6 units

tests.name: Surface Areas and Volumes Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Surface Areas and Volumes CBSE Online Test 03. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A cube of side 4 cm contains a sphere touching its sides. Find the approximate volume of the gap in between.
questions.explanation:
Gap between the two = volume of cube - volume of sphere
= edge3 - {tex}{4\over 3} \pi r^3{/tex}
= 43 - {tex}{4\over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 23 (sphere touches cube, so diameter of sphere would be 4)
= 64 - 33.52
= 30.48 cm3

questions.question: The ratio of the radii of two spheres whose volumes are in the ratio 64 : 27 is
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere 1 : Volume of sphere 2
{tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r_1 ^3{/tex} : {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r_2 ^3{/tex}
{tex}r_1 ^3 : r_2 ^3{/tex} = 64 : 27
r1 : r2 = 4 : 3

questions.question: Given that the surface area of a spherical shot put is 616 cm2 its diameter is
questions.explanation:
Given,
Surface area of sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 616
4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r2 = 616
r2 = 49
r = 7 cm
So diameter = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 14 cm

questions.question: The radius of a sphere is 2r, then its volume will be
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere= {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi r^3{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi{/tex} (2r)³
= {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi{/tex} 8r³
= {tex}\frac{32}{3}\pi {r^3}{/tex}

questions.question: If a spherical balloon grows to twice its radius when inflated, then the ratio of the volume of the inflated balloon to the original balloon is
questions.explanation:
Volume of the inflated balloon : Volume of the original balloon
= {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi (2r)^3{/tex} : {tex}\frac{4}{3} \pi (r)^3{/tex} 
= 8  :  1

questions.question: If the total surface area of a hemisphere is 1848 cm2, then the diameter is
questions.explanation:
Total surface area of hemisphere = 1848 cm2
2 {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 + {tex}\pi{/tex}r2  = 1848
3{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 1848
r2 = {tex}\frac{(1848 \times 7)}{22 \times 3}{/tex}
r2 = 196
r = 14 cm
So, diameter is 28 cm.

questions.question: The total surface area of a cone whose radius is {tex}\frac r2{/tex} and slant height 2l is
questions.explanation:
TSA of cone = {tex}\pi r l+\pi r^{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\pi{/tex}r(1 + r)
= {tex}\pi \frac{r}{2}\left(2 l+\frac{r}{2}\right){/tex}
= {tex}\pi r\left(1+\frac{r}{4}\right){/tex}

questions.question: The total surface area of a cone of radius 7 m and slant height 10 m is
questions.explanation:
TSA of cone = {tex}\pi{/tex}r(l + r)
= {tex}\frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7(10 + 7)
= 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 17
= 374 m2

questions.question: The volume of a cone is 1570 cm2. If it is 15 cm high then its base area is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cone = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r2 {tex}\times{/tex} h
1570   = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}  {tex}\times{/tex} r2 {tex}\times{/tex} 15
{tex}\pi r^2{/tex} = 314 cm2 
Base area is 314 cm2

questions.question: If the slant height of a cone of base radius 7 cm is 25 cm, then its height is
questions.explanation:
l2 = r2 + h2
h2 = l2 - r2
= 252 - 72
= 625 - 49
= 576
h = {tex} { \sqrt{576} }{/tex} 
h = 24 cm

questions.question: The diameter of the base of a cone of height 15 cm and volume 770 cm3 is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cone ={tex} {1 \over 3}\pi r^2 h{/tex} 
770 = {tex}= {1 \over 3}× {22\over7} × r^2 × 15{/tex} 
r2 ={tex}{ 770 × 3 × 7 \over 22 × 15}{/tex} 
r2 = 49
r = 7 cm
Thus diameter 2r = 14 cm.

questions.question: A conical tent is 21 m high and the diameter of its base is 4 m. If 10 men sleep in it, then the average number of cubic dm of air space per man is
questions.explanation:
The volume of conical tent = {tex} {1 \over 3}\pi r^2 h{/tex} 
= {tex} {1 \over 3}\ × {22\over 7}\ × 2^2\ × 21{/tex} 
= 88 m3 or 88000 dm3 (1 m = 10 dm)
So, the space required for one person would be 
 = {tex} {88000 \over 10}{/tex} 
= 8800 dm3

questions.question: A conical pandal 240 m in radius and 100 m high is made of cloth which is 100{tex}\pi {/tex} m wide. Then, the length of cloth used to make the pandal is
questions.explanation:
Surface area of conical pandal = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
l = {tex}{\sqrt{r^2+h^2}}{/tex} 
l ={tex} { \sqrt{240^2 + 100^2}}{/tex} 
l ={tex} { \sqrt{67600}}{/tex} 
l = 260 m
Now surfcae area of pandal = {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 240 {tex}\times{/tex} 260 = area of cloth used
= {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 240 {tex}\times{/tex} 260 = 100{tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} length
Length of cloth = {tex} {240 ×260 \over 100}{/tex} 
= 624 m

questions.question: The curved surface area of a cylinder and a cone is equal. If their base radius is same, then the ratio of the slant height of the cone to the height of the cylinder is
questions.explanation:
CSA of cone = CSA of cylinder
{tex}\pi{/tex}rl = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
l = 2h
l : h = 2 : 1

questions.question: If the ratio of the radii of bases of two cones is 3 : 1 and the ratio of their heights is 1 : 3, then the ratio of their volumes is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cone1 : Volume of cone2
{tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r_1 ^2 h_1{/tex} :  {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r_2 ^2 h_2{/tex} 
= 32 {tex}\times{/tex} 1 : 12 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 = 9 : 3
3 : 1

tests.name: Surface Areas and Volumes Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The cost of digging a pit of dimensions 4.5 m {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5m {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 m at the rate of ₹20 per cubic metre is
questions.explanation:
Cost of digging would be = (4.5 m {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 m {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 m) {tex}\times{/tex} 20
= ₹562.50

questions.question: The volume of resulting cuboid formed when two cubes each of side 6 cm are joined end to end is
questions.explanation:
Volume of one cube = side3
= 63
= 216 cm3
So, volume of cuboid when two cubes are added = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 216 = 432 cm3

questions.question: The number of litres that a cuboidal water tank of dimensions 6 m {tex}\times{/tex} 5 m {tex}\times{/tex} 4.5 m can hold is
questions.explanation:
Volume of water it can hold = Volume of water tank
= l {tex}\times{/tex} b {tex}\times{/tex} h
= 6 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 {tex}\times{/tex} 4.5
= 135m3
= 135000 litres (1 m3 = 1000 litres)

questions.question: The surface area of a cuboid whose length, breadth and height are 15 cm, 10 cm and 20 cm respectively is
questions.explanation:
CSA of cuboid = 2(lb + bh + hl)
= 2(15 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 + 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 20 + 20 {tex}\times{/tex} 15)
= 2(150 + 200 + 300)
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 650
= 1300 cm2

questions.question: The difference between the total surface area and the lateral surface area of a cuboid of length 20 cm, breadth 10 cm and height 40 cm is
questions.explanation:
TSA of cuboid = 2(lb + bh + hl), Lateral surface area of cuboid = 2h(l + b)
So, their difference is = 2(lb + bh + hl) - 2(bh + hl)
= 2(lb)
= 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 10 {tex}\times{/tex} 20
= 400cm2

questions.question: The volume of a cylinder whose circumference of the base is 132 cm and height 25 cm is
questions.explanation:
Given, 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r = 132, r ={tex} {132 \over 2{tex}\pi{/tex}}{/tex} 
Volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi{/tex}r²h
 = {tex}\pi{/tex}({tex} {132 \over 2{tex}\pi{/tex}}{/tex})2 {tex}\times{/tex} 25
={tex}{66 ×66×7 \over 22}× 25{/tex} 
= 34650 cm3

questions.question: If the curved surface area of a cylindrical pillar is 264 m2 and its volume is 924 m3 then its diameter is
questions.explanation:
Given,
CSA of cylinder = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh = 264
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex}rh = 132 m2
Volume of cylinder =  {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h = 924
Putting {tex}\pi{/tex}rh here, 924 = 132 {tex}\times{/tex} r
r = {tex} {924 \over 132}{/tex} 
r = 7 m
So diameter is 2r = 14 m

questions.question: If the area of the base of a right circular cylinder is 15400 cm2 and its volume is 92400 cm3 then its curved surface area is
questions.explanation:
CSA of cylinder = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
Now, given, {tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 15400 and {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h = 92400
area of base = {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r2 = 15400
r2 = 4900
r = 70 cm
So, Volume = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h = 92400
= 15400 {tex}\times{/tex} h = 92400
h = {tex}{92400 \over 15400}{/tex} 
h = 6 cm
Now, CSA is = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
=  2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 70 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
= 2640 cm2

questions.question: The total surface area of a 7 cm high cylinder having a volume of 448{tex}\pi {/tex} cm3 is
questions.explanation:
Volume of cylinder = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h = 448{tex}\pi{/tex}
i.e. r2h = 448
r2 {tex}\times{/tex} h = 448
r2 ={tex}\frac {448}{7}{/tex}
r2 = 64
r =  8 cm
Total surface area = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh + 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r(h + r)
= {tex} \frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 8(7 + 8)
= {tex} \frac{22}{7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 16 {tex}\times{/tex} 15
= {tex}\frac{5280}{7}{/tex} cm2

questions.question: If the circumference of the base of a 9 m high conical tent is 44 m, then the volume of air contained in it is
questions.explanation:
Given, 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r = 44 m²
r = {tex}{44 ×7\over 2×22}{/tex} 
r = 7 m
Now, Volume of air inside tent = {tex}{1\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
= {tex}{1\over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 49 {tex}\times{/tex} 9
= 469 m3

questions.question: If a conical glass is 35 cm in diameter and 12 cm deep, then its capacity in litres is
questions.explanation:
Since, diameter = 35 cm, so radius, r = 17.5 cm
Now, Volume of glass = {tex}{1\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
 = {tex}{1\over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (17.5)2 {tex}\times{/tex} 12
 = {tex}{80580\over 21}{/tex} 
= 3850 cm3
Now, 1 cm3 = 0.001 litres
So, 3850 cm3 = 3.85 litres

questions.question: To make a closed hollow cone of base radius 7 cm and height 24 cm, the area of metal sheet required is
questions.explanation:
Given r = 7 cm, h = 24 cm
So, slant height, l = {tex} { \sqrt{r² +h²}}{/tex} 
= {tex}{ \sqrt{7² + 24²}}{/tex} 
= {tex}{ \sqrt{49 + 576}}{/tex} 
= {tex}{ \sqrt{625}}{/tex} 
= 25 cm
So, surface area of hollow cone = curved surface area + area of base
= {tex}\pi{/tex}rl + {tex}\pi{/tex}r2  
= {tex}\pi{/tex}r(l + r)
= {tex} {22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 (25 + 7)
= 22 {tex}\times{/tex} 32
= 704 cm2

questions.question: The area of canvas required for a conical tent of height 24 m and base radius 7 m is
questions.explanation:
Slant height, l ={tex} {\sqrt{r² + h² }}{/tex} 
= {tex} {\sqrt{7² + 24² }}{/tex} 
= {tex} {\sqrt{49 + 576 }}{/tex} 
= {tex} {\sqrt{625}}{/tex} 
= 25 m
CSA of cone = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
= {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 25
= 550m2

questions.question: A conical vessel whose internal depth is 42 cm and internal diameter is 48 cm is full of water. If 1 cubic dm of water weight 1 kg wt, then the weight of water in the conical vessel is
questions.explanation:
Volume of conical vessel = {tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r2 {tex}\times{/tex} h 
= {tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 42
= 25344 cm3 
= 25.344 dm3  (1dm3 = 1000 cm3)
= 25.344 kg wt. (1 kg- wt = 1dm3)

questions.question: The height of a conical vessel is 3.5 cm. If its capacity is 3.3 litres of milk, then the diameter of its base is
questions.explanation:
1 litre = 1000 cm3
So, 3.3 litre = 3300 cm3 = volume of conical vessel
Thus, Volume of conical vessel = {tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r²h = 3300
{tex}{1 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r2 {tex}\times{/tex} 3.5 = 3300
r² = {tex} {3300 ×21\over 22 ×3.5}{/tex} 
r2 = 900
r = 30 cm
So, diameter = 2r = 60 cm

questions.question: If the volume of a sphere is 4851 cm3, then its surface area is
questions.explanation:
Given, 
Volume of sphere = 4851= {tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r3
4851 = {tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex}× {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex}×r³
r3 = {tex}{ 4851 ×21\over 88}{/tex} = 1157.625
r = {tex} {^3 \sqrt{1157.625}}{/tex} 
r = 10.5
Now, Surface area of sphere = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 10.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 10.5
= 1386 cm²

questions.question: A hemispherical bowl is made of steel 0.25 cm thick. If the inner radius of the bowl is 3.25 cm, then the outer curved surface area of the bowl is
questions.explanation:
Here, outer curved surface area is asked, so, thickness would be added to radius.
Thus, r = 3.25 + 0.25 = 3.5 cm
Surface area of bowl = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r2 = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (3.5)2
= {tex} {2×22×3.5×3.5 \over 7}{/tex} 
= 77 cm2

questions.question: If the surface area of a solid sphere is 1386 cm2 then the total surface area of the solid hemisphere of the same radius is
questions.explanation:
Surface area of a solid sphere is 1386 cm2 = 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2
So, Total Surfce area of hemisphere = 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r²
Thus, 3{tex}\pi{/tex}r² = {tex}{1386 ×3\over 4}{/tex} 
= 1039.5 cm2

questions.question: The number of spherical bullets each 5 dm in diameter which can be cast from a rectangular block of lead 11 m long, 10 m broad and 5 high is
questions.explanation:
Here, radius of spherical bullets = 2.5 dm or 0.25m(1 dm = 0.1 m)
Let the number of bullets be n 
Now, volume of n number of bullets = volume of rectangular block
n× {tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r³ = l×b×h
n×{tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex}×{tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex}× 0.25×0.25×0.25=11×10×5
n = {tex}{11×10×5×21\over 88×0.25×0.25×0.25}{/tex}
n = 8400

questions.question: The number of solid sphere each 6 cm in diameter, which can be moulded from a solid cylinder of height 45 cm and diameter 4 cm without any loss is
questions.explanation:
Given, diameter of ball = 6 cm and diameter of cylinder = 4 cm
So, radius of ball, R = 3 cm Radius of cylinder, r = 2 cm
Let the number of balls be n
So, volume of n balls = volume of a cylinder
n {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{4\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}R3 = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
n {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{4\over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 27 = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 45
n = {tex}{4×45 \over 4×9}{/tex} 
n = 5

questions.question: If a hollow sphere of internal and external diameters 4 cm and 8 cm respectively melted into a cone of base diameter 8 cm, then the height of the cone formed is
questions.explanation:
Given, the external and internal diameter of sphere = 8 and 4
So, external(R) and internal radius(r) of sphere = 4 and 2 
Volume of sphere = volume of cone
{tex}{4 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}(R3 - r3) = {tex}\frac{1}{3} \pi r_c^ 2 h{/tex}
4(43 - 23) = 42 {tex}\times{/tex} h(radius of cone = rc = 4)
4h = 64 - 8
4h = 56
h = 14 cm

questions.question: If a sphere of radius 2r has the same volume as that of a cone with a circular base of radius r, then the height of the cone is
questions.explanation:
Given, Volume of sphere = Volume of cone
{tex}{4\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}(2r)3 = {tex}{1\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h
4 {tex}\times{/tex} 8r3 = r2h
h = 32 r

questions.question: If the radius and slant height of a cone are in the ratio 7 : 13 and its curved surface area is 286 cm2 then its radius is
questions.explanation:
Let radius = 7y and  Slant height = 13y
CSA of cone = {tex}\pi{/tex}rl
286 = {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 7y {tex}\times{/tex} 13 y
y2 = {tex}{286 \over 22×13}{/tex} 
y = 1 cm
Hence radius = 7y
= 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 1
= 7 cm

questions.question: The curved surface area of a right circular cylinder which just encloses a sphere of radius r is
questions.explanation:
Here, height of cylinder would be equal to diameter of sphere i.e. 2r
So, CSA of the cylinder is 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
= 2{tex}\pi{/tex}r(2r)
= 4{tex}\pi{/tex}r2

questions.question: If a sphere is inscribed in a cube, then the ratio of the volume of the cube to the volume of the sphere is
questions.explanation:
Let side of cube be a
Here, side of cube= diameter of sphere
so, radius of sphere = {tex}{a \over 2}{/tex}
The volume of cube : volume of sphere
a³ : {tex}{4\over 3} {tex}\pi{/tex} r^3{/tex}
a³ : {tex}{4\over 3} {tex}\pi{/tex} ({a\over2})^3{/tex}
{tex}3 \times 8 \times a^{3} : 4 \pi a^{3}{/tex}
{tex}6: \pi{/tex}

questions.question: In a cylindrical drum of radius 4.2 m and height 3.5 m, the number of full bags of wheat can be emptied if the space required for wheat in each bag is 2.1 cu m, is
questions.explanation:
Let the number of bags be n
Volume of drum = n(volume of each bag)
{tex}\pi{/tex}R²h   = n(2.1)
{tex}{ {22 \over 7}× (4.2)^2 ×35}{/tex} = n(2.1)
n = {tex}{ {22 \over 7}× (4.2)^2 ×35}\over 2.1{/tex} 
= 92

questions.question: The curved surface of cylinder is 484 cm2 and height is 5.5 cm. Its radius is
questions.explanation:
CSA of cylinder = 2{tex}\pi{/tex}rh
484  = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22 \over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} r {tex}\times{/tex} 5.5
r = {tex} {484 × 7 \over 2×22 × 5.5}{/tex} 
r = 14 cm

questions.question: A solid sphere of radius 3 cm is melted and then cast into small spherical balls, each of diameter 0.6 cm. The number of small balls so obtained is
questions.explanation:
Let number of ball formed be n
Volume of sphere  = n(volume of one spherical ball)
{tex} {4\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}R3 = n({tex} {4\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r3)
n = {tex}({R\over r})^3{/tex} 
n = {tex}({3 \over 0.3})^3{/tex} 
n = 1000

questions.question: An icecream cone has hemispherical top. If the height of the cone is 9 cm and base radius is 2.5 cm, then the volume of icecream is
questions.explanation:
Height of ice-cream cone is 9 cm and radius of the hemispherical top is 2.5 cm.
Now, Volume of ice-cream cone = Volume of cone + volume of Hemispherical top
=  {tex} {1\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2h + {tex} {2 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r3
= {tex}{1\over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}r2(h + 2r)
= {tex} {1\over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5(9 + 5)
= {tex} {1\over 3}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} {tex}{22\over 7}{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 2.5 {tex}\times{/tex} 14
= 91.67 cm3

questions.question: The diameter of a sphere is 42 cm. It is melted and drawn into a cylindrical wire of diameter 2.8 cm. Then, the length of the wire is
questions.explanation:
Volume of sphere = volume of cylindrical wire
{tex} {4 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex}R3 = {tex}\pi{/tex}r2h 
{tex} {4 \over 3}{/tex}{tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} 213 = {tex}\pi{/tex} {tex}\times{/tex} (1.4)2 {tex}\times{/tex} h 
h = {tex} {4 × 21 ×21×21\over 3 × 1.4×1.4}{/tex} 
h = 6300cm or 63 m

tests.name: Statistics Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Statistics CBSE Online Test 01. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A student collects information about the number of school going children in a locality consisting of a hundred households. The data collected by him is
questions.explanation: Data obtained through the information collected by the investigator herself or himself with defininte objective in his/her mind is called as primary data.

questions.question: To analyse the election results, the data is collected from a newspapers. The data thus collected is known as
questions.explanation: Secondary data is the readily available data collected by someone else & published in newspapers or journals etc.

questions.question:
Which of the following variables are discrete?

  1. Size of shoes,
  2. Number of pages in a book,
  3. Distance travelled by a train,
  4. Time
questions.explanation:
Discrete variables are variables that can only take finite values.
Size of shoes and pages in the book have a finite number.

questions.question: For a given data, the difference between the maximum and minimum observation is known as its
questions.explanation: Difference between maximum and minimum value of observation is called as range.

questions.question: In an examination, ten students scored the following marks: 60, 58, 90, 51, 47, 81, 70, 95, 87, 99. The range of this data is
questions.explanation:
Difference between the maximum and minimum value of the observation is called as range.
So, 99 - 47 = 52

questions.question: The range of the data 12, 25, 15, 18, 17, 20, 22, 6, 16, 11, 8, 19, 10, 30, 20, 32 is
questions.explanation:
Difference between the maximum & minimum value of the observation is called as range.
So, 32 - 6 = 26

questions.question: A data is such that its maximum value is 75 and range is 20, then the minimum value is
questions.explanation:
Difference between the maximum and minimum value of the observations is called as range.
Let, minimum value be 'x'
75 - x = 20
So, x = 55

questions.question: The minimum value of a data is 82 and range is 38, then the maximum value is
questions.explanation:
The difference between the maximum and minimum value of the observation is called a range.
Let the maximum value be x.
So,
x - 82 = 38
x = 82 + 38
x = 120

questions.question: In a grouped frequency distribution, the class intervals are 0-10, 10-20, 20-30, .., then the class width is
questions.explanation:
The class width is the difference between the upper or lower class limits of consecutive classes. In this case, class width equals to the difference between the lower limits of the first two classes.
w = 10 - 0
So, the class width is 10

questions.question: In a grouped frequency distribution, the class intervals are 1-20, 21-40, 41-60, ..., then the class width is
questions.explanation:
The class width is the difference between the upper or lower class limits of consecutive classes.
In this case, class width equals to the difference between the lower limits of the first two classes.
Let, W be the class width
W = 21 - 1 = 20
So class width is 20

questions.question: Class size of a distribution having 28, 34, 40, 46 and 52 as its class marks is
questions.explanation:
Class size is the difference between two consecutive values of the class mark.
Here, the difference between two consecutive class mark is 6
i.e 34 - 28 = 6

questions.question: Given the class intervals 0-10, 10-20, 20-30,…, then 10 is considered in class
questions.explanation: As the classes are continuous, the upper limits does not include in that particular class.

questions.question: Given the class intervals 1-10, 11-20, 21-30, …, then 20 is considered in class
questions.explanation: In a discontinuous class both lower and upper limits belong to that particular class.

questions.question: The class mark of the class interval 2.4-6.6 is
questions.explanation:
Class mark = {tex}\frac{upper \ limit + lower \ limit}{2}{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{2.4+6.6}{2}{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{9}{2}{/tex} 
So class mark is 4.5.

questions.question: The class marks of a frequency distribution are as given below: 38, 43, 48, 53, 58. The class corresponding to the class mark 43 is
questions.explanation: As the class size = 5, Class interval is got by subtracting and adding 2.5 to 43.

tests.name: Statistics Test 02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Mathematics Class 09 Statistics CBSE Online Test 02. Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.

  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Class mark of a particular class is 9.5 and the class size is 6, then the class interval is
questions.explanation:
Class mark = {tex}\frac{\text {upper limit}+\text {lower limit}}{2}{/tex} 
2 {tex}\times{/tex} 9.5 = upper limit + lower limit
19 = upper limit + lower limit
Class size = upper limit - lower limit
6 = upper limit - lower limit
Solving above two equations we get,
2 {tex}\times{/tex} upper limit = 25
upper limit = 12.5
Hence, lower lower limit = 12.5 - 6 = 6.5
So, the class interval is 6.5 - 12.5.

questions.question: The class size of a distribution is 25 and the first class interval is 200-224. Then, the class marks of first two class intervals are
questions.explanation:
For 1st class mark
Class mark = {tex}{( upper limit + Lower limit ) \over 2}{/tex} 
1st class mark = {tex}{200+224 \over 2}{/tex} 
1st class mark = 212
Add the class size to get the class mark of the next class
2nd class mark = 212 + 25
2nd class mark = 237
Hence, the class mark of first two intervals are 212 and 237.

questions.question:
Observe the table given below and choose the correct alternative: The class mark for R is

Column P Q R S T E
Marks scored 30-40 40-50 50-60 6-70 70-80 80-90
Number if students 4 8 12 10 7 4
questions.explanation:
Class mark = ( upper limit + Lower limit ) / 2
{tex}\frac{50+60}{2}{/tex}
{tex}\frac{110}{2}{/tex}
So class mark is 55

questions.question: In a frequency distribution, the mid-value of a class is 60.5 and the width of the class is 10. The lower limit of the class is
questions.explanation:
upper limit - lower limit = class width
upper limit - lower limit = 10
{tex}\frac{\text {upperlimit}+\text {lowerlimit}}{2}{/tex} = Mid - value 
upper limit + lower limit = 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 60.5
upper limit + lower limit = 121
By solving the above two equations, we get
upperlimit = 65.5
lowerlimit = 55.5

questions.question: The width of each of five continuous classes in a frequency distribution is 5 and the lower class limit of the lowest class is 10. The upper class limit of the highest class is
questions.explanation: The classes are 10-15,15-20,20-25,25-30,30-35 so that upper limit of the highest class is 35

questions.question: Let m be the mid-point and l be the upper class limit of a class in a continuous frequency distribution. The lower class limit of the class is
questions.explanation:
Let the lower limit = k
Mid point = m
Upper limit = l
Mid point = {tex}\frac {(upper \ limit + lower \ limit)} {2}{/tex}
m = {tex}\frac {(k + l)}{2}{/tex} 
2m = k + l
k = 2m - l 
Therefore, lower limit = 2m - l

questions.question:
A grouped frequency table with class intervals of equal size using 3-5 (5 included in this interval) as one of the class intervals is constructed for the following data. The frequency of the class 3-5 is

1 4 7 2 0 3 9 2 3 7 6 3 5
2 5 5 6 2 3 5 1 0 4 6 4 6
questions.explanation:
count all the numbers in the frequency table between 3 to 5.
Frequency of the numbers from 3 to 5  is 11.

questions.question:
The given cumulative frequency distribution shows the class intervals and their corresponding cumulative frequencies. Then the frequency of class interval 20-30 is

Class 10-20 20-30 30-40
Cumulative frequency 5 14 25
questions.explanation:
A cumulative frequency distribution is the sum of the class and all classes below it in a frequency distribution.
Subtract the previous cumulative frequency (c.f) from the cumulative frequency of the current class.
So frequency of the class interval 20-30 is 14 - 5 = 9

questions.question:
‘Less than’ cumulative frequency table for a given data is as follows. Then, the frequency of class interval 20-30 is

Marks Less than 10 Less than 20 Less than 300 Less than 40
Cumulative frequency 3 17 37 92
questions.explanation:
A cumulative frequency distribution is the sum of the class and all classes below it in a frequency distribution.
Less than 30 has the class interval 20-30. Frequency of this class interval will be corresponding to

Marks Cumulative
frequency		 Class Frequency
Less than 10 3 1-10 3
Less than 20 17 10-20 14
Less than 30 37 20-30 20
Less than 40 92 30-40 55

37 - 17 = 20

questions.question:
‘More than’ cumulative frequency table for a given data is as follows: Then, the frequency of the class interval 70-80 is

Marks More than 89 More than 79 More than 69 More than 59
Cumulative frequency 8 18 30 65
questions.explanation:
A cumulative frequency distribution is the sum of the class and all classes below it in a frequency distribution.

Subtract cumulative frequency of class more than 70  from the next cummulative Frequency of class more than 69.

{tex}30-18=12{/tex}

questions.question: In a bar graph, 0.25 cm length of a bar represents 100 people. Then, the length of bar which represents 2000 people is
questions.explanation:
Use unitary method
0.25 cm - 100 people
So 1 cm - 400 people
So for 2000 people:
{tex}\frac{2000\;}{400}{/tex}= 5 cm

questions.question: In a bar graph if 1 cm represents 30 km, then the length of bar needed to represent 75 km is
questions.explanation:
1 cm = 30 km
So  for 75 km
{tex}\frac{75\;}{30}{/tex} = 2.5 cm

questions.question: In a bar graph, the widths of bars

options.html: are proportional to the corresponding frequencies
are proportional to the corresponding heights
are proportional to the space between two consecutive bars

questions.explanation:
Bar graph is a pictorial representation of data variables(x-axis versus it value on y-axis( height of the bar).
Hence width of bar has no significance in bar graph.

questions.question: In a histogram, which of the following is proportional to the frequency of the corresponding class?
questions.explanation: In, Histogram each rectangle is drawn, where width equivalent to class interval and height equivalent to the frequency of the class.

questions.question: For drawing a frequency polygon of a continuous frequency distribution, we plot the points whose ordinates are the frequency of respective classes and abscissa are respectively
questions.explanation: Frequency polygon is the line graph plotted with class marks on x-axis & frequency of the class on y-axis.
